cvars.description: Max amount of per thread slack data to allow during a capture. Higher values result in better performance due to fewer allocations but higher memory overhead

cvars.name: csv.NamedEventsExclusive
cvars.description: Determines whether to emit named events for exclusive stats

cvars.name: csv.NamedEventsTiming
cvars.description: Determines whether to emit named events for non-exclusive timing stats

cvars.name: csv.PauseProcessingThread
cvars.description: Debug only - When 1, blocks the processing thread to simulate starvation

cvars.name: csv.RecordActorCounts
cvars.description: Record actor counts by class when performing CSV capture

cvars.name: csv.RecordActorCountsThreshold
cvars.description: Number of instances of an native Actor class required before recording to CSV stat

cvars.name: csv.RecordActorCountThreshold
cvars.description: Number of instances of a native Actor class required before recording to a CSV stat

cvars.name: csv.RecordTickCounts
cvars.description: Record tick counts by context when performing CSV capture

cvars.name: csv.statCounts
cvars.description: If 1, outputs count stats

cvars.name: csv.TargetFrameRateOverride
cvars.description: If 0, Defaults to calculating the target frame rate using rhi.SyncInterval and Max refresh rate.

cvars.name: csv.trackWaitsAllThreads
cvars.description: Determines whether to track waits on all threads. Note that this incurs a lot of overhead

cvars.name: csv.trackWaitsGT
cvars.description: Determines whether to track game thread waits. Note that this incurs overhead

cvars.name: csv.trackWaitsRT
cvars.description: Determines whether to track render thread waits. Note that this incurs overhead

cvars.name: csv.WriteBufferSize
cvars.description: When non-zero, defines the size of the write buffer to use whilst writing the CSV file. A non-zero value is required for GZip compressed output.

cvars.name: CurveEditor.DrawCurveKeys
cvars.description: When true we draw curve keys, when false we do not.

cvars.name: CurveEditor.DrawCurveLines
cvars.description: When true we draw curve lines, when false we do not.

cvars.name: CurveEditor.MaxCurvesPerPinnedView
cvars.description: When CurveEditor.PinnedViews is 1, defines the maximum number of curves allowed on a pinned view (0 for no maximum).

cvars.name: CurveEditor.PinnedViews
cvars.description: Whether pinning a curve should also cause it to be exclusively added to a pinned view or not (default: off), rather than simply always remain visible.

cvars.name: CurveEditor.UseCurveCache
cvars.description: When true we cache curve values, when false we always regenerate.

cvars.name: D3D12.AdjustTexturePoolSizeBasedOnBudget
cvars.description: Indicates if the RHI should lower the texture pool size when the application is over the memory budget provided by the OS. This can result in lower quality textures (but hopefully improve performance).

cvars.name: d3d12.AllowDiscardResources
cvars.description: Whether to call DiscardResources after transient aliasing acquire. This is not needed on some platforms if newly acquired resources are cleared before use.

cvars.name: d3d12.AllowPoolAllocateIndirectArgBuffers
cvars.description: Allow indirect args to be pool allocated (otherwise they will be committed resources) (default: 0)

cvars.name: d3d12.BatchResourceBarriers
cvars.description: Whether to allow batching resource barriers

cvars.name: D3D12.Bindless.ResourceDescriptorHeapSize
cvars.description: Bindless resource descriptor heap size

cvars.name: D3D12.Bindless.SamplerDescriptorHeapSize
cvars.description: Bindless sampler descriptor heap size

cvars.name: D3D12.BindlessOnlineDescriptorHeapBlockSize
cvars.description: Block size for sub allocations on the global view descriptor heap.

cvars.name: D3D12.BindlessOnlineDescriptorHeapSize
cvars.description: Online descriptor heap size

cvars.name: d3d12.BindResourceLabels
cvars.description: Whether to enable binding of debug names to D3D12 resources.

cvars.name: D3D12.EmitRgpFrameMarkers
cvars.description: Enables/Disables frame markers for AMD's RGP tool.

cvars.name: D3D12.EvictAllResidentResourcesInBackground
cvars.description: Force D3D12 resource residency manager to evict all tracked unused resources when the application is not focused

cvars.name: d3d12.ExtraDepthTransitions
cvars.description: Adds extra transitions for the depth buffer to fix validation issues. However, this currently breaks async compute

cvars.name: d3d12.FastAllocator.MinPagesToRetain
cvars.description: Minimum number of pages to retain. Pages below this limit will never be released. Pages above can be released after being unused for a certain number of frames.

cvars.name: d3d12.FastConstantAllocatorPageSize
cvars.description: Page size for the fast constant allocator

cvars.name: D3D12.GlobalResourceDescriptorHeapSize
cvars.description: Global resource descriptor heap size

cvars.name: D3D12.GlobalSamplerDescriptorHeapSize
cvars.description: Global sampler descriptor heap size

cvars.name: D3D12.GlobalSamplerHeapSize
cvars.description: Global sampler descriptor heap size

cvars.name: D3D12.InsertOuterOcclusionQuery
cvars.description: If true, enable a dummy outer occlusion query around occlusion query batches. Can help performance on some GPU architectures

cvars.name: D3D12.LocalViewHeapSize
cvars.description: Local view heap size

cvars.name: D3D12.LockTexture2DRHIFlush
cvars.description: If enabled, we do RHIThread flush on LockTexture2D. Likely not required on any platform, but keeping just for testing for now 0: off (default) 1: on

cvars.name: D3D12.LogViewportEvents
cvars.description: Log all the viewport events.

cvars.name: D3D12.MaxCommandsPerCommandList
cvars.description: Flush command list to GPU after certain amount of enqueued commands (draw, dispatch, copy, ...) (default value 10000)

cvars.name: D3D12.OnlineDescriptorHeapBlockSize
cvars.description: Block size for sub allocations on the global view descriptor heap.

cvars.name: D3D12.OnlineDescriptorHeapSize
cvars.description: Online descriptor heap size

cvars.name: d3d12.PoolAllocator.ReadOnlyTextureMaxAllocationSize
cvars.description: Maximum size of a single allocation in the VRAM ReadOnly Texture pool allocator (default 64MB)

cvars.name: d3d12.PoolAllocator.ReadOnlyTextureVRAMPoolSize
cvars.description: Pool size of a single VRAM ReadOnly Texture memory pool (default 64MB)

cvars.name: d3d12.PoolAllocator.RTUAVTextureMaxAllocationSize
cvars.description: Maximum size of a single allocation in the VRAM RTUAV Texture pool allocator (default 0MB - disabled)

cvars.name: d3d12.PoolAllocator.RTUAVTextureVRAMPoolSize
cvars.description: Pool size of a single VRAM RTUAV Texture memory pool (default 0MB - disabled)

cvars.name: D3D12.PSO.DiskCache
cvars.description: Enables a disk cache for Pipeline State Objects (PSOs). PSO descs are cached to disk so subsequent runs can create PSOs at load-time instead of at run-time. This cache contains data that is independent of hardware, driver, or machine that it was created on. It can be distributed with shipping content. 0 to disable the pipeline state disk cache 1 to enable the pipeline state disk cache (default)

cvars.name: D3D12.PSO.DriverOptimizedDiskCache
cvars.description: Enables a disk cache for driver-optimized Pipeline State Objects (PSOs). PSO descs are cached to disk so subsequent runs can create PSOs at load-time instead of at run-time. This cache contains data specific to the hardware, driver, and machine that it was created on. 0 to disable the driver-optimized pipeline state disk cache 1 to enable the driver-optimized pipeline state disk cache

cvars.name: D3D12.PSO.StallWarningThresholdInMs
cvars.description: Sets a threshold of when to logs messages about stalls due to PSO creation. Value is in milliseconds. (100 is the default)

cvars.name: D3D12.PSOPrecache.KeepLowLevel
cvars.description: Keep in memory the d3d12 PSO blob for precached PSOs. Consumes more memory but reduces stalls.

cvars.name: d3d12.ReadOnlyTextureAllocator.MaxPoolSize
cvars.description: Maximum allocation granularity (in bytes) of each size list

cvars.name: d3d12.ReadOnlyTextureAllocator.MinNumToPool
cvars.description: Texture pool of each size list must be large enough to store thismany textures unless constrained by maximum allocation granularity

cvars.name: d3d12.ReadOnlyTextureAllocator.MinPoolSize
cvars.description: Minimum allocation granularity (in bytes) of each size list

cvars.name: d3d12.ReservedResourceHeapSizeMB
cvars.description: Size of the backing heaps for reserved resources in megabytes (default 16MB).

cvars.name: D3D12.ResidencyManagement
cvars.description: Controls whether D3D12 resource residency management is active (default = on).

cvars.name: D3D12.SamplerWarningThreshold
cvars.description: Threshold to start warning about creating too many sampler states

cvars.name: d3d12.SegListTrackLeaks
cvars.description: 1: Enable leak tracking in d3d12 seglist's

cvars.name: D3D12.StablePowerState
cvars.description: If true, enable stable power state. This increases GPU timing measurement accuracy but may decrease overall GPU clock rate.

cvars.name: D3D12.TexturePoolOnlyAccountStreamableTexture
cvars.description: Texture streaming pool size only account streamable texture . - 0: All texture types are counted in the pool (legacy, default). - 1: Only streamable textures are counted in the pool. When enabling the new behaviour, r.Streaming.PoolSize will need to be re-adjusted.

cvars.name: D3D12.TrackAllAllocations
cvars.description: Controls whether D3D12 RHI should track all allocation information (default = off).

cvars.name: D3D12.TrackedReleasedAllocationFrameRetention
cvars.description: Amount of frames for which we keep freed allocation data around when resource tracking is enabled

cvars.name: d3d12.TransientAllocator.FullAliasingBarrier
cvars.description: Inserts a full aliasing barrier on an transient acquire operation. Useful to debug if an aliasing barrier is missing.

cvars.name: D3D12.UnsafeCrossGPUTransfers
cvars.description: Disables cross GPU synchronization correctness, for a gain in performance (Default: true).

cvars.name: d3d12.UploadAllocator.PendingDeleteSizeForceFlushInGB
cvars.description: If given threshold of GBs in the pending delete is queue is reached, then a force GPU flush is triggered to reduce memory load (1 by default, 0 to disable)

cvars.name: d3d12.UploadHeap.BigBlock.MaxAllocationSize
cvars.description: Maximum allocation size on the big block allocator for upload memory

cvars.name: d3d12.UploadHeap.BigBlock.PoolSize
cvars.description: Pool size for the upload memory big block allocator

cvars.name: d3d12.UploadHeap.SmallBlock.MaxAllocationSize
cvars.description: Maximum allocation size on the small block allocator for upload memory

cvars.name: d3d12.UploadHeap.SmallBlock.PoolSize
cvars.description: Pool size for the upload memory small block allocator

cvars.name: D3D12.UseUpdateTexture3DComputeShader
cvars.description: If enabled, use a compute shader for UpdateTexture3D. Avoids alignment restrictions 0: off (default) 1: on

cvars.name: d3d12.VRAMBufferPoolDefrag
cvars.description: Defrag the VRAM buffer pool

cvars.name: d3d12.VRAMBufferPoolDefrag.MaxCopySizePerFrame
cvars.description: Max amount of data to copy during defragmentation in a single frame (default 32MB)

cvars.name: d3d12.VRAMTexturePoolDefrag
cvars.description: Defrag the VRAM Texture pool (enabled by default)

cvars.name: d3d12.VRAMTexturePoolDefrag.MaxCopySizePerFrame
cvars.description: Max amount of data to copy during defragmentation in a single frame (default 32MB)

cvars.name: D3D12.ZeroBufferSizeInMB
cvars.description: The D3D12 RHI needs a static allocation of zeroes to use when streaming textures asynchronously. It should be large enough to support the largest mipmap you need to stream. The default is 4MB.

cvars.name: DDC.Graph
cvars.description: Name of the graph to use for the Derived Data Cache.

cvars.name: DebugViewModeHelpers.Enable
cvars.description: Specifies whether to enable the debug view mode shaders. Typically only disabled for a special case editor build, if it doesn't require them

cvars.name: demo.AsyncLoadWorld
cvars.description: If 1, we will use seamless server travel to load the replay world asynchronously

cvars.name: demo.CheckpointSaveMaxMSPerFrameOverride
cvars.description: If >= 0, this value will override the CheckpointSaveMaxMSPerFrame member variable, which is the maximum time allowed each frame to spend on saving a checkpoint. If 0, it will save the checkpoint in a single frame, regardless of how long it takes.

cvars.name: demo.ClientRecordAsyncEndOfFrame
cvars.description: If true, TickFlush will be called on a thread in parallel with Slate.

cvars.name: demo.CullDistanceOverride
cvars.description: If > 0, will represent distance from any viewer where actors will stop being recorded.

cvars.name: demo.DecreaseRepPrioritizeThreshold
cvars.description: The % of Replicated to Prioritized actors at which prioritize time will be increased.

cvars.name: demo.DestructionInfoPriority
cvars.description: Replay net priority assigned to destruction infos during recording.

cvars.name: demo.EnableCheckpoints
cvars.description: Whether or not checkpoints save on the server

cvars.name: Demo.ExceededBudgetWarningInterval
cvars.description: When > 0, we will wait this many seconds between logging warnings for demo recording exceeding time budgets.

cvars.name: demo.FastForwardDestroyTearOffActors
cvars.description: If true, the driver will destroy any torn-off actors immediately while fast-forwarding a replay.

cvars.name: demo.FastForwardIgnoreRPCs
cvars.description: If true, RPCs will be discarded during playback fast forward.

cvars.name: demo.FastForwardLevelsPausePlayback
cvars.description: If true, pause channels and playback while fast forward levels task is running.

cvars.name: demo.FastForwardSkipRepNotifies
cvars.description: If true, the driver will optimize fast-forwarding by deferring calls to RepNotify functions until the fast-forward is complete.

cvars.name: demo.ForceDisableAsyncPackageMapLoading
cvars.description: If true, async package map loading of network assets will be disabled.

cvars.name: demo.ForcePersistentLevelPriority
cvars.description: If true, force persistent level to record first when prioritizing and using streaming level fixes.

cvars.name: demo.GotoTimeInSeconds
cvars.description: For testing only, jump to a particular time

cvars.name: demo.IncreaseRepPrioritizeThreshold
cvars.description: The % of Replicated to Prioritized actors at which prioritize time will be decreased.

cvars.name: demo.InternalPauseChannels
cvars.description: If true, run standard logic for PauseChannels rather than letting the game handle it via FOnPauseChannelsDelegate.

cvars.name: demo.JumpToEndOfLiveReplay
cvars.description: If true, fast forward to a few seconds before the end when starting playback, if the replay is still being recorded.

cvars.name: demo.LateActorDormancyCheck
cvars.description: If true, check if an actor should become dormant as late as possible- when serializing it to the demo archive.

cvars.name: demo.LateDestructionInfoPrioritize
cvars.description: If true, process destruction infos at the end of the prioritization phase.

cvars.name: demo.LoadCheckpointGarbageCollect
cvars.description: If nonzero, CollectGarbage will be called during LoadCheckpoint after the old actors and connection are cleaned up.

cvars.name: demo.Loop
cvars.description: <1> : play replay from beginning once it reaches the end / <0> : stop replay at the end

cvars.name: demo.LoopCount
cvars.description: If > 1, will play the replay that many times before stopping.

cvars.name: demo.MaximumRecDestructionInfoTime
cvars.description: Maximum percentage of frame to use replicating destruction infos, if per frame limit is enabled.

cvars.name: demo.MaximumRepPrioritizePercent
cvars.description: Maximum percent of time that may be spent prioritizing actors, regardless of throttling.

cvars.name: demo.MinimumRepPrioritizePercent
cvars.description: Minimum percent of time that must be spent prioritizing actors, regardless of throttling.

cvars.name: demo.MinRecordHz
cvars.description: Minimum number of demo frames recorded per second (use with care)

cvars.name: demo.QueueCheckpointChannels
cvars.description: If true, the driver will put all channels created during checkpoint loading into queuing mode, to amortize the cost of spawning new actors across multiple frames.

cvars.name: demo.RecordHz
cvars.description: Maximum number of demo frames recorded per second

cvars.name: demo.RecordHzWhenNotRelevant
cvars.description: Record at this frequency when actor is not relevant.

cvars.name: demo.RecordUnicastRPCs
cvars.description: When true, also record unicast client rpcs on actors that share a net driver name with the demo driver.

cvars.name: demo.ReplayStreamerAutoDemoPrefix
cvars.description: Prefix to use when generating automatic demo names.

cvars.name: demo.ReplayStreamerAutoDemoUseDateTimePostfix
cvars.description: When enabled, uses the current time as a postfix for automatic demo names instead of indices

cvars.name: demo.SaveRollbackActorState
cvars.description: If true, rollback actors will save some replicated state to apply when respawned.

cvars.name: demo.SkipTime
cvars.description: Skip fixed amount of network replay time (in seconds)

cvars.name: demo.TimeDilation
cvars.description: Override time dilation during demo playback (-1 = don't override)

cvars.name: demo.UseAdaptiveReplayUpdateFrequency
cvars.description: If 1, NetUpdateFrequency will be calculated based on how often actors actually write something when recording to a replay

cvars.name: demo.UseNetRelevancy
cvars.description: If 1, will enable relevancy checks and distance culling, using all connected clients as reference.

cvars.name: demo.ViewTargetPriorityScale
cvars.description: Scale view target priority by this value when prioritization is enabled.

cvars.name: demo.WithDeltaCheckpoints
cvars.description: If true, record checkpoints as a delta from the previous checkpoint.

cvars.name: demo.WithGameSpecificFrameData
cvars.description: If true, allow game specific data to be recorded with each demo frame.

cvars.name: demo.WithLevelStreamingFixes
cvars.description: If 1, provides fixes for level streaming (but breaks backwards compatibility).

cvars.name: demo.WithTimeBurnIn
cvars.description: If true, adds an on screen message with the current DemoTime and Changelist.

cvars.name: DetailsPanel.Style.EnableCardLayout
cvars.description: Specifies whether the card layout is in effect for the Details View.

cvars.name: DetailsPanel.UI.ForceShowComponentEditor
cvars.description: If true, forces the component editor to show in the main viewport and blueprint details panel for UObjects which normally have it hidden.

cvars.name: DisableOrphanPins
cvars.description: 0=Orphan pins are enabled (default), 1=Orphan pins are disabled (note: this option will go away in the future)

cvars.name: DoPooledThreadWaitTimeouts
cvars.description: If enabled, uses the old behaviour for waking up pool threads every 10ms. Otherwise, lets pooled threads sleep until data arrives.

cvars.name: dp.AllowScalabilityGroupsToChangeAtRuntime
cvars.description: If true, device profile scalability bucket cvars will be set with scalabilitypriority which allows them to be changed at runtime. Off by default.

cvars.name: dp.Override
cvars.description: DeviceProfile override - setting this will use the named DP as the active DP. In addition, it will restore any previous overrides before setting (does a dp.OverridePop before setting after the first time). The commandline -dp option will override this on startup, but not when setting this at runtime

cvars.name: DumpCopyPropertiesForUnrelatedObjects
cvars.description: Dump the objects that are cross class copied

cvars.name: DumpStatPackets
cvars.description: If true, dump stat packets.

cvars.name: Editor.AllowPlayWorldFeature
cvars.description: When true play world is allowed.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncAssetCompilation
cvars.description: 1 - Async assets compilation is enabled. 2 - Async assets compilation is enabled but on pause (for debugging). When enabled, assets will be replaced by placeholders until they are ready to reduce stalls on the game thread and improve overall editor performance.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncAssetCompilationMaxConcurrency
cvars.description: Set the maximum number of concurrent assets compilation, -1 for unlimited.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncAssetCompilationMaxMemoryUsage
cvars.description: 0 - No hard memory limit, will be tuned against system available memory (recommended default). N - Try to limit total memory usage for asset compilation to this amount (in GB). Try to stay under specified memory limit for asset compilation by reducing concurrency when under memory pressure.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncAssetCompilationMemoryPerCore
cvars.description: How much memory (in GB) should tasks reserve that report a required memory amount Unknown (-1).

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncAssetCompilationResume
cvars.description: Number of queued work to resume while paused.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncSkinnedAssetCompilation
cvars.description: 1 - Async skinned assets compilation is enabled. 2 - Async skinned assets compilation is enabled but on pause (for debugging). When enabled, skinned assets will be replaced by placeholders until they are ready to reduce stalls on the game thread and improve overall editor performance.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncSkinnedAssetCompilationMaxConcurrency
cvars.description: Set the maximum number of concurrent skinned assets compilation, -1 for unlimited.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncSkinnedAssetCompilationResume
cvars.description: Number of queued work to resume while paused.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncSoundWaveCompilation
cvars.description: 1 - Async soundwaves compilation is enabled. 2 - Async soundwaves compilation is enabled but on pause (for debugging). When enabled, soundwaves will be replaced by placeholders until they are ready to reduce stalls on the game thread and improve overall editor performance.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncSoundWaveCompilationMaxConcurrency
cvars.description: Set the maximum number of concurrent soundwaves compilation, -1 for unlimited.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncSoundWaveCompilationResume
cvars.description: Number of queued work to resume while paused.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncStaticMeshCompilation
cvars.description: 1 - Async static meshes compilation is enabled. 2 - Async static meshes compilation is enabled but on pause (for debugging). When enabled, static meshes will be replaced by placeholders until they are ready to reduce stalls on the game thread and improve overall editor performance.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncStaticMeshCompilationMaxConcurrency
cvars.description: Set the maximum number of concurrent static meshes compilation, -1 for unlimited.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncStaticMeshCompilationResume
cvars.description: Number of queued work to resume while paused.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncStaticMeshPlayInEditorDebugDraw
cvars.description: 0 - Debug draw for async static mesh compilation is disabled. 1 - Debug draw for async static mesh compilation is enabled. The collision sphere around the player is drawn in white and can be adjusted with Editor.AsyncStaticMeshPlayInEditorDistance Any static meshes affecting the physics that are still being compiled will have their bounding box drawn in green. Any static meshes that were waited on due to being too close to the player will have their bounding box drawn in red for a couple of seconds.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncStaticMeshPlayInEditorDistance
cvars.description: Scale applied to the player bounding sphere to determine how far away to force meshes compilation before resuming play. The effect can be seen during play session when Editor.AsyncStaticMeshPlayInEditorDebugDraw = 1.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncStaticMeshPlayInEditorMode
cvars.description: 0 - Wait until all static meshes are built before entering PIE. (Slowest but causes no visual or behavior artifacts.) 1 - Wait until all static meshes affecting navigation and physics are built before entering PIE. (Some visuals might be missing during compilation.) 2 - Wait only on static meshes affecting navigation and physics when they are close to the player. (Fastest while still preventing falling through the floor and going through objects.)

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncTextureCompilation
cvars.description: 1 - Async textures compilation is enabled. 2 - Async textures compilation is enabled but on pause (for debugging). When enabled, textures will be replaced by placeholders until they are ready to reduce stalls on the game thread and improve overall editor performance.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncTextureCompilationMaxConcurrency
cvars.description: Set the maximum number of concurrent textures compilation, -1 for unlimited.

cvars.name: Editor.AsyncTextureCompilationResume
cvars.description: Number of queued work to resume while paused.

cvars.name: Editor.ComponentVisualizer.AutoSelectComponent
cvars.description: Automatically adds the spline component to the selection set if avaialable when a point is selected on the spline

cvars.name: Editor.HDRNITLevel
cvars.description: Sets The desired NIT level of the editor when running on HDR

cvars.name: Editor.HDRSupport
cvars.description: Sets whether or not we should allow the editor to run on HDR monitors

cvars.name: Editor.ObjectReverseLookupMask
cvars.description: -1 - Does validation on all objects type (slowest) 0 - Skip validation on all objects type 1 - Perform validation on Texture to Textures lookups 2 - Perform validation on Texture to Materials lookups 4 - Perform validation on Material to Primitives lookups 8 - Perform validation on StaticMesh To Components lookups 16 - Perform validation on Material to Materials lookups

cvars.name: Editor.ObjectReverseLookupMode
cvars.description: 0 - Reverse lookup tables are computed every time they are needed (slower behavior) 1 - Maintain permanent reverse lookup tables (faster behavior) 2 - Comparison mode (slowest to do validation between both mode)

cvars.name: Editor.ReflectEditorLevelVisibilityWithGame
cvars.description: Enables the transaction of game visibility state when editor visibility state changes. 0 - game state is not reflected with editor. 1 - game state is relfected with editor.

cvars.name: Editor.UseLegacyGetReferencersForDeletion
cvars.description: Choose the algorithm to be used when detecting referencers of any assets/objects being deleted. 0: Use the most optimized version (default) 1: Use the slower legacy version (for debug/comparison)

cvars.name: Editorpaths.Enabled
cvars.description: Enable experimental Editor Path support.

cvars.name: EnableHighDPIAwareness
cvars.description: Enables or disables high dpi mode

cvars.name: EnableLeakTest
cvars.description: If set to 1, enables leak test, for testing stats based memory profiler

cvars.name: Engine.DelayTrimMemoryDuringMapLoadMode
cvars.description: 0: TrimMemory during LoadMap as normal 1: Delay TrimMemory until the end of LoadMap (initial boot up) 2: Delay TrimMemory in every LoadMap call

cvars.name: Engine.MinNumOverlapsToUseTMap
cvars.description: Min number of overlaps required before using a TMap for deduplication

cvars.name: Engine.SupressWarningsInOnScreenDisplay
cvars.description: 0: Show both errors and warnings on screen, 1: Show only errors on screen (in either case only when DurationOfErrorsAndWarningsOnHUD is greater than zero)

cvars.name: Engine.VerifyLoadMapWorldCleanup.Severity
cvars.description: Controls severity of logging when the engine detects that a UWorld was leaked during LoadMap. 0 - all reference tracing and logging is disabled 1 - logs an error 2 - ensure 3 - fatal error

cvars.name: Engine.VerifyLoadMapWorldCleanup.TraceMode
cvars.description: Controls detail level of reference tracing when the engine detects that a world was leaked during LoadMap. 0 - direct references only 1 - full reference trace

cvars.name: EnhancedEditorInput.bAutomaticallyStartConsumingInput
cvars.description: Should the UEnhancedInputEditorSubsystem be started as soon as it is inialized?

cvars.name: EnhancedEditorInput.bShouldLogAllInputs
cvars.description: Should each InputKey call be logged?

cvars.name: EnhancedInput.bEnableAutoUpgrade
cvars.description: Should your project automatically be set to use Enhanced Input if it is currently using the legacy input system?

cvars.name: EnhancedInput.bEnableNameValidation
cvars.description: Flag to enable or disable name validation in the editor for UPlayerMappableKeySettings assets

cvars.name: enhancedInput.bp.bShouldWarnOnUnsupportedInputPin
cvars.description: Should the Enhanced Input event node throw a warning if a Unsuported" pin has a connection?"

cvars.name: EnhancedInput.bShouldLogAllWorldSubsystemInputs
cvars.description: Should each InputKey call to the World subsystem be logged?

cvars.name: EnhancedInput.Editor.EnableMappingNameValidation
cvars.description: Enables editor validation on player mapping names

cvars.name: EnhancedInput.EnableDefaultMappingContexts
cvars.description: Should the UEnhancedInputDeveloperSettings::DefaultMappingContexts be applied to every UEnhancedPlayerInput?

cvars.name: EnhancedInput.Mappings.bCheckForEmptyKeyMappingsDuringValidation
cvars.description: When true, Enhanced Input key mappings will throw an error during validation if they are mapped to an empty key.

cvars.name: EnhancedInput.OnlyTriggerLastActionInChord
cvars.description: Should only the last action in a ChordedAction trigger be fired? If this is disabled, then the dependant chords will be fired as well

cvars.name: ExternalPluginCookedAssetRootPath
cvars.description: Root path to use when estimating the cooked path external plugin assets, or empty to use the standard engine/project root.

cvars.name: fc.BlockSize
cvars.description: Size of each block in KB in the global file cache object Should match packaging compression block size for optimal reading from packege

cvars.name: fc.NumBlocks
cvars.description: Number of blocks in the global file cache object

cvars.name: foliage.CullAll
cvars.description: If greater than zero, everything is considered culled.

cvars.name: foliage.CullAllInVertexShader
cvars.description: Debugging, if this is greater than 0, cull all instances in the vertex shader.

cvars.name: foliage.CullDistanceScale
cvars.description: Controls the cull distance scale. Foliage must opt-in to cull distance scaling through the foliage type.

cvars.name: foliage.DebugBuildTreeAsyncDelayInSeconds
cvars.description: Adds a delay (in seconds) to BuildTreeAsync tasks for debugging

cvars.name: foliage.DensityScale
cvars.description: Controls the amount of foliage to render. Foliage must opt-in to density scaling through the foliage type.

cvars.name: foliage.DisableCull
cvars.description: If greater than zero, no culling occurs based on frustum.

cvars.name: foliage.DiscardDataOnLoad
cvars.description: 1: Discard foliage data on load if the foliage type has it enabled; 0: Keep foliage data regardless of whether the foliage type has it enabled or not (requires reloading level)

cvars.name: foliage.DitheredLOD
cvars.description: If greater than zero, dithered LOD is used, otherwise popping LOD is used.

cvars.name: foliage.ForceLOD
cvars.description: If greater than or equal to zero, forces the foliage LOD to that level.

cvars.name: foliage.InstanceRuns
cvars.description: Whether to use the InstanceRuns feature of FMeshBatch to compress foliage draw call data sent to the renderer. Not supported by the Mesh Draw Command pipeline.

cvars.name: foliage.LODDistanceScale
cvars.description: Scale factor for the distance used in computing LOD for foliage.

cvars.name: foliage.MaxEndCullDistance
cvars.description: Max distance for end culling (0 disabled).

cvars.name: foliage.MaxOcclusionQueriesPerComponent
cvars.description: Controls the granularity of occlusion culling. 16-128 is a reasonable range.

cvars.name: foliage.MaxTrianglesToRender
cvars.description: This is an absolute limit on the number of foliage triangles to render in one traversal. This is used to prevent a silly LOD parameter mistake from causing the OS to kill the GPU.

cvars.name: foliage.MinimumScreenSize
cvars.description: This controls the screen size at which we cull foliage instances entirely.

cvars.name: foliage.MinInstancesPerOcclusionQuery
cvars.description: Controls the granualrity of occlusion culling. 1024 to 65536 is a reasonable range. This is not exact, actual minimum might be off by a factor of two.

cvars.name: foliage.MinLOD
cvars.description: Used to discard the top LODs for performance evaluation. -1: Disable all effects of this cvar.

cvars.name: foliage.MinOcclusionQueriesPerComponent
cvars.description: Controls the granularity of occlusion culling. 2 should be the Min.

cvars.name: foliage.MinVertsToSplitNode
cvars.description: Controls the accuracy between culling and LOD accuracy and culling and CPU performance.

cvars.name: foliage.OffGroundThreshold
cvars.description: Maximum distance from base component (in local space) at which instance is still considered as valid

cvars.name: foliage.OnlyLOD
cvars.description: If greater than or equal to zero, only renders the foliage LOD at that level.

cvars.name: foliage.OverestimateLOD
cvars.description: If greater than zero and dithered LOD is not used, then we use an overestimate of LOD instead of an underestimate.

cvars.name: foliage.RandomLODRange
cvars.description: Random distance added to each instance distance to compute LOD.

cvars.name: foliage.SplitFactor
cvars.description: This controls the branching factor of the foliage tree.

cvars.name: ForceDecompressionFails
cvars.description: If > 0, then force decompression failures to test the panic sync read fallback.

cvars.name: ForcePakProcessReads
cvars.description: If true, then Asynchronous reads from pak files will always used the FPakProcessedReadRequest system that is ordinarily only used on compressed files.

cvars.name: framegrabber.framelatency
cvars.description: How many frames to wait before reading back a frame. 0 frames will work but cause a performance regression due to CPU and GPU syncing up.

cvars.name: FreezeAtPosition
cvars.description: This console variable stores the position and rotation for the FreezeAt command which allows to lock the camera in order to provide more deterministic render profiling. The FreezeAtPosition can be set in the ConsoleVariables.ini (start the map with MAPNAME?bTourist=1). Also see the FreezeAt command console command. The number syntax if the same as the one used by the BugIt command: The first three values define the position, the next three define the rotation. Example: FreezeAtPosition 2819.5520 416.2633 75.1500 65378 -25879 0

cvars.name: FullSizeUnitGraph
cvars.description: If true, the unit graph is the old full size, full brightness version.

cvars.name: FX.AllowAsyncTick
cvars.description: allow parallel ticking of particle systems.

cvars.name: FX.AllowCulling
cvars.description: Allow emitters to be culled.

cvars.name: fx.AllowFastPathFunctionLibrary
cvars.description: If > 0 Allow the graph to insert custom fastpath operations into the graph.

cvars.name: FX.AllowGPUParticles
cvars.description: If true, allow the usage of GPU particles.

cvars.name: FX.AllowGPUSorting
cvars.description: Allow particles to be sorted on the GPU.

cvars.name: FX.BatchAsync
cvars.description: If 1, particle async tasks are batched because they often take less time than it takes to wake up a task thread. No effect on editor.

cvars.name: FX.BatchAsyncBatchSize
cvars.description: When FX.BatchAsync = 1, controls the number of particle systems grouped together for threading.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.AdjustedUsageDecayRate
cvars.description: Rate at which the FX budget adjusted usage value is allowed to decay. This helps prevent FX flipping off/on if the usage oscilates over the cull threshold as the FX are culled/enabled.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.AdjustedUsageMax
cvars.description: Max value for FX Budget adjusted usage. Prevents one very long frame from keeping the usage above 1.0 for long periods under budget.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.Debug.GameThreadConcurrentTimeOverride
cvars.description: When >= 0.0 overrides the reported time for FX on the GameThreadConcurrent. Useful for observing/debugging the impact on other systems.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.Debug.GameThreadTimeOverride
cvars.description: When >= 0.0 overrides the reported time for FX on the GameThread. Useful for observing/debugging the impact on other systems.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.Debug.RenderThreadTimeOverride
cvars.description: When >= 0.0 overrides the reported time for FX on the RenderThread. Useful for observing/debugging the impact on other systems.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.Enabled
cvars.description: Controls whether we track global FX budgets.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.EnabledInEditor
cvars.description: Controls whether we track global FX budgets in editor builds.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.GameThread
cvars.description: Budget (in ms) for all combined FX work that runs only on the gamethread. As this budget is approached or exceeded, various FX systems will attempt to scale down more and mroe agressively to remain in budget.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.GameThreadConcurrent
cvars.description: Budget (in ms) for all combined FX work that runs on the gamethread or on a concurrent task spawned from the game thread. As this budget is approached or exceeded, various FX systems will attempt to scale down more and mroe agressively to remain in budget.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.HistoryFrames
cvars.description: Number of frames the global FX budget tracking will hold to work out it's average frame time.

cvars.name: fx.Budget.RenderThread
cvars.description: Budget (in ms) for all combined FX work that runs on the Render Thread. As this budget is approached or exceeded, various FX systems will attempt to scale down more and mroe agressively to remain in budget.

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.BeamRenderingEnabled
cvars.description: Controls if beam rendering is enabled for Cascade

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.GpuSpriteDynamicAllocations
cvars.description: Controls if gpu sprite are allocated dynamically

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.GpuSpriteRenderingEnabled
cvars.description: Controls if gpu sprite rendering is enabled for Cascade

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.MeshRenderingEnabled
cvars.description: Controls if mesh rendering is enabled for Cascade

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.SetTemplateDenyList
cvars.description: Set the template deny List to use. (i.e. P_SystemA,P_SystemB)

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.SkipZeroDeltaTime
cvars.description: When enabled a delta tick time of nearly 0.0 will cause us to skip the component update. This fixes issue like PSA_Velocity aligned sprites, but could cause issues with things that rely on accurate velocities (i.e. TSR).

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.SpriteRenderingEnabled
cvars.description: Controls if sprite rendering is enabled for Cascade

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.TrailRenderingEnabled
cvars.description: Controls if trail rendering is enabled for Cascade

cvars.name: fx.Cascade.UseVelocityForMotionBlur
cvars.description: When enabled velocity will be used to approximate velocity for vertex factories that support this.

cvars.name: fx.DeferrPSCDeactivation
cvars.description: If > 0, all deactivations on Particle System Components is deferred until next tick.

cvars.name: fx.DetailedCSVStats
cvars.description: If true, we write detailed partilce stats to the CSV profiler.

cvars.name: fx.DetailedCSVStats.MemoryMode
cvars.description: Gathers approximate memory information depending on the mode. 0 = Disabled (default). 1 = Minimal information (small performance impact). 2 = Full information (large performance impact).

cvars.name: fx.DumpGraphKeyGen
cvars.description: If > 0 the key generation will be dumped to the log.

cvars.name: fx.DumpParticleData
cvars.description: If > 0 current frame particle data will be dumped after simulation.

cvars.name: fx.DumpParticleParameterStores
cvars.description: If > 0 current frame particle parameter stores will be dumped when updated.

cvars.name: fx.DumpSystemData
cvars.description: If > 0, results of system simulations will be dumped to the log.

cvars.name: fx.DumpVMIR
cvars.description: If > 0 verbose logging is enabled for the vm compiler backend.

cvars.name: FX.EarlyScheduleAsync
cvars.description: If 1, particle system components that can run async will be scheduled earlier in the frame

cvars.name: fx.EnableCircularAnimTrailDump
cvars.description: Controls logging for when circular links are discovered in anim trails. 0 = No logging. 1 = Minimal logging. 2 = Verbose logging.

cvars.name: fx.EnableEmitterMergeChangeIdLogging
cvars.description: If > 0 verbose change id information will be logged to help with debuggin merge issues.

cvars.name: fx.EnableNiagaraCRHandler
cvars.description: If > 0 Niagara will push some state into the crash reporter. This is not free so should not be used unless actively tracking a crash in the wild. Even then it should only be enabled on the platforms needed etc.

cvars.name: fx.EnableNiagaraLightRendering
cvars.description: If == 0, Niagara Light Renderers are disabled.

cvars.name: fx.EnableNiagaraMeshRendering
cvars.description: If == 0, Niagara Mesh Renderers are disabled.

cvars.name: fx.EnableNiagaraRibbonRendering
cvars.description: If == 0, Niagara Ribbon Renderers are disabled.

cvars.name: fx.EnableNiagaraRuntimeCycleCounts
cvars.description: Toggle for runtime cylce counts tracking Niagara's frame time.

cvars.name: fx.EnableNiagaraSpriteRendering
cvars.description: If == 0, Niagara Sprite Renderers are disabled.

cvars.name: fx.EnableVerboseNiagaraChangeIdLogging
cvars.description: If > 0 Verbose change id logging info will be printed.

cvars.name: fx.ExecVMScripts
cvars.description: If > 0 VM scripts will be executed, otherwise they won't, useful for looking at the bytecode for a crashing compiled script.

cvars.name: fx.ForceCompileOnLoad
cvars.description: If > 0 emitters will be forced to compile on load.

cvars.name: fx.ForceExecVMPath
cvars.description: If < 0, the legacy VM path will be used, if > 0 the experimental version will be used, and the default if 0.

cvars.name: fx.ForceFailIfPreviouslyNotSetOnMerge
cvars.description: If > 0, when merging in from parent emitters swap linked variables in the stack to be Fail If Previously Not Set" for their default type."

cvars.name: fx.ForceMergeOnLoad
cvars.description: If > 0 emitters will be forced to merge on load.

cvars.name: fx.ForceNiagaraCacheDump
cvars.description: If > 0 all cached graph traversal data will be dumped

cvars.name: fx.ForceNiagaraCompileToFail
cvars.description: If > 0 emitters will go through the motions of a compile, but will never set valid bytecode.

cvars.name: fx.ForceNiagaraSpawnAttachedSolo
cvars.description: If > 0 Niagara systems which are spawned attached will be force to spawn in solo mode for debugging.

cvars.name: fx.ForceNiagaraTranslatorDump
cvars.description: If > 0 all translation generated HLSL will be dumped

cvars.name: fx.ForceNiagaraTranslatorSingleThreaded
cvars.description: If > 0 all translation will occur one at a time, useful for debugging.

cvars.name: fx.ForceNiagaraVMBinaryDump
cvars.description: If > 0 all translation generated binary text will be dumped

cvars.name: fx.ForceSafeScriptAttributeTrim
cvars.description: If > 0 attribute trimming will use a less aggressive algorithm for removing script attributes.

cvars.name: FX.FreezeGPUSimulation
cvars.description: Freeze particles simulated on the GPU.

cvars.name: FX.FreezeParticleSimulation
cvars.description: Freeze particle simulation.

cvars.name: fx.FXAllowParticleMeshLODs
cvars.description: If we allow particle meshes to use LODs or not

cvars.name: FX.GPUCollisionDepthBounds
cvars.description: Limits the depth bounds when searching for a collision plane.

cvars.name: fx.GPUSimulationDynTextureSizeXY
cvars.description: GPU Particle simulation texture XY dimension when dynamic resizing is enabled (default=16)

cvars.name: fx.GPUSimulationTextureSizeX
cvars.description: GPU Particle simulation texture X dimension (default=1024); set in project renderer settings, potentially overridden by device profile.

cvars.name: fx.GPUSimulationTextureSizeY
cvars.description: GPU Particle simulation texture Y dimension (default=1024); set in project renderer settings, potentially overridden by device profile.

cvars.name: fx.GPUSort.BufferSlack
cvars.description: Slack ratio when resizing GPU sort buffers. Must be bigger than 1 (default=2)

cvars.name: fx.GPUSort.FrameCountBeforeShrinking
cvars.description: Number of consecutive frames where the GPU sort buffer is considered oversized before allowing shrinking. (default=100)

cvars.name: fx.GPUSort.MinBufferSize
cvars.description: Minimum GPU sort buffer size, in particles (default=8192)

cvars.name: fx.GPUSort.StressTest
cvars.description: Force a stress test on the GPU sort by release persistent data every frame (default=0)

cvars.name: FX.GPUSpawnWarningThreshold
cvars.description: Warning threshold for spawning of GPU particles.

cvars.name: fx.LastRenderTimeSafetyBias
cvars.description: The time to bias the LastRenderTime value to allow for the delay from it being written by the RT.

cvars.name: fx.LogCompileIdGeneration
cvars.description: If > 0 all compile id generation will be logged. If 2 or greater, log detailed info.

cvars.name: fx.LogCompileStaticVars
cvars.description: If > 0 all compile id generation dealing with static variables will be logged.

cvars.name: fx.LogNiagaraSystemChanges
cvars.description: If > 0 Niagara Systems will be written to a text format when opened and closed in the editor.

cvars.name: fx.LWCTileRecache
cvars.description: When we cross this number of LWC tiles from where we started the FX we need to recache the LWC tile to avoid artifacts. When this occurs the system may need to reset, cull particles too far away, or do some additional processing to handle it. Setting this value to 0 will remove this behavior but could introduce rendering & simulation artifacts.

cvars.name: FX.MaxCPUParticlesPerEmitter
cvars.description: Maximum number of CPU particles allowed per-emitter.

cvars.name: FX.MaxGPUParticlesSpawnedPerFrame
cvars.description: Maximum number of GPU particles allowed to spawn per-frame per-emitter.

cvars.name: fx.MaxNiagaraCPUParticlesPerEmitter
cvars.description: The max number of supported CPU particles per emitter in Niagara.

cvars.name: fx.MaxNiagaraGPUParticlesSpawnPerFrame
cvars.description: The max number of GPU particles we expect to spawn in a single frame.

cvars.name: fx.MaxNiagaraNeighborGridCells
cvars.description: The max number of supported grid cells in Niagara. Overflowing this threshold will cause the sim to warn and fail.

cvars.name: fx.MaxNiagaraRasterizationGridCells
cvars.description: The max number of supported grid cells in Niagara. Overflowing this threshold will cause the sim to warn and fail.

cvars.name: FX.MaxParticleTilePreAllocation
cvars.description: Maximum tile preallocation for GPU particles.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AllowAsyncWorkToEndOfFrame
cvars.description: Allow async work to continue until the end of the frame, if false it will complete within the tick group it's started in.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AllowCullProxies
cvars.description: Toggles whether Niagara will use Cull Proxy systems in place of systems culled by scalability.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AllowDeferredReset
cvars.description: If we are running async work when a reset is requested we will instead queue for the finalize to perform, this avoid stalling the GameThread.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AllowPrimedPools
cvars.description: Allow Niagara pools to be primed.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AllowVisibilityCullingForDynamicBounds
cvars.description: Allow async work to continue until the end of the frame, if false it will complete within the tick group it's started in.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Analytics.ReportOnCook
cvars.description: If true then basic system info will be gathered and reported as part of the editor analytics for every cooked system.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Array.PositionDebugRadius
cvars.description: When using the Niagara Debugger the radius of position array debug display

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AsyncGpuTrace.GlobalSdfEnabled
cvars.description: If disabled AsyncGpuTrace will not be supported against Global SDF.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AsyncGpuTrace.HWRayTraceEnabled
cvars.description: If disabled AsyncGpuTrace will not be supported against the HW ray tracing scene.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AsyncTrace.CountsScratchPadBucketSize
cvars.description: Scratch bucket size for the async gpu trace counts buffer. This buffer requires 4.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.AsyncTrace.ScratchPadBucketSize
cvars.description: Size (in elements) for async gpu traces scratch buffer buckets.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.BaselineGenerationDelay
cvars.description: Time we delay before match start for generating niagara perfoamnce baselines in a cooked game.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Batcher.DebugLogging
cvars.description: Enables a lot of spew to the log to debug the batcher.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Batcher.TickFlush.MaxPendingTicks
cvars.description: The maximum number of unprocess ticks before we process them. The larger the number the more data we process in a single frame.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Batcher.TickFlush.MaxQueuedFrames
cvars.description: The number of unprocessed frames with queued ticks before we process them. The larger the number the more data we process in a single frame, this is generally only a concern when the application does not have focus.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Batcher.TickFlush.Mode
cvars.description: What to do when we go over our max queued frames. 0 = Keep ticks queued, can result in a long pause when gaining focus again. 1 = (Default) Process all queued ticks with dummy view / buffer data, may result in incorrect simulation due to missing depth collisions, etc. 2 = Kill all pending ticks, may result in incorrect simulation due to missing frames of data, i.e. a particle reset.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Collision.CPUEnabled
cvars.description: Controls if CPU collisions are enabled or not.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Compilation.MaxActiveTaskCount
cvars.description: The maximum number of active Niagara system compilations that can be going concurrantly.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CompileDDCWaitTimeout
cvars.description: During script compilation, how long do we wait for the ddc to answer in seconds before starting shader compilation?

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CompileDumpTimings
cvars.description: If enabled a file containing compile metrics for the different compiled scripts will be dumped to the log folder

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CompileHashAllDataInterfaces
cvars.description: Forces Niagara to include all data interfaces as part of compile hash generation. 0 = Disabled. 1 = Enabled, but hash once, better for perf. 2 = Enabled, generated hash always good for iteration on Niagara work.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CompileValidateMode
cvars.description: Controls how the validate compile mode will report differences it encounters when comparing default/async compiles

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CompileWaitLoggingCap
cvars.description: During automation, how many times do we log before failing compilation?

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CompileWaitLoggingThreshold
cvars.description: During automation, how long do we wait for a compile result before logging.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ComponentRenderComponentCountWarning
cvars.description: The max number of allowed components before a ui warning is shown in the component renderer.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ComponentRenderPoolInactiveTimeLimit
cvars.description: The time in seconds an inactive component can linger in the pool before being destroyed.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ComponentWarnAsleepCullReaction
cvars.description: When enabled we will warn if a NiagaraComponent completes naturally but has Asleep mode set for cullreaction.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ComponentWarnNullAsset
cvars.description: When enabled we will warn if a NiagaraComponent is activate with a null asset. This is sometimes useful for tracking down components that can be removed.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CompressScriptByteCode
cvars.description: Should we compress script bytecode to save memory. Will be uncompressed on demand.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.CSVSplitTime
cvars.description: Length of Niagara's split time events passed to the CSV profiler. There are used to give check more confined stat averages.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.AllowAsyncLoad
cvars.description: True if we should attempt to load systems etc asynchronosly.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.AllowLazyHandlerInit
cvars.description: True if we allow lazy initialization of NDC handlers.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.BlockAsyncLoadOnUse
cvars.description: True if we should block on any pending async loads when those assets are used.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.Enabled
cvars.description: If true, Niagara Data Channels will be enabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.ForceReadPrevFrame
cvars.description: When true this will force Niagara systems with NDC read DIs to read from the previous frame.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.ForceReadTickGroup
cvars.description: When >= 0 this will force Niagara systems with NDC read DIs to tick in the given Tick Group.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.FrameDataToCapture
cvars.description: The number of frames the debugger will capture for write requests.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.LogWritesToOutputLog
cvars.description: 0=Disabled, 1=Log write summary, 2=Also write data; If >0, the NDC debugger will print all data channel writes to the output log.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DataChannels.WarnOnLateWrites
cvars.description: If true, late writes to NDCs will generate warnings. Late meaning after their final allowed tick group.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Debug.GlobalLoopTime
cvars.description: If > 0 all Niagara FX will reset every N seconds.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DebugDraw.Enabled
cvars.description: Enable or disable the Debug Draw Data Interface, note does not fully disable the overhead.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DecalRenderer.DrawDebug
cvars.description: When none zero will draw debug information.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DecalRenderer.Enabled
cvars.description: If == 0, Niagara Decal Renderers are disabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DelayScriptAsyncOptimization
cvars.description: Should we delay the async optimization until the emitter is activated?

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DeletePythonFilesOnError
cvars.description: This determines whether we keep the intermediate python used by module/emitter versioning around when they were executed and resulted in an error.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DigestGraphCacheSize
cvars.description: Defines the size of the cache for digested Niagara graphs.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DumpNans
cvars.description: If not 0 any NaNs will be dumped always.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.DumpNansOnce
cvars.description: If not 0 any NaNs will be dumped for the first emitter that encounters NaNs.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Emitter.ComputePSOPrecacheMode
cvars.description: Controlls how PSO precaching should be done for Niagara compute shaders 0 = Disabled (Default). 1 = Enabled if r.PSOPrecaching is also enabled. Emitters are not allowed to run until they complete if r.PSOPrecache.ProxyCreationWhenPSOReady=1 2 = Force Enabled. 3 = Force Enabled, emitters are not allowed to run until they complete.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Emitter.MaxGPUBufferElements
cvars.description: Maximum elements per GPU buffer, for example 4k elements would restrict a float buffer to be 16k maximum per buffer. Note: If you request something smaller than what will satisfy a single unit of work it will be increased to that size. Default 0 which will allow the buffer to be the maximum allowed by the RHI.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.EmitterBounds.DynamicExpandMultiplier
cvars.description: Multiplier used on dynamic bounds gathering, i.e. 1 means no change, 1.1 means increase by 10%. This value is applied after we calculate any dynamic bounds snapping.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.EmitterBounds.DynamicSnapValue
cvars.description: The value used to snap (round up) dynamic bounds calculations to.For example, a snap of 128 and a value of 1 would result in 128

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.EmitterBounds.FixedExpandMultiplier
cvars.description: Multiplier used on fixed bounds gathering, i.e. 1 means no change, 1.1 means increase by 10%.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.EnableCustomInlineDynamicInputFormats
cvars.description: If > 0 and experimental inline editors for dynamic input trees are enabled, custom formats which are defined on scripts will be applied.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.EnableExperimentalInlineDynamicInputs
cvars.description: If > 0 experimental inline editors for dynamic input trees will be available via right click menu in the stack.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.EventSpawnsUpdateAttributeInitialValues
cvars.description: If > 0 Niagara Event Spawn Scripts will update the Initial.* values for particle attributes.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.FailIfNotSetSeverity
cvars.description: The severity of messages emitted by Parameters with Default Mode Fail If Not Set". 3 = Error, 2 = Warning, 1= Log, 0 = Disabled."

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.FailStaticMeshDataInterface
cvars.description: When enabled we will fail out using static mesh data interfaces.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ForceLastTickGroup
cvars.description: Force Niagara ticks to be in the last tick group, this mirrors old behavour and can be useful to test for async overlapping issues.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ForceWaitForCompilationOnActivate
cvars.description: When a component is activated it will stall waiting for any pending shader compilation.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GeometryComponentRenderPoolInactiveTimeLimit
cvars.description: The time in seconds an inactive component can linger in the pool before being destroyed.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuComputeDebug.DrawDebugEnabled
cvars.description: Should we draw any of the debug information or not.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuComputeDebug.FourComponentMode
cvars.description: Adjust how we visualize four component types 0 = Visualize RGB (defaut) 1 = Visualize A

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuComputeDebug.MaxLineInstances
cvars.description: Maximum number of line draw we support in a single frame.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuComputeDebug.MaxTextureHeight
cvars.description: The maximum height we will visualize a texture at, this is to avoid things becoming too large on screen.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuComputeDebug.MinTextureHeight
cvars.description: The minimum height we will visualize a texture at, smaller textures will be scaled up to match this.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuComputeDebug.OccludedLineColorScale
cvars.description: Scalar value to adjust occluded lines, where 0 means transparent and 1 is opaque. Default is 0.05 or 5%

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuComputeDebug.ShowNaNInf
cvars.description: When enabled will show NaNs as flashing colors.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuEmitterCheckFloat16Support
cvars.description: When enabled we check to see if the RHI has support for Float16 UAV read / write, if it doesn't GPU emitters that use Float16 are banned from running.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuProfiling.Enabled
cvars.description: Primary control to allow Niagara to use GPU profiling or not.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GpuScriptsCompiledBySystem
cvars.description: If true GPU shaders for Niagara scripts will be compiled along with the NiagaraSystem (when using AsyncTask compilation mode).

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GraphDataCacheSize
cvars.description: Maximum number of elements to store within the GraphDataCache.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.GraphDataCacheValidation
cvars.description: If true will perform validation on retrieving data from the data FNiagaraGraphDataCache.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Grid2D.OverrideFormat
cvars.description: Optional override for all grids to use this format.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Grid2D.ResolutionMultiplier
cvars.description: Optional global modifier to grid resolution

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Grid3D.OverrideFormat
cvars.description: Optional override for all grids to use this format.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Grid3D.ResolutionMultiplier
cvars.description: Optional global modifier to grid resolution

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Grid3D.UseRGBAGrid
cvars.description: Use RGBA textures when possible

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.IndirectArgsPool.AllowShrinking
cvars.description: Allow the indirect args pool to shrink after a number of frames below a low water mark.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.IndirectArgsPool.BlockSizeFactor
cvars.description: Multiplier on the indirect args pool size when needing to increase it from running out of space. (default=2.0)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.IndirectArgsPool.LowWaterAmount
cvars.description: Percentage (0-1) of the indirect args pool that is considered low and worthy of shrinking

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.IndirectArgsPool.LowWaterFrames
cvars.description: The number of frames to wait to shrink the indirect args pool for being below the low water mark. (default=150)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.IndirectArgsPool.MinSize
cvars.description: Minimum number of draw indirect args allocated into the pool. (default=256)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LeakDetector.DebugMessageTime
cvars.description: Time we display the debug message for on screen.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LeakDetector.Enabled
cvars.description: Enables or disables the leak detector.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LeakDetector.GrowthCountThreshold
cvars.description: We need to see growth this many times without a drop in count before we consider it a leak.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LeakDetector.ReportActiveLeaks
cvars.description: How do we report active components leaks?0 - Never report.1 - Report immediately. (default)2 - Report on GC.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LeakDetector.ReportTotalLeaks
cvars.description: How do we report total components leaks?0 - Never report.1 - Report immediately.2 - Report on GC. (default)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LeakDetector.TickDeltaSeconds
cvars.description: The time in seconds that must pass before we sample the component information.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LegacyDeviceProfile
cvars.description: This is a special case CVar that allows us to use CVar conditions to maintain behavior with legacy device profiles. Do not use directly for new content. Legacy device profiles can be given a specific value for this CVarand then CVar conditions used to enable / disable as appropriate to match with legacy assets with enabled / disabled content based on them.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LogFoundButNotAllowedAssets
cvars.description: If > 0 assets which are found, but not allowed in the current editor context will be printed to the log.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LogVerboseWarnings
cvars.description: Enable to output more verbose warnings to the log file, these are considered dismissable warnings but may provide information when debugging. Default is enabled in editor builds and disabled in non editor builds.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LUT.OptimizeThreshold
cvars.description: Error Threshold used when optimizing Curve LUTs, setting to 0.0 or below will result in no optimization

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.LUT.VerifyPostLoad
cvars.description: Enable to verify LUTs match in PostLoad vs the Loaded Data

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.MaxCompilePollTimePerFrame
cvars.description: When a lot of system compile tasks queue up, this is the max time per frame that is used to advance them.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.MaxStatRecordedFrames
cvars.description: The number of frames recorded for the stat performance display of niagara cpu and gpu scripts.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.MeshRenderer.CalcMeshUsedParticleCount
cvars.description: Optimization which will inspect renderer vis / mesh index to determine which set of meshes we can potential render with.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.NDIExport.GPUMaxReadbackCount
cvars.description: Maximum buffer instance count for the GPU readback when in PerParticleMode, where <= 0 means ignore.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.NDISpline.GDisableLUTs
cvars.description: Should we turn off all LUTs on CPU?

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.NDIStaticMesh.UseInlineLODsOnly
cvars.description: When enabled Niagara will never use streaming LOD levels, only inline LODs.0 = Streaming LODs can be sampled.1 = Only inlined LODs can be sampled.2 = Only inlined LODs can be sampled by default but each DI can override this if desired.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ObjectNeedsLoadMode
cvars.description: How we decide to handle objects that need loading 0 - Do nothing 1 - Validate objects are loaded 2 - Validate objects are loaded and force preload

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.OnDemandCompileEnabled
cvars.description: Compiles Niagara Systems on demand rather than on post load.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ParticleRead.IgnoreUnsafeReads
cvars.description: When enabled we will allow unsafe reads to compile with a warning, however the read result will be invalid.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.PerfTestFrames
cvars.description: How many frames to gather in each performance test.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.PreloadSelectablePluginAssetsOnDemand
cvars.description: If > 0 then niagara system, emitter, and script assets provided by the niagara plugin will be preloaded when a dialog is opened to select them. This is a temoporary workaround for asset registry issues in cooked editor builds.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.PruneEmittersOnCook
cvars.description: If > 0 this platform will prune disabled emitters during cook.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.PSOPrecache.ReverseCulling
cvars.description: Also Precache PSOs with with reverse culling set when not 2 sided. (default 1)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.QualityLevel
cvars.description: The quality level for Niagara Effects.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.QualityLevel.Max
cvars.description: The Maximum quality level for Niagara Effects.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.QualityLevel.Min
cvars.description: The minimum quality level for Niagara Effects.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Renderer.CookOutStaticEnabledBinding
cvars.description: If none zero renderers with static variables used for enabled binding will cook out if they are not enabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.RenderTarget.IgnoreCookedOut
cvars.description: Ignores create render targets for cooked out emitter, i.e. ones that are not used by any GPU emitter.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.RenderTarget.ResolutionMultiplier
cvars.description: Optional global modifier to Niagara render target resolution.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.RenderTarget2D.SimCacheCompressed
cvars.description: When enabled compression is used for the sim cache data.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.RenderTargetVolume.SimCacheCompressed
cvars.description: When enabled compression is used for the sim cache data.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.RenderTargetVolume.SimCacheDataStorageMode
cvars.description: Backing storage type for Volume RT sim cache data. 0 uses raw data, 1 uses OpenVDB, 2 uses SVT

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.RenderTargetVolume.SimCacheEnabled
cvars.description: When enabled we can write data into the simulation cache.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.RenderTargetVolume.SimCacheUseOpenVDBFloatGrids
cvars.description: Use OpenVDB float grids as output.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Scalability.CanPreventCullingOnPlayerFX
cvars.description: When enabled Niagara can optionally prevent scalability culling on FX linked to the player.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Scalability.DistanceCulling
cvars.description: When non-zero, high level scalability culling based on distance is enabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Scalability.GlobalBudgetCulling
cvars.description: When non-zero, high level scalability culling based on global time budget is enabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Scalability.InstanceCountCulling
cvars.description: When non-zero, high level scalability culling based on instance count is enabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Scalability.MinMaxDistance
cvars.description: Minimum value for Niagara's Max distance value. Primariy to prevent divide by zero issues and ensure a sensible distance value for sorted significance culling.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Scalability.VisibilityCulling
cvars.description: When non-zero, high level scalability culling based on visibility is enabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SetEmitterDenyList
cvars.description: Set the emitter deny list to use. (i.e. NS_SystemA:EmitterA,NS_SystemB:EmitterA)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SetGpuDataInterfaceDenyList
cvars.description: Set the Gpu data interface deny list to use. (i.e. UMyDataInteraceA,UMyDataInteraceB)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SetGpuDenyList
cvars.description: Set Gpu deny list to use, more targetted than to allow comparing OS,OSVersion,CPU,GPU. Format is OSLabel,OSVersion,CPU,GPU. Blank entries are assumed to auto pass matching.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SetGpuEmitterDenyList
cvars.description: Set the Gpu emitter deny list to use. (i.e. NS_SystemA:EmitterA,NS_SystemB:EmitterA)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SetGpuRHIAdapterDenyList
cvars.description: Set Gpu RHI Adapter deny list to use, comma separated and uses wildcards, i.e. (MyGpu) would exclude anything that contains MyGpu

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SetGpuRHIDenyList
cvars.description: Set Gpu RHI deny list to use, comma separated and uses wildcards, i.e. (MyRHI) would exclude anything that contains MyRHI

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SetSystemDenyList
cvars.description: Set the system deny List to use. (i.e. NS_SystemA,NS_SystemB)

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Shader.ForceBindEverything
cvars.description: Forces Niagara to display errors about missing shader bindings.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.ShowAllocationWarnings
cvars.description: If not 0 then frequent reallocations and over-allocations of particle memory will cause warnings in the log.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Solo.AllowAsyncWorkToEndOfFrame
cvars.description: Allow async work to continue until the end of the frame for solo Niagara instances, if false it will complete within the tick group it started in.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.Solo.TickEarly
cvars.description: When enabled will tick kin the first available tick group.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.StripByteCodeOverride
cvars.description: Overrides project settings to strip byte code from the script on load.. -1 = Enabled, strips Experimental VM. 0 = Disabled. 1 = Enabled, strips original VM.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.AllowASync
cvars.description: If > 0, system post tick is parallelized.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.BatchGPUTickSubmit
cvars.description: The if non zero we allow GPU Ticks to be submitted to the Render Thread in batches.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.ConcurrentGPUTickInit
cvars.description: The if non zero we allow GPU Ticks to be initialized in the System's concurrent tick rather than on the game thread.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.MaxTickSubsteps
cvars.description: The max number of possible substeps per frame when a system uses a fixed tick delta.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.SkipTickDeltaSeconds
cvars.description: When none zero we skip all ticks with a delta seconds less than equal to this number.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.TaskStallTimeout
cvars.description: Timeout in microseconds for Niagara simulation tasks to be considered stalled. When this is > 0 we busy wait as opposed to joining the TG so avoid using execpt for debugging.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.TickBatchSize
cvars.description: The number of system instances to process per async task.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.TickTaskAllowFrameOverlap
cvars.description: When enabled we allow ticks to overlap beyond PostActorTick until either EOF updates or the next tick.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.TickTaskShouldWait
cvars.description: When enabled the tick task will wait for concurrent work to complete, when disabled the task is complete once the GT tick is complete.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.SystemSimulation.UpdateOnSpawn
cvars.description: If > 0, system simulations are given a small update after spawn.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.Background
cvars.description: Task Priority When Set to Background Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.Background bnh

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.High
cvars.description: Task Priority When Set to High Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.High bnh

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.Low
cvars.description: Task Priority When Set to Low Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.Low bnh

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.Normal
cvars.description: Task Priority When Set to Normal Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.Normal bnh

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.PerfCapturePriority
cvars.description: Priority to use if performance captures are enabled. Reduces the amount of context switching for Niagara to make performance measurements more reliable.Default is 1, set to -1 to not override the default priorities.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.PostActorTickPriority
cvars.description: Any tasks we execute, such as spawning, in post actor tick will run at this priority.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.TaskPriorities.TickGroupPriority
cvars.description: Set tick group priories for Niagara.For example, TG_PrePhysics:2,TG_DuringPhysics:2

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.UseFastSetUserParametersToDefaultValues
cvars.description: When a component is activated we will check the surpession list.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.UseGlobalFXBudget
cvars.description: If true, Niagara will track performace data into the global FX budget and feed the global budget values into scalability.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.VolumeRenderer.Enabled
cvars.description: If == 0, Niagara Volume Renderers are disabled.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.WaitOnPreGC
cvars.description: Toggles whether Niagara will wait for all async tasks to complete before any GC calls.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.WarnComponentRenderCount
cvars.description: The max number of components that a single system can spawn before a log warning is shown.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.WorldManager.FirstHighPriTickGroup
cvars.description: Defines which tick groups should be set to high priority for the world manager. 0 - (Default) all tick groups will run high priority. 1 - The first tick group will be normal priority, all others high, etc.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.WorldManager.KillUniqueSims
cvars.description: System simulations will be removed when unique rather that waiting for GC.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.WorldManager.ObjectPoolEnabled
cvars.description: Should we pool objects, yay or nay.

cvars.name: fx.Niagara.WorldManager.SpawnPerTickGroup
cvars.description: Will attempt to spawn new systems earlier (default enabled).

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraAllowComputeShaders
cvars.description: If true, allow the usage compute shaders within Niagara.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraAllowGPUParticles
cvars.description: If true, allow the usage of GPU particles for Niagara.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraAllowRuntimeScalabilityChanges
cvars.description: If > 0 this platform allows niagara scalability settings changes at runtime.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraBatcher.FreeBufferEarly
cvars.description: Will take the path to release GPU buffers when possible. This will reduce memory pressure but can result in more allocations if you buffers ping pong from zero particles to many.

cvars.name: FX.NiagaraComponentPool.CleanTime
cvars.description: How often should the pool be cleaned (in seconds).

cvars.name: FX.NiagaraComponentPool.Enable
cvars.description: How many Particle System Components to preallocate when creating new ones for the pool.

cvars.name: FX.NiagaraComponentPool.KeepComponentsRegistered
cvars.description: If non-zero, components returend to the pool are kept registered with the world but set invisible. This will reduce the cost of pushing/popping components int.

cvars.name: FX.NiagaraComponentPool.KillUnusedTime
cvars.description: How long a pooled particle component needs to be unused for before it is destroyed.

cvars.name: FX.NiagaraComponentPool.Validation
cvars.description: Enables pooling validation.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraDataBufferMinSize
cvars.description: Niagara data buffer minimum allocation size in bytes (Default=512).

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraDataBufferShrinkFactor
cvars.description: Niagara data buffer size threshold for shrinking. (Default=3) The buffer will be reallocated when the used size becomes 1/F of the allocated size.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraEnablePrecompilerNamespaceDatasetCulling
cvars.description: Force the namespace fixup precompiler process to cull unused Dataset parameters. Only enabled if fx.NiagaraEnablePrecompilerNamespaceFixup is also enabled.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraEnablePrecompilerNamespaceFixup
cvars.description: Enable a precompiler stage to discover parameter name matches and convert matched parameter hlsl name tokens to appropriate namespaces.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraGlobalSystemCountScale
cvars.description: A global scale on system count thresholds for culling in Niagara.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraGPUDataBufferChunkSize
cvars.description: Niagara GPU data buffer allocation chunk size used to round GPU allocations in bytes, must be power of 2 (Default=4096)

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraGPUDataBufferShrinkFactor
cvars.description: Niagara GPU data buffer size threshold for shrinking. (Default=2) The buffer will be reallocated when the used size becomes 1/F of the allocated size.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraGPUDataWarningSize
cvars.description: Allocation size where we should log a warning.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraGpuLowLatencyTranslucencyEnabled
cvars.description: When enabled translucent materials can use the current frames simulation data no matter which tick pass Niagara uses. This can result in an additional data buffer being required but will reduce any latency when using view uniform buffer / depth buffer / distance fields / etc

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraGpuSubmitCommandHint
cvars.description: If greater than zero, we use this value to submit commands after the number of dispatches have been issued.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraLogNamespaceFixup
cvars.description: Log matched variables and pin name changes in precompile.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraMaxStatInstanceReports
cvars.description: The max number of different instances from which stat reports are aggregated.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraOptimizeCrossGPUTransfer
cvars.description: Optimizes fence waits for cross GPU transfers when rendering views on multiple GPUs via nDisplay. (Default = 1)

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraPerfReporting
cvars.description: 0 = Disabled 1 = Text Perf Report on world Transitions. 2 = Text Report for every test with poor or bad perf. 3 = As 2 but screenshots are also generated for each bad test.

cvars.name: fx.NiagaraRegenBaselinesOnWorldChange
