cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.AcceptFirstHit
cvars.description: Whether to allow shadow rays to terminate early, on first intersected primitive. This may result in worse denoising quality in some cases. (default = 0)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.AvoidSelfIntersectionTraceDistance
cvars.description: Max trace distance of epsilon trace to avoid self intersections. If set to 0, epsilon trace will not be used.

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.EnableHairVoxel
cvars.description: Enables use of hair voxel data for tracing shadow (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.EnableMaterials
cvars.description: Enables material shader binding for shadow rays. If this is disabled, then a default trivial shader is used. (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.EnableTwoSidedGeometry
cvars.description: Enables two-sided geometry when tracing shadow rays (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.HairOcclusionThreshold
cvars.description: Define the number of hair that need to be crossed, before casting occlusion (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.Lights.Directional
cvars.description: Enables ray tracing shadows for directional lights (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.Lights.Point
cvars.description: Enables ray tracing shadows for point lights (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.Lights.Rect
cvars.description: Enables ray tracing shadows for rect light (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.Lights.Spot
cvars.description: Enables ray tracing shadows for spot lights (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.LODTransitionEnd
cvars.description: The end of an LOD transition range (default = 5000)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.LODTransitionStart
cvars.description: The start of an LOD transition range (default = 4000)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.MaxBatchSize
cvars.description: Maximum number of shadows to trace at the same time.

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.MaxTranslucencyHitCount
cvars.description: -1: Evaluate all intersections (default). 0: Disable translucent shadow testing.>0: Limit the number of intersections.

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.SamplesPerPixel
cvars.description: Sets the samples-per-pixel for directional light occlusion (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Shadows.Translucency
cvars.description: 0: Translucent material will not cast shadow (by default).1: Translucent material cast approximate translucent shadows based on opacity (Very expensive).

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Sky.HairOcclusionThreshold
cvars.description: Define the number of hair that need to be crossed, before casting occlusion (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight
cvars.description: Enables ray tracing SkyLight (default = 0)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.DecoupleSampleGeneration
cvars.description: Decouples sample generation from ray traversal (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.Denoiser
cvars.description: Denoising options (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.EnableMaterials
cvars.description: Enables material shader binding for shadow rays. If this is disabled, then a default trivial shader is used. (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.EnableTwoSidedGeometry
cvars.description: Enables two-sided geometry when tracing shadow rays (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.HairVoxel
cvars.description: Include hair voxel representation to estimate sky occlusion

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.MaxRayDistance
cvars.description: Sets the max ray distance for ray tracing SkyLight (default = 1.0e7)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.MaxShadowThickness
cvars.description: Sets the max shadow thickness for translucent materials for ray tracing SkyLight (default = 1.0e3)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.SamplesPerPixel
cvars.description: Sets the samples-per-pixel for ray tracing SkyLight (default = -1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.Sampling.StopLevel
cvars.description: Sets the stop level for MIP-sampling (default = 0)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.SkyLight.ScreenPercentage
cvars.description: Screen percentage at which to evaluate sky occlusion

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency
cvars.description: -1: Value driven by postprocess volume (default) 0: ray tracing translucency off (use raster) 1: ray tracing translucency enabled

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.DirectLighting
cvars.description: Enables ray tracing translucency direct lighting (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.EmissiveAndIndirectLighting
cvars.description: Enables ray tracing translucency emissive and indirect lighting (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.HeightFog
cvars.description: Enables height fog in ray traced Translucency (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.MaxRayDistance
cvars.description: Sets the maximum ray distance for ray traced translucency rays. When ray shortening is used, skybox will not be sampled in RT translucency pass and will be composited later, together with local reflection captures. Negative values turn off this optimization. (default = -1 (infinite rays))

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.MaxRefractionRays
cvars.description: Sets the maximum number of refraction rays for ray traced translucency (default = -1 (max bounces driven by postprocessing volume)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.MaxRoughness
cvars.description: Sets the maximum roughness until which ray tracing reflections will be visible (default = -1 (max roughness driven by postprocessing volume))

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.MinRayDistance
cvars.description: Sets the minimum ray distance for ray traced translucency rays. Actual translucency ray length is computed as Lerp(MaxRayDistance, MinRayDistance, Roughness), i.e. translucency rays become shorter when traced from rougher surfaces. (default = -1 (infinite rays))

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.PrimaryRayBias
cvars.description: Sets the bias to be subtracted from the primary ray TMax in ray traced Translucency. Larger bias reduces the chance of opaque objects being intersected in ray traversal, saving performance, but at the risk of skipping some thin translucent objects in proximity of opaque objects. (recommended range: 0.00001 - 0.1) (default = 0.00001)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.Refraction
cvars.description: Enables refraction in ray traced Translucency (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.SamplesPerPixel
cvars.description: Sets the samples-per-pixel for Translucency (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Translucency.Shadows
cvars.description: Enables shadows in ray tracing translucency) -1: Shadows driven by postprocessing volume (default) 0: Shadows disabled 1: Hard shadows 2: Soft area shadows

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Transmission.MeanFreePathType
cvars.description: 0: Use the extinction scale from subsurface profile as MFP.1: Use the max MFP from Subsurface profile to generate samples for transmission (Substrate is not supported).

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Transmission.RejectionSamplingTrials
cvars.description: Determines the number of rejection-sampling trials (default = 0)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Transmission.SamplingTechnique
cvars.description: 0: Uses constant tracking of an infinite homogeneous medium 1: Uses constant tracking of a finite homogeneous medium whose extent is determined by transmission sampling distance (default)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.Transmission.TransmissionSamplingDistanceCulling
cvars.description: Enables visibility testing to cull transmission sampling distance (default = 1)

cvars.name: r.RayTracing.UseTextureLod
cvars.description: Enable automatic texture mip level selection in ray tracing material shaders. 0: highest resolution mip level is used for all texture (default). 1: texture LOD is approximated based on total ray length, output resolution and texel density at hit point (ray cone method).

cvars.name: r.RDG.AsyncCompute
cvars.description: Controls the async compute policy. 0:disabled, no async compute is used; 1:enabled for passes tagged for async compute (default); 2:enabled for all compute passes implemented to use the compute command list;

cvars.name: r.RDG.Breakpoint
cvars.description: Breakpoint in debugger when certain conditions are met. 0: off (default); 1: On an RDG warning; 2: When a graph / pass matching the debug filters compiles; 3: When a graph / pass matching the debug filters executes; 4: When a graph / pass / resource matching the debug filters is created or destroyed;

cvars.name: r.RDG.ClobberResources
cvars.description: Clears all render targets and texture / buffer UAVs with the requested clear color at allocation time. Useful for debugging. 0:off (default); 1: 1000 on RGBA channels; 2: NaN on RGBA channels; 3: +INFINITY on RGBA channels.

cvars.name: r.RDG.CullPasses
cvars.description: The graph will cull passes with unused outputs. 0:off; 1:on(default);

cvars.name: r.RDG.Debug
cvars.description: Allow to output warnings for inefficiencies found during wiring and execution of the passes. 0: disabled; 1: emit warning once (default); 2: emit warning everytime issue is detected.

cvars.name: r.RDG.Debug.DisableTransientResources
cvars.description: Filters out transient resources from the transient allocator. Use r.rdg.debug.resourcefilter to specify the filter. Defaults to all resources if enabled.

cvars.name: r.RDG.Debug.ExtendResourceLifetimes
cvars.description: Extends the resource lifetimes of resources (or a specific resource filter specified by r.RDG.Debug.ResourceFilter) so that they cannot overlap memory with any other resource within the graph. Useful to debug if transient aliasing is causing issues. 0: disabled (default); 1: enabled;

cvars.name: r.RDG.Debug.FlushGPU
cvars.description: Enables flushing the GPU after every pass. Disables async compute (r.RDG.AsyncCompute=0) and parallel execute (r.RDG.ParallelExecute=0) when set. 0: disabled (default); 1: enabled.

cvars.name: r.RDG.Debug.GraphFilter
cvars.description: Filters certain debug events to a specific graph. Set to 'None' to reset.

cvars.name: r.RDG.Debug.PassFilter
cvars.description: Filters certain debug events to specific passes. Set to 'None' to reset.

cvars.name: r.RDG.Debug.ResourceFilter
cvars.description: Filters certain debug events to a specific resource. Set to 'None' to reset.

cvars.name: r.RDG.Events
cvars.description: Controls how RDG events are emitted. 0: off; 1: events are enabled and RDG_EVENT_SCOPE_FINAL is respected; (default) 2: all events are enabled (RDG_EVENT_SCOPE_FINAL is ignored);

cvars.name: r.RDG.ImmediateMode
cvars.description: Executes passes as they get created. Useful to have a callstack of the wiring code when crashing in the pass' lambda.

cvars.name: r.RDG.MergeRenderPasses
cvars.description: The graph will merge identical, contiguous render passes into a single render pass. 0:off; 1:on(default);

cvars.name: r.RDG.OverlapUAVs
cvars.description: RDG will overlap UAV work when requested; if disabled, UAV barriers are always inserted.

cvars.name: r.RDG.ParallelDestruction
cvars.description: RDG will destruct the graph using an async task. 0: graph destruction is done synchronously; 1: graph destruction may be done asynchronously (default);

cvars.name: r.RDG.ParallelExecute
cvars.description: Whether to enable parallel execution of passes when supported. 0: off; 1: on (default)

cvars.name: r.RDG.ParallelExecute.PassMax
cvars.description: The maximum span of contiguous passes eligible for parallel execution for the span to be offloaded to a task.

cvars.name: r.RDG.ParallelExecute.PassMin
cvars.description: The minimum span of contiguous passes eligible for parallel execution for the span to be offloaded to a task.

cvars.name: r.RDG.ParallelExecuteStress
cvars.description: Stress tests the parallel execution path by launching one task per pass. Render pass merging is also disabled.

cvars.name: r.RDG.ParallelSetup
cvars.description: RDG will setup passes in parallel when prompted by calls to FRDGBuilder::FlushSetupQueue. 0: pass setup is done synchronously in AddPass; 1: pass setup is done asynchronously (default);

cvars.name: r.RDG.TransientAllocator
cvars.description: RDG will use the RHITransientResourceAllocator to allocate all transient resources. 0: disables the transient allocator; 1: enables the transient allocator (default); 2: enables the transient allocator for resources with FastVRAM flag only

cvars.name: r.RDG.TransientAllocator.IndirectArgumentBuffers
cvars.description: Whether indirect argument buffers should use transient resource allocator. Default: 0

cvars.name: r.RDG.TransientExtractedResources
cvars.description: RDG will allocate extracted resources as transient, unless explicitly marked non-transient by the user. 0: disables external transient resources; 1: enables external transient resources (default); 2: force enables all external transient resources (not recommended);

cvars.name: r.RDG.TransitionLog
cvars.description: Logs resource transitions to the console. 0: disabled(default); >0: enabled for N frames; <0: enabled;

cvars.name: r.RDG.Validation
cvars.description: Enables validation of correctness in API calls and pass parameter dependencies. 0: disabled; 1: enabled (default);

cvars.name: r.RDG.VerboseCSVStats
cvars.description: Controls the verbosity of CSV profiling stats for RDG. 0: emits one CSV profile for graph execution; 1: emits a CSV profile for each phase of graph execution.

cvars.name: r.ReadBuffer.AlignSize
cvars.description: The alignment size (in instances) to allocate in blocks for rendering read buffers. i.e. 64*1024 = 256k for a float buffer

cvars.name: r.ReadBuffer.MaxRenderingBytesAllocatedPerFrame
cvars.description: The maximum number of transient rendering read buffer bytes to allocate before we start panic logging who is doing the allocations

cvars.name: r.ReadBuffer.MinSize
cvars.description: The minimum size (in instances) to allocate in blocks for rendering read buffers. i.e. 256*1024 = 1mb for a float buffer

cvars.name: r.RectLightAtlas.Debug
cvars.description: Enable rect. light atlas debug information.

cvars.name: r.RectLightAtlas.Debug.MipLevel
cvars.description: Set MIP level for visualizing atlas texture in debug mode.

cvars.name: r.RectLightAtlas.FilterQuality
cvars.description: Define the filtering quality used for filtering texture (0:Box filter, 1:Gaussian filter).

cvars.name: r.RectLightAtlas.ForceUpdate
cvars.description: Force rect. light atlas update very frame.

cvars.name: r.RectLightAtlas.MaxResolution
cvars.description: The maximum resolution for storing rect. light textures.

cvars.name: r.RectLightAtlas.MaxTextureRatio
cvars.description: Define the max Width/Height or Height/Width ratio that a texture can have.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionCapture.EnableDeferredReflectionsAndSkyLighting
cvars.description: Whether to evaluate deferred reflections and sky contribution when rendering reflection captures.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionCapture.EnableLightFunctions
cvars.description: 0. Disable light functions in reflection/sky light capture (default). Others. Enable light functions.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionCaptureResolution
cvars.description: Set the resolution for all reflection capture cubemaps. Should be set via project's Render Settings. Must be power of 2. Defaults to 128.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionCaptureSupersampleFactor
cvars.description: Super sample factor when rendering reflection captures. Default = 1, no super sampling Maximum clamped to 8.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionCaptureUpdateEveryFrame
cvars.description: When set, reflection captures will constantly be scheduled for update.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionEnvironment
cvars.description: Whether to render the reflection environment feature, which implements local reflections through Reflection Capture actors. 0: off 1: on and blend with scene (default) 2: on and overwrite scene (only in non-shipping builds)

cvars.name: r.ReflectionEnvironmentBeginMixingRoughness
cvars.description: Min roughness value at which to begin mixing reflection captures with lightmap indirect diffuse.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionEnvironmentEndMixingRoughness
cvars.description: Min roughness value at which to end mixing reflection captures with lightmap indirect diffuse.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionEnvironmentLightmapMixBasedOnRoughness
cvars.description: Whether to reduce lightmap mixing with reflection captures for very smooth surfaces. This is useful to make sure reflection captures match SSR / planar reflections in brightness.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionEnvironmentLightmapMixing
cvars.description: Whether to mix indirect specular from reflection captures with indirect diffuse from lightmaps for rough surfaces.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionEnvironmentLightmapMixLargestWeight
cvars.description: When set to 1 can be used to clamp lightmap mixing such that only darkening from lightmaps are applied to reflection captures.

cvars.name: r.ReflectionMethod
cvars.description: 0 - None. Reflections can come from placed Reflection Captures, Planar Reflections and Skylight but no global reflection method will be used. 1 - Lumen. Use Lumen Reflections, which supports Screen / Software / Hardware Ray Tracing together and integrates with Lumen Global Illumination for rough reflections and Global Illumination seen in reflections. 2 - SSR. Standalone Screen Space Reflections. Low cost, but limited by screen space information.

cvars.name: r.Reflections.Denoiser
cvars.description: Choose the denoising algorithm. 0: Disabled; 1: Forces the default denoiser of the renderer; 2: GScreenSpaceDenoiser which may be overriden by a third party plugin (default).

cvars.name: r.Reflections.Denoiser.PreConvolution
cvars.description: Number of pre-convolution passes (default = 1).

cvars.name: r.Reflections.Denoiser.ReconstructionSamples
cvars.description: Maximum number of samples for the reconstruction pass (default = 8).

cvars.name: r.Reflections.Denoiser.TemporalAccumulation
cvars.description: Accumulates the samples over multiple frames.

cvars.name: r.Refraction.Blur
cvars.description: Enables rough refractions, i.e. blurring of the background.

cvars.name: r.Refraction.Blur.MaxExposedLuminance
cvars.description: Clamp scene pre-exposed luminance to this maximum value. It helps to reduce bright specular highlight flickering, even when r.Refraction.Blur.TemporalAA=1.

cvars.name: r.Refraction.Blur.MaxStandardDeviationInScreenRatio
cvars.description: This will clamp the maximum refraction blur radius on screen.

cvars.name: r.Refraction.Blur.TemporalAA
cvars.description: Enables temporal AA of the scene color buffer in order to avoid flickering in rough refractions.

cvars.name: r.Refraction.OffsetQuality
cvars.description: When enabled, the offset buffer is made float for higher quality. This is important to maintain the softness of the blurred scene buffer.

cvars.name: r.RefractionQuality
cvars.description: Defines the distorion/refraction quality which allows to adjust for quality or performance. <=0: off (fastest) 1: low quality (not yet implemented) 2: normal quality (default) 3: high quality (e.g. color fringe, not yet implemented)

cvars.name: r.RHICmdBufferWriteLocks
cvars.description: Only relevant with an RHI thread. Debugging option to diagnose problems with buffered locks.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdBypass
cvars.description: Whether to bypass the rhi command list and send the rhi commands immediately. 0: Disable (required for the multithreaded renderer) 1: Enable (convenient for debugging low level graphics API calls, can suppress artifacts from multithreaded renderer code)

cvars.name: r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks
cvars.description: If true, then we flush the render thread tasks every pass. For issue diagnosis. This is a main switch for more granular cvars.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksBasePass
cvars.description: Wait for completion of parallel render thread tasks at the end of the base pass. A more granular version of r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks. If either r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks or r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksBasePass is > 0 we will flush.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksPrePass
cvars.description: Wait for completion of parallel render thread tasks at the end of the pre pass. A more granular version of r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks. If either r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks or r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksPrePass is > 0 we will flush.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksShadowPass
cvars.description: Wait for completion of parallel render thread tasks at the end of each shadow pass. A more granular version of r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks. If either r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks or r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksShadowPass is > 0 we will flush.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksSingleLayerWater
cvars.description: Wait for completion of parallel render thread tasks at the end of Single layer water. A more granular version of r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks. If either r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks or r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksSingleLayerWater is > 0 we will flush.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksTranslucentPass
cvars.description: Wait for completion of parallel render thread tasks at the end of the translucent pass. A more granular version of r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks. If either r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks or r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksTranslucentPass is > 0 we will flush.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksVelocityPass
cvars.description: Wait for completion of parallel render thread tasks at the end of the velocity pass. A more granular version of r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks. If either r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasks or r.RHICmdFlushRenderThreadTasksVelocityPass is > 0 we will flush.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdMaxOutstandingMemoryBeforeFlush
cvars.description: In kilobytes. The amount of outstanding memory before the RHI will force a flush. This should generally be set high enough that it doesn't happen on typical frames.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdMergeSmallDeferredContexts
cvars.description: When it can be determined, merge small parallel translate tasks based on r.RHICmdMinDrawsPerParallelCmdList.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdMinCmdlistForParallelSubmit
cvars.description: Minimum number of parallel translate command lists to submit. If there are fewer than this number, they just run on the RHI thread and immediate context.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdMinDrawsPerParallelCmdList
cvars.description: The minimum number of draws per cmdlist. If the total number of draws is less than this, then no parallel work will be done at all. This can't always be honored or done correctly.

cvars.name: r.RHICmdWidth
cvars.description: Controls the task granularity of a great number of things in the parallel renderer.

cvars.name: r.RHIValidation.DebugBreak.Transitions
cvars.description: Controls whether the debugger should break when a validation error is encountered. 0: disabled; 1: break in the debugger if a validation error is encountered.

cvars.name: r.SceneCapture.AllowRenderInMainRenderer
cvars.description: Whether to allow SceneDepth & DeviceDepth scene capture to render in the main renderer as an optimization. 0: render as an independent renderer. 1: render as part of the main renderer if Render in Main Renderer is enabled on scene capture component.

cvars.name: r.SceneCapture.CullByDetailMode
cvars.description: Whether to prevent scene capture updates according to the current detail mode

cvars.name: r.SceneCapture.DepthPrepassOptimization
cvars.description: Whether to apply optimized render path when capturing depth prepass for scene capture 2D. Experimental! Warning: turning it on means rendering after depth pre-pass (e.g. SingleLayerWater) is ignored, hence result is different from when CVar is off.

cvars.name: r.SceneCapture.EnableOrthographicTiling
cvars.description: Render the scene in n frames (i.e TileCount) - Ignored in Perspective mode, works only in Orthographic mode and when r.SceneCapture.OverrideOrthographicTilingValues is on.

cvars.name: r.SceneCapture.OrthographicNumXTiles
cvars.description: Number of X tiles to render. Ignored in Perspective mode, works only in Orthographic mode and when r.SceneCapture.OverrideOrthographicTilingValues is on.

cvars.name: r.SceneCapture.OrthographicNumYTiles
cvars.description: Number of Y tiles to render. Ignored in Perspective mode, works only in Orthographic mode and when r.SceneCapture.OverrideOrthographicTilingValues is on.

cvars.name: r.SceneCapture.OverrideOrthographicTilingValues
cvars.description: Override defined orthographic values from SceneCaptureComponent2D - Ignored in Perspective mode.

cvars.name: r.SceneColorFormat
cvars.description: Defines the memory layout (RGBA) used for the scene color (affects performance, mostly through bandwidth, quality especially with translucency). 0: PF_B8G8R8A8 32Bit (mostly for testing, likely to unusable with HDR) 1: PF_A2B10G10R10 32Bit 2: PF_FloatR11G11B10 32Bit 3: PF_FloatRGB 32Bit 4: PF_FloatRGBA 64Bit (default, might be overkill, especially if translucency is mostly using SeparateTranslucency) 5: PF_A32B32G32R32F 128Bit (unreasonable but good for testing)

cvars.name: r.SceneColorFringe.Max
cvars.description: Allows to clamp the postprocess setting (in percent, Scene chromatic aberration / color fringe to simulate an artifact that happens in real-world lens, mostly visible in the image corners) -1: don't clamp (default) -2: to test extreme fringe

cvars.name: r.SceneColorFringeQuality
cvars.description: 0: off but best for performance 1: 3 texture samples (default)

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling
cvars.description: Enable/Disable scene culling. While enabled, it will only build the instance hierarchy if used by any system - currently that corresponds to Nanite being enabled. Forces a recreate of all render state since (at present) there is only an incremental update path.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.Async.Query
cvars.description: Enable/Disable async culling scene queries.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.Async.Update
cvars.description: Enable/Disable async culling scene update.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.DebugRenderMode
cvars.description: SceneCulling debug render mode. 0 = Disabled (default) 1 = Enabled

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.ExplicitCellBounds
cvars.description: Enable to to construct explicit cell bounds by processing the instance bounds as the scene is updated. Adds some GPU cost to the update but this is typically more than paid for by improved culling.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.MaxCellSize
cvars.description: Hierarchy max cell size. Objects with larger bounds will be classified as uncullable.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.MinCellSize
cvars.description: Set the minimum cell size & level in the hierarchy, rounded to nearest POT. Clamps the level for object footprints. This trades culling effectiveness for construction cost and memory use. The minimum cell size is (will be) used for precomputing the key for static ISMs. Currently read-only as there is implementation to rebuild the whole structure on a change.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.Precomputed
cvars.description: Enable/Disable precomputed spatial hashes for scene culling.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.SmallFootprintSideThreshold
cvars.description: Queries with a smaller footprint (maximum) side (in number of cells in the lowest level) go down the footprint based path. The default (16) <=> a footprint of 16x16x16 cells or 8 blocks

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.TreatInstancedDynamicAsUnCullable
cvars.description: If this is turned on (default), dynamic primitives with instances are treated as uncullable (not put into the hierarchy and instead brute-forced on the GPU). This significantly reduces the hierarchy update cost on the CPU and for scenes with a large proportion of static elements, does not increase the GPU cost.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.ValidateAllInstanceAllocations
cvars.description: Perform validation of all instance IDs stored in the grid. This is very slow.

cvars.name: r.SceneCulling.ValidateGPUData
cvars.description: Perform readback and validation of uploaded GPU-data against CPU copy. This is quite slow and forces CPU/GPU syncs.

cvars.name: r.SceneDepthHZBAsyncCompute
cvars.description: Selects whether HZB for scene depth buffer should be built with async compute. 0: Don't use async compute (default) 1: Use async compute, start as soon as possible 2: Use async compute, start after ComputeLightGrid.CompactLinks pass

cvars.name: r.SceneRenderCleanUpMode
cvars.description: Controls when to perform clean up of the scene renderer. 0: clean up is performed immediately after render on the render thread. 1: clean up deferred until the start of the next scene render on the render thread. 2: clean up deferred until the start of the next scene render on the render thread, with some work distributed to an async task. (default)

cvars.name: r.SceneRenderTargetResizeMethod
cvars.description: Control the scene render target resize method: (This value is only used in game mode and on windowing platforms unless 'r.SceneRenderTargetsResizingMethodForceOverride' is enabled.) 0: Resize to match requested render size (Default) (Least memory use, can cause stalls when size changes e.g. ScreenPercentage) 1: Fixed to screen resolution. 2: Expands to encompass the largest requested render dimension. (Most memory use, least prone to allocation stalls.)

cvars.name: r.SceneRenderTargetResizeMethodForceOverride
cvars.description: Forces 'r.SceneRenderTargetResizeMethod' to be respected on all configurations. 0: Disabled. 1: Enabled.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.AllowDistributedCompilation
cvars.description: If 0, only local (spawned by the engine) ShaderCompileWorkers will be used. If 1, SCWs will be distributed using one of several possible backends (XGE, FASTBuild, SN-DBS)

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.CrashOnHungShaderMaps
cvars.description: If set to 1, the shader compiler will crash on hung shadermaps.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDDCKeyAsset
cvars.description: if set, assets containing this name will print their full DDC key hash information when requested

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDiscardCacheOutputs
cvars.description: if != 0, cache outputs are discarded (not added to the output map) for debugging purposes. Eliminates usefulness of the cache, but allows repeated triggering of the same jobs for stress testing (for example, rapid undo/redo in the Material editor).

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDumpDetailedShaderSource
cvars.description: If true, and if the preprocessed job cache is enabled, this will dump multiple copies of the shader source for any job which has debug output enabled: 1. The unmodified output of the preprocessing step as constructed by the PreprocessShader implementation of the IShaderFormat (Preprocessed_.usf 2. The stripped version of the above (with comments, line directives, and whitespace-only lines removed), which is the version hashed for inclusion in the job input hash when the preprocessed job cache is enabled (Stripped_.usf) 3. The final source as passed to the platform compiler (this will differ if the IShaderFormat compile function applies further modifications to the source after preprocessing; otherwise this will be the same as 2 above (.usf) If false, or the preprocessed job cache is disabled, this will simply dump whatever source is passed to the compiler (equivalent to either 1 or 3 depending on if the IShaderFormat implementation modifies the source in the compile step.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDumpJobDiagnostics
cvars.description: If true, all diagnostic messages (errors and warnings) for each shader job will be dumped alongside other debug data (in Diagnostics.txt)

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDumpJobInputHashes
cvars.description: If true, the job input hash will be dumped alongside other debug data (in InputHash.txt)

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDumpShaderCode
cvars.description: If true, each shader job will dump a ShaderCode.bin containing the contents of the output shader code object (the contents of this can differ for each shader format; note that this is the data that is hashed to produce the OutputHash.txt file)

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugDumpWorkerInputs
cvars.description: If true, worker input files will be saved for each individual compile job alongside other debug data (note that r.DumpShaderDebugInfo must also be enabled for this to function)

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugStallDDCQuery
cvars.description: For debugging, a value in milliseconds to stall in the DDC completion callback, to help reproduce threading bugs, or simulate higher latency DDC for perf testing.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugStallSubmitJob
cvars.description: For debugging, a value in milliseconds to stall in SubmitJob, to help reproduce threading bugs.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DebugValidateJobCache
cvars.description: Enables debug mode for job cache which will fully execute all jobs and validate that job outputs with matching input hashes match.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DistributedControllerTimeout
cvars.description: Maximum number of seconds we expect to pass between getting distributed controller complete a task (this is used to detect problems with the distribution controllers).

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DistributedMinBatchSize
cvars.description: Minimum number of shaders to compile with a distributed controller. Smaller number of shaders will compile locally.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DumpCompileJobInputs
cvars.description: if != 0, unpreprocessed input of the shader compiler jobs will be dumped into the debug directory for closer inspection. This is a debugging feature which is disabled by default.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DumpDDCKeys
cvars.description: if != 0, DDC keys for each shadermap will be dumped into project's Saved directory (ShaderDDCKeys subdirectory)

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.DumpDebugInfoForCacheHits
cvars.description: If true, debug info (via IShaderFormat::OutputDebugData) will be output for all jobs including duplicates and cache/DDC hits. If false, only jobs that actually executed compilation will dump debug info.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.EmitWarningsOnLoad
cvars.description: When 1, shader compiler warnings are emitted to the log for all shaders as they are loaded.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCache
cvars.description: if != 0, shader compiler cache (based on the unpreprocessed input hash) will be disabled. By default, it is enabled.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCacheDDC
cvars.description: Skips compilation of all shaders on Material and Material Instance PostLoad and relies on on-demand shader compilation to compile what is needed.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCacheDDCCookEnableRemotePolicy
cvars.description: If true, individual shader jobs will be cached to remote/shared DDC instances in all cook commandlet only; if false they will only cache to DDC instances on the local machine.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCacheDDCEnableRemotePolicy
cvars.description: If true, individual shader jobs will be cached to remote/shared DDC instances in all operation modes; if false they will only cache to DDC instances on the local machine.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.JobCacheOverflowReducePercent
cvars.description: When shader compiler job cache memory overflows, reduce memory to this percentage of the maximum. Reduces overhead relative to cleaning up items one at a time when at max budget.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.MaxDumpedShaderSources
cvars.description: Maximum number of preprocessed shader sources to dump as a build artifact on shader compile errors. By default 10.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryMB
cvars.description: if != 0, shader compiler cache will be limited to this many megabytes (16GB by default). If 0, the usage will be unlimited. Minimum of this or r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryPercent applies.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryPercent
cvars.description: if != 0, shader compiler cache will be limited to this percentage of available physical RAM (5% by default). If 0, the usage will be unlimited. Minimum of this or r.ShaderCompiler.MaxJobCacheMemoryMB applies.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.ParallelInProcess
cvars.description: EXPERIMENTAL- If true, shader compilation will be executed in-process in parallel. Note that this will serialize if the legacy preprocessor is enabled.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.ParallelSubmitJobs
cvars.description: if != 0, FShaderJobCache::SubmitJobs will run in multiple parallel tasks, instead of the game thread.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.PerShaderDDCAsync
cvars.description: if != 0, Per-shader DDC queries will run async, instead of in the SubmitJobs task.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.PerShaderDDCCook
cvars.description: If true, per-shader DDC caching will be enabled during cooks.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.PerShaderDDCGlobal
cvars.description: if != 0, Per-shader DDC queries enabled for global and default shaders.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.PreprocessedJobCache
cvars.description: If enabled will shader compile jobs will be preprocessed at submission time in the cook process (when the job is queued) and generate job input hashes based on preprocessed source.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.PrintIndividualProcessStats
cvars.description: If true, in a multiprocess cook, stats will be printed for each individual process (as well as aggregated across all).

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.RecompileShadersOnSave
cvars.description: When enabled, the editor will attempt to recompile any shader files that have changed when saved. Useful for iterating on shaders in the editor. Default: false

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.ShadermapCompilationTimeout
cvars.description: Maximum number of seconds a single shadermap (which can be comprised of multiple jobs) can be compiled after being considered hung.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.StatsPrintoutInterval
cvars.description: Minimum interval (in seconds) between printing out debugging stats (by default, no closer than once per minute).

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.ThreadLocalPreprocessBuffer
cvars.description: Amount to preallocate for preprocess output per worker thread, to save reallocation overhead in the preprocessor.

cvars.name: r.ShaderCompiler.TooLongIOThresholdSeconds
cvars.description: By default, task files for SCW will be read/written sequentially, but if we ever spend more than this time (0.3s by default) doing that, we'll switch to parallel.We don't default to parallel writes as it increases the CPU overhead from the shader compiler.

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.Baseline.Deferred.PS
cvars.description: Minimum number of instructions for pixel shaders in deferred shading (default=111)

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.Baseline.Deferred.UnlitPS
cvars.description: Minimum number of instructions for unlit material pixel shaders in deferred shading (default=33)

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.Baseline.Deferred.VS
cvars.description: Minimum number of instructions for vertex shaders in deferred shading (default=41)

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.Baseline.Forward.PS
cvars.description: Minimum number of instructions for pixel shaders in forward shading (default=635)

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.Baseline.Forward.UnlitPS
cvars.description: Minimum number of instructions for unlit material pixel shaders in forward shading (default=47)

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.Baseline.Forward.VS
cvars.description: Minimum number of instructions for vertex shaders in forward shading (default=134)

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.CacheShaders
cvars.description: If non zero, store the shader complexity shaders in the material shader map, to prevent compile on-the-fly lag. (default=0)

cvars.name: r.ShaderComplexity.MobileMaskedCostMultiplier
cvars.description: Extra cost of masked materials if we do not use masked in early Z optimization

cvars.name: r.ShaderDevelopmentMode
cvars.description: 0: Default, 1: Enable various shader development utilities, such as the ability to retry on failed shader compile, and extra logging as shaders are compiled.

cvars.name: r.ShaderLibrary.PrintExtendedStats
cvars.description: if != 0, shader library will produce extended stats, including the textual representation

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.AlwaysGenerateOSCache
cvars.description: 1 generates the cache every run, 0 generates it only when it is missing.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.AutoSaveTime
cvars.description: Set the time where any logged PSO's will be saved if the number is < r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveAfterPSOsLogged. Disabled when r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveAfterPSOsLogged is 0

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.AutoSaveTimeBoundPSO
cvars.description: Set the time where any logged PSO's will be saved when -logpso is on the command line.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BackgroundBatchSize
cvars.description: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to compile in a single batch operation when compiling in the background. Defaults to a maximum of 1 per frame, due to async. file IO it is less in practice.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BackgroundBatchTime
cvars.description: The target time (in ms) to spend precompiling each frame when in the background or 0.0 to disable. When precompiling is faster the batch size will grow and when slower will shrink to attempt to occupy the full amount. Defaults to 0.0 (off).

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BatchSize
cvars.description: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to compile in a single batch operation when compiling takes priority. Defaults to a maximum of 50 per frame, due to async. file IO it is less in practice.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.BatchTime
cvars.description: The target time (in ms) to spend precompiling each frame when compiling takes priority or 0.0 to disable. When precompiling is faster the batch size will grow and when slower will shrink to attempt to occupy the full amount. Defaults to 16.0 (max. ms per-frame of precompilation).

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.ClearOSCache
cvars.description: 1 Enables the OS level clear after install, 0 disables it.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.DoNotPrecompileComputePSO
cvars.description: Disables precompilation of compute PSOs (replayed from a recorded file) on start. This is a safety switch in case things go wrong

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.Enabled
cvars.description: 1 Enables the PipelineFileCache, 0 disables it.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.ExcludePrecachePSO
cvars.description: 1 excludes saving runtime-precached graphics PSOs in the file cache, 0 (default) includes them. Excluding precached PSOs currently requires r.PSOPrecaching = 1 and r.PSOPrecache.Validation != 0.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.GameFileMaskEnabled
cvars.description: Set non zero to use GameFileMask during PSO precompile - recording should always save out the usage masks to make that data availble when needed.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.GlobalShadersOnlyWhenPSOPrecaching
cvars.description: Only compile PSOs from the GlobalShader cache when runtime PSOPrecaching is enabled (default disabled)

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.LazyLoadShadersWhenPSOCacheIsPresent
cvars.description: Non-Zero: If we load a PSO cache, then lazy load from the shader code library. This assumes the PSO cache is more or less complete. This will only work on RHIs that support the library+Hash CreateShader API (GRHISupportsLazyShaderCodeLoading == true).

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.LogPSO
cvars.description: 1 Logs new PSO entries into the file cache and allows saving.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.MaxPrecompileTime
cvars.description: The maximum time to allow a PSO to be precompiled. if greather than 0, the amount of wall time we will allow pre-compile of PSOs and then switch to background processing.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.MinBindCount
cvars.description: The minimum bind count to allow a PSO to be precompiled. Changes to this value will not affect PSOs that have already been removed from consideration.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.OnlyOpenUserCache
cvars.description: If > 0, only the user cache file will be opened, and the static file cache will not be opened. Defaults to 0.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PrecompileBatchSize
cvars.description: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to compile in a single batch operation when pre-optimizing the cache. Defaults to a maximum of 50 per frame, due to async. file IO it is less in practice.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PrecompileBatchTime
cvars.description: The target time (in ms) to spend precompiling each frame when cpre-optimizing or 0.0 to disable. When precompiling is faster the batch size will grow and when slower will shrink to attempt to occupy the full amount. Defaults to 10.0 (off).

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PreCompileMask
cvars.description: Mask used to precompile the cache. Defaults to all PSOs (-1)

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PreOptimizeEnabled
cvars.description: Set non zero to PreOptimize PSOs - this allows some PSOs to be compiled in the foreground before going in to game

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.PrintNewPSODescriptors
cvars.description: 1 prints descriptions for all new PSO entries to the log/console while 0 does not. 2 prints additional details about graphics PSO. Defaults to 0.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.ReportPSO
cvars.description: 1 reports new PSO entries via a delegate, but does not record or modify any cache file.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveAfterPSOsLogged
cvars.description: Set the number of PipelineStateObjects to log before automatically saving. 0 will disable automatic saving (which is the default now, as automatic saving is found to be broken).

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveBoundPSOLog
cvars.description: If > 0 then a log of all bound PSOs for this run of the program will be saved to a writable user cache file. Defaults to 0 but is forced on with -logpso.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.SaveUserCache
cvars.description: If > 0 then any missed PSOs will be saved to a writable user cache file for subsequent runs to load and avoid in-game hitches. Enabled by default on macOS only.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.StartupMode
cvars.description: Sets the startup mode for the PSO cache, determining what the cache does after initialisation: 0: Precompilation is paused and nothing will compile until a call to ResumeBatching(). 1: Precompilation is enabled in the 'Fast' mode. 2: Precompilation is enabled in the 'Background' mode. Default is 1.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.UserCacheUnusedElementCheckPeriod
cvars.description: The amount of time in days between running the garbage collection on unused PSOs in the user cache. Use a negative value to disable.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCache.UserCacheUnusedElementRetainDays
cvars.description: The amount of time in days to keep unused PSO entries in the cache.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCacheTools.IgnoreObsoleteStableCacheFiles
cvars.description: When set to the default value of 0, building the cache (and usually the whole cook) will fail if any .spc file can't be loaded, to prevent further testing. By setting to 1, a project may choose to ignore this instead (warning will still be issued).

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelineCacheTools.IncludeComputePSODuringCook
cvars.description: 0 disables cook-time addition, 1 enables cook-time addition, 2 adds only Niagara PSOs.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPipelines
cvars.description: Enable using Shader pipelines.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint
cvars.description: ShaderPrint debugging toggle.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.DrawOccludedLines
cvars.description: Whether to draw occluded lines using checkboarding and lower opacity.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.FontSize
cvars.description: ShaderPrint font size.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.FontSpacingX
cvars.description: ShaderPrint horizontal spacing between symbols.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.FontSpacingY
cvars.description: ShaderPrint vertical spacing between symbols.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.Lock
cvars.description: Lock the line drawing.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxCharacters
cvars.description: ShaderPrint output buffer size.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxLine
cvars.description: ShaderPrint max line count.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxTriangle
cvars.description: ShaderPrint max triangle count.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.MaxWidget
cvars.description: ShaderPrint max widget count.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom
cvars.description: Enable zoom magnification around the mouse cursor.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom.Corner
cvars.description: Select in which corner the zoom magnifer is displayed (0:top-left, 1:top-right, 2:bottom-right, 3:bottom-left).

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom.Factor
cvars.description: Zoom factor for magnification around the mouse cursor.

cvars.name: r.ShaderPrint.Zoom.Pixel
cvars.description: Number of pixels magnified around the mouse cursor.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.AllowCompilingThroughWorkers
cvars.description: Allows shader compilation through external ShaderCompileWorker processes. 1 - (Default) Allows external shader compiler workers 0 - Disallows external shader compiler workers. Will run shader compilation in proc of UE process.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.AllowUniqueSymbols
cvars.description: When enabled, this tells supported shader compilers to generate symbols based on source files. Enabling this can cause a drastic increase in the number of symbol files, enable only if absolutely necessary. This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.BoundsChecking
cvars.description: Whether to enforce bounds-checking & flush-to-zero/ignore for buffer reads & writes in shaders. Defaults to 1 (enabled). Not all shader languages can omit bounds checking.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.CheckLevel
cvars.description: 0 => DO_CHECK=0, DO_GUARD_SLOW=0, 1 => DO_CHECK=1, DO_GUARD_SLOW=0, 2 => DO_CHECK=1, DO_GUARD_SLOW=1 for all shaders.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.ExtraData
cvars.description: Enables generation of extra shader data that can be used at runtime. This includes shader names and other platform specific data. This can add bloat to compiled shaders and can prevent shaders from being deduplicated. This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.FastMath
cvars.description: Whether to use fast-math optimisations in shaders.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.FlowControlMode
cvars.description: Specifies whether the shader compiler should preserve or unroll flow-control in shader code. This is primarily a debugging aid and will override any per-shader or per-material settings if not left at the default value (0). 0: Off (Default) - Entirely at the discretion of the platform compiler or the specific shader/material. 1: Prefer - Attempt to preserve flow-control. 2: Avoid - Attempt to unroll and flatten flow-control.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.ForceDXC
cvars.description: Forces DirectX Shader Compiler (DXC) to be used for all shaders instead of HLSLcc if supported. 1: Force new compiler for all shaders (default) 0: Disable

cvars.name: r.Shaders.GenerateSymbols
cvars.description: Enables generation of data for shader debugging when compiling shaders. This explicitly does not write any shader symbols to disk. This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.Optimize
cvars.description: Whether to optimize shaders. When using graphical debuggers like Nsight it can be useful to disable this on startup. This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.PropagateLocalWorkerOOMs
cvars.description: When set, out-of-memory conditions in a local shader compile worker will be treated as regular out-of-memory conditions and propagated to the main process. This is useful when running in environment with hard memory limits, where it does not matter which process in particular caused us to violate the memory limit.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.RemoveDeadCode
cvars.description: Run a preprocessing step that removes unreferenced code before compiling shaders. This can improve the compilation speed for shaders which include many large utility headers. 0: Keep all input source code. 1: Remove unreferenced code before compilation (Default)

cvars.name: r.Shaders.RemoveUnusedInterpolators
cvars.description: Enables removing unused interpolators mode when compiling shader pipelines. 0: Disable (default) 1: Enable removing unused

cvars.name: r.Shaders.SkipCompression
cvars.description: Skips shader compression after compiling. Shader compression time can be quite significant when using debug shaders. This CVar is only valid in non-shipping/test builds.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.SymbolPathOverride
cvars.description: Override output location of shader symbols. If the path contains the text '{Platform}', that will be replaced with the shader platform string. Empty: use default location Saved/ShaderSymbols/{Platform} This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.Symbols
cvars.description: Enables debugging of shaders in platform specific graphics debuggers. This will generate and write shader symbols. This enables the behavior of both r.Shaders.GenerateSymbols and r.Shaders.WriteSymbols. Enables shader debugging features that require shaders to be recompiled. This compiles shaders with symbols and also includes extra runtime information like shader names. When using graphical debuggers it can be useful to enable this on startup. This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.SymbolsInfo
cvars.description: In lieu of a full set of platform shader PDBs, save out a slimmer ShaderSymbols.Info which contains shader platform hashes and shader debug info. An option for when it is not practical to save PDBs for shaders all the time. This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.UseGBufferRefactor
cvars.description: Whether to use the refactored GBuffer that autogenerates encode/decode functions. Will be removed before UE5 ships.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.Validation
cvars.description: Enabled shader compiler validation warnings and errors.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.WarningsAsErrors
cvars.description: Whether to treat warnings as errors when compiling shaders. (0: disabled (default), 1: global shaders only, 2: all shaders)). This setting may be ignored on older platforms.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.WriteSymbols
cvars.description: Enables writing shader symbols to disk for platforms that support that. This explicitly does not enable generation of shader symbols. This setting can be overriden in any Engine.ini under the [ShaderCompiler] section.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.WriteSymbols.Zip
cvars.description: 0: Export as loose files. 1: Export as an uncompressed archive.

cvars.name: r.Shaders.ZeroInitialise
cvars.description: Whether to enforce zero initialise local variables of primitive type in shaders. Defaults to 1 (enabled). Not all shader languages can omit zero initialisation.

cvars.name: r.ShaderSource.CompressionLevel
cvars.description: Compression level for shader source stored in memory. See FOodleDataCompression::ECompressionLevel enum for supported values; default is SuperFast.

cvars.name: r.ShaderSource.CompressionMethod
cvars.description: Compression method for shader source stored in memory. See FOodleDataCompression::ECompressor enum for supported values; defaults to Mermaid.

cvars.name: r.Shading.EnergyConservation
cvars.description: 0 to disable energy conservation on shading models. 0: off 1: on

cvars.name: r.Shading.EnergyConservation.Format
cvars.description: Energy conservation table format 0: 16bits, 1: 32bits.

cvars.name: r.Shading.EnergyPreservation
cvars.description: 0 to disable energy preservation on shading models, i.e. the energy attenuation on diffuse lighting caused by the specular reflection. Require energy conservation to be enabled 0: off 1: on

cvars.name: r.Shading.FurnaceTest
cvars.description: Enable/disable furnace for shading validation.

cvars.name: r.Shading.FurnaceTest.SampleCount
cvars.description: Number of sampler per pixel used for furnace tests.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.AlwaysAllocateMaxResolutionAtlases
cvars.description: If enabled, will always allocate shadow map atlases at the maximum resolution rather than trimming unused space. This will waste more memory but can possibly reduce render target pool fragmentation and thrash.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CachedShadowsCastFromMovablePrimitives
cvars.description: Whether movable primitives should cast a shadow from cached whole scene shadows (movable point and spot lights). Disabling this can be used to remove the copy of the cached shadowmap.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CachePreshadow
cvars.description: Whether preshadows can be cached as an optimization

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CacheWholeSceneShadows
cvars.description: When enabled, movable point and spot light whole scene shadow depths from static primitives will be cached as an optimization.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CacheWPOPrimitives
cvars.description: Whether primitives whose materials use World Position Offset should be considered movable for cached shadowmaps. Enablings this gives more correct, but slower whole scene shadows from materials that use WPO.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSM.MaxCascades
cvars.description: The maximum number of cascades with which to render dynamic directional light shadows.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSM.TransitionScale
cvars.description: Allows to scale the cascaded shadow map transition region. Clamped within 0..2. 0: no transition (fastest) 1: as specific in the light settings (default) 2: 2x larger than what was specified in the light

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMCaching
cvars.description: 0: Render CSM every frame. 1: Enable CSM caching. (default)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMDepthBias
cvars.description: Constant depth bias used by CSM

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMDepthBoundsTest
cvars.description: Whether to use depth bounds tests rather than stencil tests for the CSM bounds

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMReceiverBias
cvars.description: Receiver bias used by CSM. Value between 0 and 1.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMScissorOptim
cvars.description: Compute optimized scissor rect size to exclude portions of the CSM slices outside the view frustum

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMScrollingOverlapAreaThrottle
cvars.description: The minimum ratio of the overlap area for CSM scrolling.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMShadowDistanceFadeoutMultiplier
cvars.description: Multiplier for the CSM distance fade

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMSlopeScaleDepthBias
cvars.description: Slope scale depth bias used by CSM

cvars.name: r.Shadow.CSMSplitPenumbraScale
cvars.description: Scale applied to the penumbra size of Cascaded Shadow Map splits, useful for minimizing the transition between splits

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser
cvars.description: Choose the denoising algorithm. 0: Disabled (default); 1: Forces the default denoiser of the renderer; 2: GScreenSpaceDenoiser witch may be overriden by a third party plugin.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.HistoryConvolutionSamples
cvars.description: Number of samples to use to convolve the history over time.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.MaxBatchSize
cvars.description: Maximum number of shadow to denoise at the same time.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.PreConvolution
cvars.description: Number of pre-convolution passes (default = 1).

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Denoiser.ReconstructionSamples
cvars.description: Maximum number of samples for the reconstruction pass (default = 16).

cvars.name: r.Shadow.DetectVertexShaderLayerAtRuntime
cvars.description: Forces the compilation of the vslayer shader permutation even if the platform (RHI) does not declare compile-time support through RHISupportsVertexShaderLayer.Enabled by default for windows/SM5 as DX11 almost universally supports this at runtime.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.DistanceScale
cvars.description: Scalability option to trade shadow distance versus performance for directional lights (clamped within a reasonable range). <1: shorter distance 1: normal (default) >1: larger distance

cvars.name: r.Shadow.DrawPreshadowFrustums
cvars.description: visualize preshadow frustums when the shadowfrustums show flag is enabled

cvars.name: r.Shadow.EnableModulatedSelfShadow
cvars.description: Allows modulated shadows to affect the shadow caster. (mobile only)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.FadeExponent
cvars.description: Controls the rate at which shadows are faded out

cvars.name: r.Shadow.FadeResolution
cvars.description: Resolution in texels below which shadows are faded out

cvars.name: r.Shadow.FarShadow.LODDistanceFactor
cvars.description: Multiplier for LOD selection distance when rendering far shadows

cvars.name: r.Shadow.FarShadowDistanceOverride
cvars.description: Overriding far shadow distance for all directional lighst

cvars.name: r.Shadow.FarShadowStaticMeshLODBias
cvars.description: Notice: only selected geometry types (static meshes and landscapes) respect this value.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.FilterMethod
cvars.description: Chooses the shadow filtering method. 0: Uniform PCF (default) 1: PCSS (experimental)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.ForceSerialSingleRenderPass
cvars.description: Force Serial shadow passes to render in 1 pass.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.ForceSingleSampleShadowingFromStationary
cvars.description: Whether to force all components to act as if they have bSingleSampleShadowFromStationaryLights enabled. Useful for scalability when dynamic shadows are disabled.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.FreezeCamera
cvars.description: Debug the shadow methods by allowing to observe the system from outside. 0: default 1: freeze camera at current location

cvars.name: r.Shadow.LightViewConvexHullCull
cvars.description: Enables culling of shadow casters that do not intersect the convex hull of the light origin and view frustum.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.LODDistanceFactor
cvars.description: Multiplier for LOD selection distance when rendering regular shadows (global)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.LODDistanceFactor.CascadeScale
cvars.description: Multiplier for LOD selection distance when rendering regular shadows (scales with cascade index)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MaxCSMResolution
cvars.description: Max square dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering Cascaded Shadow depths. Range 4 to hardware limit. Higher = better quality shadows but at a performance cost.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MaxCSMScrollingStaticShadowSubjects
cvars.description: The maximum number of extra static shadow subjects need to be drawed when scrolling CSM.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MaxNumFarShadowCascades
cvars.description: Max number of far shadow cascades that can be cast from a directional light

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MaxNumPointShadowCacheUpdatesPerFrame
cvars.description: Maximum number of point light shadow cache updates allowed per frame.Only affect updates caused by resolution change. -1 means no limit.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MaxNumSpotShadowCacheUpdatesPerFrame
cvars.description: Maximum number of spot light shadow cache updates allowed per frame.Only affect updates caused by resolution change. -1 means no limit.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MaxResolution
cvars.description: Max square dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering shadow depths. Range 4 to hardware limit. Higher = better quality shadows but at a performance cost.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MaxSoftKernelSize
cvars.description: Mazimum size of the softening kernels in pixels.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MinPreShadowResolution
cvars.description: Minimum dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering preshadow depths

cvars.name: r.Shadow.MinResolution
cvars.description: Minimum dimensions (in texels) allowed for rendering shadow subject depths

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Nanite
cvars.description: Enables shadows from Nanite meshes.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.NaniteLODBias
cvars.description: LOD bias for nanite geometry in shadows. 0 = full detail. >0 = reduced detail.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.NaniteUpdateStreaming
cvars.description: Produce Nanite geometry streaming requests from shadow map rendering.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.NaniteUseHZB
cvars.description: Enables HZB for Nanite shadows.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.OcclusionCullCascadedShadowMaps
cvars.description: Whether to use occlusion culling on cascaded shadow maps. Disabled by default because rapid view changes reveal new regions too quickly for latent occlusion queries to work with.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PerObject
cvars.description: Whether to render per object shadows (character casting on world) 0: off 1: on (default)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceMin
cvars.description: Minimum cast distance for Per-Object shadows, i.e., CastDistDance = Max(r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceRadiusScale x object-radius, r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceMin). Default: UE_FLOAT_HUGE_DISTANCE / 32.0f

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectCastDistanceRadiusScale
cvars.description: PerObjectCastDistanceRadiusScale The scale factor multiplied with the radius of the object to calculate the maximum distance a per-object directional shadow can reach. This will only take effect after a certain (large) radius. Default is 8 times the object radius.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectDirectionalDepthBias
cvars.description: Constant depth bias used by per-object shadows from directional lights Lower values give better shadow contact, but increase self-shadowing artifacts

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectDirectionalSlopeDepthBias
cvars.description: Slope scale depth bias used by per-object shadows from directional lights Lower values give better shadow contact, but increase self-shadowing artifacts

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectSpotLightDepthBias
cvars.description: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for per-object projected shadows from spot lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PerObjectSpotLightSlopeDepthBias
cvars.description: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for per-object projected shadows from spot lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PointLightDepthBias
cvars.description: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from point lights. (0.03 avoids peter paning but has some shadow acne)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PointLightSlopeScaleDepthBias
cvars.description: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from point lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PreshadowExpand
cvars.description: How much bounds will be expanded when rendering a cached preshadow (0.15 = 15% larger)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PreShadowFadeResolution
cvars.description: Resolution in texels below which preshadows are faded out

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PreShadowResolutionFactor
cvars.description: Mulitplier for preshadow resolution

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Preshadows
cvars.description: Whether to allow preshadows (static world casting on character)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.PreshadowsForceLowestDetailLevel
cvars.description: When enabled, static meshes render their lowest detail level into preshadow depth maps. Disabled by default as it causes artifacts with poor quality LODs (tree billboard).

cvars.name: r.Shadow.RadiusThreshold
cvars.description: Cull shadow casters if they are too small, value is the minimal screen space bounding sphere radius

cvars.name: r.Shadow.RectLightDepthBias
cvars.description: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from rect lights. (0.03 avoids peter paning but has some shadow acne)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.RectLightReceiverBias
cvars.description: Receiver bias used by rect light. Value between 0 and 1.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.RectLightSlopeScaleDepthBias
cvars.description: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for shadows from rect lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.ResolutionScaleZeroDisablesSm
cvars.description: DEPRECATED: If 1 (default) then setting Shadow Resolution Scale to zero disables shadow maps for the light.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Scene.LightActiveFrameCount
cvars.description: Number of frames before a light that has been moving (updated or transform changed) goes to inactive state. This determines the number of frames that the MobilityFactor goes to zero over, and thus a higher number spreads invalidations out over a longer time.

cvars.name: r.shadow.ShadowMapsRenderEarly
cvars.description: If enabled, shadows will render earlier in the frame. This can help async compute scheduling on some platforms Note: This is not compatible with VSMs

cvars.name: r.Shadow.ShadowMaxSlopeScaleDepthBias
cvars.description: Max Slope depth bias used for shadows for all lights Higher values give better self-shadowing, but increase self-shadowing artifacts

cvars.name: r.Shadow.ShouldBeginDeferredCullingAfterShadowRendering
cvars.description: Temporary: If turned on, a new deferred scope of instance culling will be started directly after shadow depth rendering (iff instance occlusion culling is enabled) to work around a bug.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.SkipCullingNaniteMeshes
cvars.description: When enabled, CPU culling will ignore nanite meshes.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightDepthBias
cvars.description: Depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for whole-scene projected shadows from spot lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightReceiverBias
cvars.description: Receiver bias used by spotlights. Value between 0 and 1.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightSlopeDepthBias
cvars.description: Slope scale depth bias that is applied in the depth pass for whole-scene projected shadows from spot lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.SpotLightTransitionScale
cvars.description: Transition scale for spotlights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.StencilCulling
cvars.description: Whether to use stencil light culling during shadow projection (default) or only depth.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.StencilOptimization
cvars.description: Removes stencil clears between shadow projections by zeroing the stencil during testing

cvars.name: r.Shadow.TexelsPerPixel
cvars.description: The ratio of subject pixels to shadow texels for per-object shadows

cvars.name: r.Shadow.TexelsPerPixelPointlight
cvars.description: The ratio of subject pixels to shadow texels for point lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.TexelsPerPixelRectlight
cvars.description: The ratio of subject pixels to shadow texels for rect lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.TexelsPerPixelSpotlight
cvars.description: The ratio of subject pixels to shadow texels for spotlights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.TransitionScale
cvars.description: This controls the 'fade in' region between a caster and where its shadow shows up. Larger values make a smaller region which will have more self shadowing artifacts

cvars.name: r.Shadow.TranslucentPerObject.ProjectEnabled
cvars.description: Enable/Disable translucency shadows on a per-project basis. Turning off can significantly reduce the number of permutations if your project has many translucent materials.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.UnbuiltNumWholeSceneDynamicShadowCascades
cvars.description: DynamicShadowCascades to use when using CSM to preview unbuilt lighting from a directional light

cvars.name: r.Shadow.UnbuiltPreviewInGame
cvars.description: Whether to render unbuilt preview shadows in game. When enabled and lighting is not built, expensive preview shadows will be rendered in game. When disabled, lighting in game and editor won't match which can appear to be a bug.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.UnbuiltWholeSceneDynamicShadowRadius
cvars.description: WholeSceneDynamicShadowRadius to use when using CSM to preview unbuilt lighting from a directional light

cvars.name: r.Shadow.UseOctreeForCulling
cvars.description: Whether to use the primitive octree for shadow subject culling. The octree culls large groups of primitives at a time, but introduces cache misses walking the data structure.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.AccumulateStats
cvars.description: When enabled, VSM stats will be collected over multiple frames and written to a CSV file

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.AllocatePagePoolAsReservedResource
cvars.description: Allocate VSM page pool as a reserved/virtual texture, backed by N small physical memory allocations to reduce fragmentation.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache
cvars.description: Turn on to enable caching

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.AllocateViaLRU
cvars.description: Prioritizes keeping more recently requested cached physical pages when allocating for new requests.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.ClipmapPanning
cvars.description: Enable support for panning cached clipmap pages for directional lights, allowing re-use of cached data when the camera moves. Keep this enabled outside of debugging.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.DebugSkipDynamicPageInvalidation
cvars.description: Skip invalidation of cached pages when geometry moves for debugging purposes. This will create obvious visual artifacts when disabled.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.DeformableMeshesInvalidate
cvars.description: If enabled, Primitive Proxies that are marked as having deformable meshes (HasDeformableMesh() == true) cause invalidations regardless of whether their transforms are updated.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.DrawInvalidatingBounds
cvars.description: Turn on debug render cache invalidating instance bounds, heat mapped by number of pages invalidated. 1 = Draw all bounds. 2 = Draw those invalidating static cached pages only 3 = Draw those invalidating dynamic cached pages only

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.ForceInvalidateDirectional
cvars.description: Forces the clipmap to always invalidate, useful to emulate a moving sun to avoid misrepresenting cache performance.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.InvalidateUseHZB
cvars.description: When enabled, instances invalidations are tested against the HZB. Instances that are fully occluded will not cause page invalidations.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.MaxPageAgeSinceLastRequest
cvars.description: The maximum number of frames to allow cached pages that aren't requested in the current frame to live. 0=disabled.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.StaticSeparate
cvars.description: When enabled, caches static objects in separate pages from dynamic objects. This can improve performance in largely static scenes, but doubles the memory cost of the physical page pool.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Cache.StaticSeparate.FramesStaticThreshold
cvars.description: Number of frames without an invalidation before an object will transition to static caching.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.FirstCoarseLevel
cvars.description: First level of the clipmap to mark coarse pages for. Lower values allow higher resolution coarse pages near the camera but increase total page counts.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.FirstLevel
cvars.description: First level of the virtual clipmap. Lower values allow higher resolution shadows closer to the camera, but may increase page count.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.GreedyLevelSelection
cvars.description: When enabled, allows greedily sampling more detailed clipmap levels if they happen to be mapped. This can increase shadow quality from certain viewing angles, but makes the clipmap boundry less stable which can exacerbate visual artifacts at low shadow resolutions.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.LastCoarseLevel
cvars.description: Last level of the clipmap to mark coarse pages for. Higher values provide dense clipmap data for a longer radius but increase total page counts.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.LastLevel
cvars.description: Last level of the virtual clipmap. Indirectly determines radius the clipmap can cover. Each extra level doubles the maximum range, but may increase page count.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.MinCameraViewportWidth
cvars.description: If greater than zero, clamps the camera viewport dimensions used to adjust the clipmap resolution. This can be useful to avoid dynamic resolution indirectly dropping the shadow resolution far too low.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.UseConservativeCulling
cvars.description: Conservative culling removes the frustum-clipped culling volume for the non-nanite geometry for VSM rendering. This means a lot more geometry is submitted, and also marked as rendered. Useful to diagnose if there are culling artifacts in virtual shadow map clip maps due to errors in the tracking code.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.WPODisableDistance
cvars.description: When enabled, disables WPO animation in clipmap levels based on a primitive's WPO disable distance and r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.WPODisableDistance.LodBias setting.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.WPODisableDistance.LodBias
cvars.description: The number of clipmap levels further than the distance that an instance would be animated to allow shadow animation. Typically 2-4 works well but may need to be adjusted for very low light angles with significant WPO movement.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Clipmap.ZRangeScale
cvars.description: Scale of the clipmap level depth range relative to the radius. Affects z-near/z-far of the shadow map. Should generally be at least 10 or it will result in excessive cache invalidations. Values that are too large cause depth imprecisions and shadow flickering.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CoarsePagePixelThresholdDynamic
cvars.description: If a dynamic (non-nanite) instance has a smaller estimated pixel footprint than this value, it should not be drawn into a coarse page. Higher values cull away more instances.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CoarsePagePixelThresholdDynamicNanite
cvars.description: If a dynamic Nanite instance has a smaller estimated pixel footprint than this value, it should not be drawn into a coarse page. Higher values cull away more instances. This value is typically lower than the non-Nanite one because Nanite has lower overhead for drawing small objects.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CoarsePagePixelThresholdStatic
cvars.description: If a static (non-nanite) instance has a smaller estimated pixel footprint than this value, it should not be drawn into a coarse page. Higher values cull away more instances. This value is typically lower than the non-static one because the static pages have better caching.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.CullBackfacingPixels
cvars.description: When enabled does not generate shadow data for pixels that are backfacing to the light.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.DebugSkipMergePhysical
cvars.description: Skip the merging of the static VSM cache into the dynamic one. This will create obvious visual artifacts when disabled.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.DistantLightForceCacheFootprintFraction
cvars.description: Fraction of footprint size below which start force-caching lights that are invalidated (i.e., are moving or re-added) Larger values may improve performance but may also produce more visible artifacts The base footprint is based on the page size. 0.0 == Never force-cache (default), 1.0 == Always force-cache.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.DistantLightMode
cvars.description: Control whether distant light mode is enabled for local lights. 0 == Off, 1 == On (default), 2 == Force All. When on, lights with a pixel footprint below the threshold are marked as distant. Updates to distant lights are throttled (force-cached), they use simpler page-table logic and the memory cost is lower.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.DynamicRes.MaxPagePoolLoadFactor
cvars.description: If allocation exceeds this factor of total page pool capacity, shadow resolution will be biased downwards. 0 = disabled

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.DynamicRes.MaxResolutionLodBias
