cvars.description: As page allocation approaches the pool capacity, VSM resolution ramps down by biasing the LOD up, similar to 'ResolutionLodBiasDirectional'. This is the maximum LOD bias to clamp to for global dynamic shadow resolution reduction. 0 = disabled

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Enable
cvars.description: Enable Virtual Shadow Maps. Renders geometry into virtualized shadow depth maps for shadowing. Provides high - quality shadows for next - gen projects with simplified setup.High efficiency culling when used with Nanite.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ForceFullHZBUpdate
cvars.description: Forces full HZB update every frame rather than just dirty pages.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ForceOnlyVirtualShadowMaps
cvars.description: If enabled, disallow creation of conventional non-virtual shadow maps for any lights that get a virtual shadow map. This can improve performance and save memory, but any geometric primitives that cannot be rendered into the virtual shadow map will not cast shadows.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ForcePerLightShadowMaskClear
cvars.description: For debugging purposes. When enabled, the shadow mask texture is cleared before the projection pass writes to it. Projection pass writes all relevant pixels, so clearing should be unnecessary.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MarkCoarsePagesDirectional
cvars.description: Marks coarse pages in directional light virtual shadow maps so that low resolution data is available everywhere.Ability to disable is primarily for profiling and debugging.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MarkCoarsePagesLocal
cvars.description: Marks coarse pages in local light virtual shadow maps so that low resolution data is available everywhere.Ability to disable is primarily for profiling and debugging.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MarkPixelPages
cvars.description: Marks pages in virtual shadow maps based on depth buffer pixels. Ability to disable is primarily for profiling and debugging.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MarkPixelPagesMipModeLocal
cvars.description: When enabled, this uses a subset of mips to reduce instance duplication in VSMs. Will result in better performance but a harsher falloff on mip transitions. 0 - Disabled: Use all 8 mips 1 - Quality Mode: Use 4 higher res mips (16k, 4k, 1k, 256) 2 - Performance Mode: Use 4 lower res mips (8k, 2k, 512, 128)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MaxDistantUpdatePerFrame
cvars.description: Maximum number of distant lights to update each frame. Invalidated lights that were missed may be updated in a later frame (round-robin).

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.MaxPhysicalPages
cvars.description: Maximum number of physical pages in the pool. More space for pages means more memory usage, but allows for higher resolution shadows. Ideally this value is large enough to fit enough pages for all the lights in the scene, but not too large to waste memory. Enable 'ShowStats' to see how many pages are allocated in the pool right now. For more page pool control, see the 'ResolutionLodBias', 'DynamicRes.' and 'Cache.StaticSeparate' cvars.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Nanite.MaterialVisibility
cvars.description: Enable Nanite CPU-side visibility filtering of draw commands, depends on r.Nanite.MaterialVisibility being enabled.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.CulledInstanceAllocationFactor
cvars.description: Allocation size scale factor for the buffer used to store instances after culling. The total size accounts for the worst-case scenario in which all instances are emitted into every clip or mip level. This is far more than we'd expect in reasonable circumstances, so this scale factor is used to reduce memory pressure. The actual number cannot be known on the CPU as the culling emits an instance for each clip/mip level that is overlapped. Setting to 1.0 is fully conservative. Lowering this is likely to produce artifacts unless you're certain the buffer won't overflow.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.IncludeInCoarsePages
cvars.description: Include non-Nanite geometry in coarse pages.Rendering non-Nanite geometry into large coarse pages can be expensive; disabling this can be a significant performance win.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.LargeInstancePageAreaThreshold
cvars.description: How large area is considered a 'large' footprint, summed over all overlapped levels, if set to -1 uses the physical page pool size / 8. Used as a threshold when storing page area coverage stats for diagnostics.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.MaxCulledInstanceAllocationSize
cvars.description: Maximum number of instances that may be output from the culling pass into all VSM mip/clip levels. At 12 byte per instance reference this represents a 1.5GB clamp.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.NumPageAreaDiagSlots
cvars.description: Number of slots in diagnostics to report non-nanite instances with the largest page area coverage, < 0 uses the max number allowed, 0 disables.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.UseHZB
cvars.description: Cull Non-Nanite instances using HZB. Set to 0 to disable. Set to 1 to use HZB from previous frame. Can incorrectly cull in some cases due to outdated data. Set to 2 to use two-pass Nanite culling with HZB from the current frame.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNaniteVSM
cvars.description: Enable support for non-nanite Virtual Shadow Maps.Read-only and to be set in a config file (requires restart).

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.NormalBias
cvars.description: Receiver offset along surface normal for shadow lookup. Scaled by distance to camera.Higher values avoid artifacts on surfaces nearly parallel to the light, but also visibility offset shadows and increase the chance of hitting unmapped pages.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.OnePassProjection
cvars.description: Projects all local light virtual shadow maps in a single pass for better performance.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.OnePassProjection.MaxLightsPerPixel
cvars.description: Maximum lights per pixel that get full filtering when using one pass projection and clustered shading.Generally set to 8 (32bpp), 16 (64bpp) or 32 (128bpp). Lower values require less transient VRAM during the lighting pass.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.OnePassProjection.SkipScreenShadowMask
cvars.description: Allows skipping the screen space shadow mask entirely when only a virtual shadow map would write into it. Should generally be left enabled outside of debugging.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.PageDilationBorderSizeDirectional
cvars.description: If a screen pixel falls within this fraction of a page border for directional lights, the adacent page will also be mapped.Higher values can reduce page misses at screen edges or disocclusions, but increase total page counts.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.PageDilationBorderSizeLocal
cvars.description: If a screen pixel falls within this fraction of a page border for local lights, the adacent page will also be mapped.Higher values can reduce page misses at screen edges or disocclusions, but increase total page counts.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.PageMarkingPixelStrideX
cvars.description: During page marking, instead of testing every screen pixel, test every Nth pixel. Page marking from screen pixels is used to determine which VSM pages are seen from the camera and need to be rendered. Increasing this value reduces page-marking costs, but could introduce artifacts due to missing pages. With sufficiently low values, it is likely a neighbouring pixel will mark the required page anyway.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.PageMarkingPixelStrideY
cvars.description: Same as PageMarkingPixelStrideX, but on the vertical axis of the screen.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ResolutionLodBiasDirectional
cvars.description: Bias applied to LOD calculations for directional lights. -1.0 doubles resolution, 1.0 halves it and so on.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ResolutionLodBiasDirectionalMoving
cvars.description: Bias applied to LOD calculations for directional lights that are moving. -1.0 doubles resolution, 1.0 halves it and so on. The bias transitions smoothly back to ResolutionLodBiasDirectional as the light transitions to non-moving, see 'r.Shadow.Scene.LightActiveFrameCount'.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ResolutionLodBiasLocal
cvars.description: Bias applied to LOD calculations for local lights. -1.0 doubles resolution, 1.0 halves it and so on.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ResolutionLodBiasLocalMoving
cvars.description: Bias applied to LOD calculations for local lights that are moving. -1.0 doubles resolution, 1.0 halves it and so on. The bias transitions smoothly back to ResolutionLodBiasLocal as the light transitions to non-moving, see 'r.Shadow.Scene.LightActiveFrameCount'.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ScreenRayLength
cvars.description: Length of the screen space shadow trace away from receiver surface (smart shadow bias) before the VSM / SMRT lookup.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ShowClipmapStats
cvars.description: Set to the number of clipmap you want to show stats for (-1 == off)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ShowLightDrawEvents
cvars.description: Enable Virtual Shadow Maps per-light draw events - may affect performance especially when there are many small lights in the scene.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.ShowStats
cvars.description: Show VSM statistics.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.AdaptiveRayCount
cvars.description: Shoot fewer rays in fully shadowed and unshadowed regions. Currently only supported with OnePassProjection.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.ExtrapolateMaxSlopeDirectional
cvars.description: Maximum depth slope when extrapolating behind occluders for directional lights. Higher values allow softer penumbra edges but can introduce light leaks behind second occluders. Setting to 0 will disable slope extrapolation slightly improving projection performance, at the cost of reduced penumbra quality.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.ExtrapolateMaxSlopeLocal
cvars.description: Maximum depth slope when extrapolating behind occluders for local lights. Higher values allow softer penumbra edges but can introduce light leaks behind second occluders. Setting to 0 will disable slope extrapolation slightly improving projection performance, at the cost of reduced penumbra quality.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.MaxRayAngleFromLight
cvars.description: Max angle (in radians) a ray is allowed to span from the light's perspective for local lights.Smaller angles limit the screen space size of shadow penumbra. Larger angles lead to more noise.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.MaxSlopeBiasLocal
cvars.description: Maximum depth slope. Low values produce artifacts if shadow resolution is insufficient. High values can worsen light leaks near occluders and sparkly pixels in shadowed areas.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.RayCountDirectional
cvars.description: Ray count for shadow map tracing of directional lights. 0 = disabled.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.RayCountLocal
cvars.description: Ray count for shadow map tracing of local lights. 0 = disabled.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.RayLengthScaleDirectional
cvars.description: Length of ray to shoot for directional lights, scaled by distance to camera.Shorter rays limit the screen space size of shadow penumbra. Longer rays require more samples to avoid shadows disconnecting from contact points.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.SamplesPerRayDirectional
cvars.description: Shadow map samples per ray for directional lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.SamplesPerRayLocal
cvars.description: Shadow map samples per ray for local lights

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.TexelDitherScaleDirectional
cvars.description: Applies a dither to the shadow map ray casts for directional lights to help hide aliasing due to insufficient shadow resolution. Setting this too high can cause shadows light leaks near occluders.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SMRT.TexelDitherScaleLocal
cvars.description: Applies a dither to the shadow map ray casts for local lights to help hide aliasing due to insufficient shadow resolution. Setting this too high can cause shadows light leaks near occluders.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.SubsurfaceShadowMinSourceAngle
cvars.description: Minimum source angle (in degrees) used for shadow & transmittance of sub-surface materials with directional lights. To emulate light diffusion with sub-surface materials, VSM can increase the light source radius depending on the material opacity. The higher this value, the more diffuse the shadowing with these materials will appear.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.TranslucentQuality
cvars.description: Quality of shadow for lit translucent surfaces. This will be applied on all translucent surfaces, and has high-performance impact. Set to 1 to enable the high-quality mode.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.UseFarShadowCulling
cvars.description: Switch between implementing the far shadow culling logic for VSMs.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.UseHZB
cvars.description: Enables HZB for (Nanite) Virtual Shadow Maps - Non-Nanite unfortunately has a separate flag with different semantics: r.Shadow.Virtual.NonNanite.UseHZB. 0 - No HZB occlusion culling 1 - Approximate Single-pass HZB occlusion culling (using previous frame HZB) 2 - Two-pass occlusion culling (default).

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Visualize
cvars.description: When the viewport view-mode is set to 'Virtual Shadow Map Visualization', this command specifies which of the various channels to display. Values entered other than the allowed values shown below will be ignored. mask mip vpage cache naniteoverdraw raycount dirty invalid merged debug clipmapvirtual

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Visualize.Advanced
cvars.description: Enable to show advanced VSM debug modes in the visualization UI menu.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Visualize.Layout
cvars.description: Overlay layout when virtual shadow map visualization is enabled: 0: Full screen 1: Thumbnail 2: Split screen

cvars.name: r.Shadow.Virtual.Visualize.LightName
cvars.description: Sets the name of a specific light to visualize (for developer use in non-shipping builds)

cvars.name: r.Shadow.WholeSceneShadowCacheMb
cvars.description: Amount of memory that can be spent caching whole scene shadows. ShadowMap allocations in a single frame can cause this to be exceeded.

cvars.name: r.Shadow.WholeSceneShadowUnbuiltInteractionThreshold
cvars.description: How many unbuilt light-primitive interactions there can be for a light before the light switches to whole scene shadows

cvars.name: r.ShadowQuality
cvars.description: Defines the shadow method which allows to adjust for quality or performance. 0:off, 1:low(unfiltered), 2:low .. 5:max (default)

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMesh.KeepMobileMinLODSettingOnDesktop
cvars.description: If non-zero, mobile setting for MinLOD will be stored in the cooked data for desktop platforms

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMesh.LODMaterialReference
cvars.description: Whether a material needs to be referenced by at least one unstripped mesh LOD to be considered as used.

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMesh.MinLodQualityLevel
cvars.description: The quality level for the Min stripping LOD.

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMesh.StripMinLodDataDuringCooking
cvars.description: If set will strip skeletal mesh LODs under the minimum renderable LOD for the target platform during cooking.

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMeshClothBlend.Enabled
cvars.description: Enable the use of the cloth blend weight value set by the skeletal mesh component. When disabled all cloth blend weight will become 0.

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMeshLODBias
cvars.description: LOD bias for skeletal meshes (does not affect animation editor viewports).

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMeshLODRadiusScale
cvars.description: Scale factor for the screen radius used in computing discrete LOD for skeletal meshes. (0.25-1)

cvars.name: r.SkeletalMeshReductionModule
cvars.description: Name of what skeletal mesh reduction module to choose. If blank it chooses any that exist.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.Allow
cvars.description: Whether or not to allow the GPU skin Cache system to be enabled.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.AllowDupedVertsForRecomputeTangents
cvars.description: 0: off (default) 1: Forces that vertices at the same position will be treated differently and has the potential to cause seams when verts are split.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.Capture
cvars.description: Trigger a render capture for the next skin cache dispatches.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.CompileShaders
cvars.description: Whether or not to compile the GPU compute skinning cache shaders. This will compile the shaders for skinning on a compute job and not skin on the vertex shader. GPUSkinVertexFactory.usf needs to be touched to cause a recompile if this changes. 0 is off(default), 1 is on

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.Debug
cvars.description: A scaling constant passed to the SkinCache shader, useful for debugging

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.DefaultBehavior
cvars.description: Default behavior if all skeletal meshes are included/excluded from the skin cache. If Support Ray Tracing is enabled on a mesh, will force inclusive behavior on that mesh. Exclusive ( 0): All skeletal meshes are excluded from the skin cache. Each must opt in individually. Inclusive ( 1): All skeletal meshes are included into the skin cache. Each must opt out individually. (default)

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.MaxDispatchesPerCmdList
cvars.description: Maximum number of compute shader dispatches which are batched together into a single command list to fix potential TDRs.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.MaxRayTracingPrimitivesPerCmdList
cvars.description: Maximum amount of primitives which are batched together into a single command list to fix potential TDRs.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.Mode
cvars.description: Whether or not to use the GPU compute skinning cache. This will perform skinning on a compute job and not skin on the vertex shader. Requires r.SkinCache.CompileShaders=1 and r.SkinCache.Allow=1 0: off 1: on(default)

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.NumTangentIntermediateBuffers
cvars.description: How many intermediate buffers to use for intermediate results while doing Recompute Tangents; more may allow the GPU to overlap compute jobs.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.PrintMemorySummary
cvars.description: Print break down of memory usage. 0: off (default), 1: print when out of memory, 2: print every frame

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.RayTracingUseTransientForScratch
cvars.description: Use Transient memory for BLAS scratch allocation to reduce memory footprint and allocation overhead.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.RecomputeTangents
cvars.description: This option enables recomputing the vertex tangents on the GPU. Can be changed at runtime, requires both r.SkinCache.CompileShaders=1, r.SkinCache.Mode=1 and r.SkinCache.Allow=1 0: off 1: on, forces all skinned object to Recompute Tangents 2: on, only recompute tangents on skinned objects who ticked the Recompute Tangents checkbox(default)

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.RecomputeTangentsParallelDispatch
cvars.description: This option enables parallel dispatches for recompute tangents. 0: off (default), triangle pass is interleaved with vertex pass, requires resource barriers in between. 1: on, batch triangle passes together, resource barrier, followed by vertex passes together, cost more memory.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.SceneMemoryLimitInMB
cvars.description: Maximum memory allowed to be allocated per World/Scene in Megs

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.SkipCompilingGPUSkinVF
cvars.description: Reduce GPU Skin Vertex Factory shader permutations. Cannot be disabled while the skin cache is turned off. False ( 0): Compile all GPU Skin Vertex factory variants. True ( 1): Don't compile all GPU Skin Vertex factory variants.

cvars.name: r.SkinCache.Visualize
cvars.description: Specifies which visualization mode to display: 'Overview' 'Memory'

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere
cvars.description: SkyAtmosphere components are rendered when this is not 0, otherwise ignored.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspective.DepthTest
cvars.description: When enabled, a depth test will be used to not write pixel closer to the camera than StartDepth, effectively improving performance.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.Depth
cvars.description: The length of the LUT in kilometers (default = 96km to get nice cloud/atmosphere interactions in the distance for default sky). Further than this distance, the last slice is used.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.DepthResolution
cvars.description: The number of depth slice to use for the aerial perspective volume texture.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.FastApplyOnOpaque
cvars.description: When enabled, the low resolution camera frustum/froxel volume containing atmospheric fog , usually used for fog on translucent surface, is used to render fog on opaque. It is faster but can result in visual artefacts if there are some high frequency details such as earth shadow or scattering lob.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.AerialPerspectiveLUT.SampleCountMaxPerSlice
cvars.description: The sample count used per slice to evaluate aerial perspective. The effective sample count is usually lower and depends on SampleCountScale on the component as well as .ini files. scattering and transmittance in camera frustum space froxel.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.DistanceToSampleCountMax
cvars.description: The distance in kilometer after which SampleCountMax samples will be used to ray march the atmosphere.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.DistantSkyLightLUT
cvars.description: Enable the generation the sky ambient lighting value.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.DistantSkyLightLUT.Altitude
cvars.description: The altitude at which the sky samples are taken to integrate the sky lighting. Default to 6km, typicaly cirrus clouds altitude.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.EditorNotifications
cvars.description: Enable the rendering of in editor notification to warn the user about missing sky dome pixels on screen. It is better to keep it enabled and will be removed when shipping.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT
cvars.description: When enabled, a look up texture is used to render the sky. It is faster but can result in visual artefacts if there are some high frequency details in the sky such as earth shadow or scattering lob.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT.DistanceToSampleCountMax
cvars.description: Fast sky distance in kilometer after which at which SampleCountMax samples will be used to ray march the atmosphere.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT.SampleCountMax
cvars.description: Fast sky maximum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance. The maximum sample count used to compute FastSkyLUT scattering. The effective sample count is usually lower and depends on distance and SampleCountScale on the component, as well as .ini files. The minimal value will be clamped to r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT.SampleCountMin + 1.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.FastSkyLUT.SampleCountMin
cvars.description: Fast sky minimum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance. The minimal value will be clamped to 1.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.LUT32
cvars.description: Use full 32bit per-channel precision for all sky LUTs.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.MultiScatteringLUT.HighQuality
cvars.description: The when enabled, 64 samples are used instead of 2, resulting in a more accurate multi scattering approximation (but also more expenssive).

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.MultiScatteringLUT.SampleCount
cvars.description: The sample count used to evaluate multi-scattering.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.SampleCountMax
cvars.description: The maximum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance The effective sample count is usually lower and depends on distance and SampleCountScale on the component, as well as .ini files. The minimal value will be clamped to r.SkyAtmosphere.SampleCountMin + 1.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.SampleCountMin
cvars.description: The minimum sample count used to compute sky/atmosphere scattering and transmittance. The minimal value will be clamped to 1.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.SampleLightShadowmap
cvars.description: Enable the sampling of atmospheric lights shadow map in order to produce volumetric shadows.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.TransmittanceLUT
cvars.description: Enable the generation of the sky transmittance.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.TransmittanceLUT.SampleCount
cvars.description: The sample count used to evaluate transmittance.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphere.TransmittanceLUT.UseSmallFormat
cvars.description: If true, the transmittance LUT will use a small R8BG8B8A8 format to store data at lower quality.

cvars.name: r.SkyAtmosphereASyncCompute
cvars.description: SkyAtmosphere on async compute (default: false). When running on the async pipe, SkyAtmosphere lut generation will overlap with the occlusion pass.

cvars.name: r.SkyLight.CubemapMaxResolution
cvars.description: Force max resolution of skylight cubemap (default to -1: takes default property value of USkyLightComponent::CubeMapResolution)

cvars.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture
cvars.description: Make sure the sky light real time capture is not run on platform where it is considered out of budget. Cannot be changed at runtime.

cvars.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.DepthBuffer
cvars.description: When enabled, the real-time sky light capture will have a depth buffer, this is for multiple meshes to be cover each other correctly. The height fog will also be applied according to the depth buffer.

cvars.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.ShadowFromOpaque
cvars.description: Opaque meshes cast shadow from directional lights onto sky and clouds when enabled.

cvars.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.TimeSlice
cvars.description: When enabled, the real-time sky light capture and convolutions will by distributed over several frames to lower the per-frame cost.

cvars.name: r.SkyLight.RealTimeReflectionCapture.TimeSlice.SkyCloudCubeFacePerFrame
cvars.description: When enabled, the real-time sky light capture, when time sliced, will not render cloud in all cube face in a single frame; but one face per frame. That is to distribute the cloud tracing cost even more, but will add latency and potentially can result in lighting discrepancy between faces if the sun is moving fast. Value in [1,6].

cvars.name: r.SkylightCapture.LODDistanceScale
cvars.description: LODDistanceScale for the Sky Light Capture. Default is 1Negative values will be clamped to 1

cvars.name: r.SkyLightingQuality
cvars.description: Defines the sky lighting quality which allows to adjust for performance. <=0: off (fastest) 1: on

cvars.name: r.SkylightIntensityMultiplier
cvars.description: Intensity scale on Stationary and Movable skylights. This is useful to control overall lighting contrast in dynamically lit games with scalability levels which disable Ambient Occlusion. For example, if medium quality disables SSAO and DFAO, reduce skylight intensity.

cvars.name: r.SkylightUpdateEveryFrame
cvars.description: Whether to update all skylights every frame. Useful for debugging.

cvars.name: r.SkySpecularOcclusionStrength
cvars.description: Strength of skylight specular occlusion from DFAO (default is 1.0)

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.RemoteDDCBehavior
cvars.description: Controls how SVTs use remote DDC. 0: The bLocalDDCOnly property controls per-SVT caching behavior, 1: Force local DDC only usage for all SVTs, 2: Force local + remote DDC usage for all SVTs

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.AsyncCompute
cvars.description: Schedule GPU work in async compute queue.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.AsyncThread
cvars.description: Perform most of the SVT streaming on an asynchronous worker thread instead of the rendering thread.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.BandwidthLimit
cvars.description: Bandwidth limit for SVT streaming in MiB/s. When requests exceed this limit, the system will stream at lower mip levels instead.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.DDCChunkSize
cvars.description: Size of DDC chunks the streaming data is split into (in MiB). A smaller size leads to more requests but can improve streaming performance. Default: 2 MiB

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.EmptyPhysicalTileTextures
cvars.description: Streams out all streamable tiles of all physical tile textures.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.ForceBlockingRequests
cvars.description: If enabled, all SVT streaming requests will block on completion, guaranteeing that requested mip levels are available in the same frame they have been requested in (if there is enough memory available to stream them in).

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.ForceEstimateFrameRate
cvars.description: Forces the streaming system to always estimate the SVT playback frame rate instead of using the explicit frame rate that is passed in with the streaming requests. Intended for debugging.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.InstanceCleanupThreshold
cvars.description: Number of SVT streaming system updates to wait until unused streaming instances are cleaned up. A streaming instance is an internal book keeping object to track playback of a SVT asset in a given context. Default: 5

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.InstanceWindowSize
cvars.description: Window size in SVT frames around which requests to a SVT are considered to belong to the same instance. A streaming instance is an internal book keeping object to track playback of a SVT asset in a given context. Default: 5.0

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.LogVerbosity
cvars.description: 0: no logging, 1: basic logging, 2: additional logging (might spam the log) 3: log everything (will spam the log)

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.MaxPendingRequests
cvars.description: Maximum number of IO requests that can be pending for installation.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.PrintMemoryStats
cvars.description: Prints memory sizes of all frames of all SVTs registered with the streaming system.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.RequestMipBias
cvars.description: Bias to apply the calculated mip level to stream at. This is used to account for the mip estimation based on projected screen space size being very conservative. The default value of 1.0 was found empirically to roughly result in a 1:1 voxel to pixel ratio.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.RequestSize
cvars.description: IO request size in KiB. The SVT streaming manager will attempt to create IO requests of roughly this size. Default: -1 (unlimited)

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.RequestSizeGranularity
cvars.description: Rounds up calculated streaming request sizes to a multiple of this value (in bytes). This avoids performance issues where the system might issue multiple very small requests.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.ReservedResourcesMemoryLimit
cvars.description: Memory limit in MiB on the maximum size of the streaming pool textures when using reserved resources. Without this limit it is theoretically possible to allocate enormous amounts of memory. Set to -1 to disable the limit. Default: -1

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.ShowDebugInfo
cvars.description: Prints debug info about the streaming SVT instances to the screen.

cvars.name: r.SparseVolumeTexture.Streaming.UseReservedResources
cvars.description: Allocate the SVT tile data texture (streaming pool) as a reserved/virtual texture, backed by N small physical memory allocations to reduce fragmentation. This lifts the 2GB resource size limit and also allows for better GPU memory management when allocating the texture.

cvars.name: r.SplineMesh.NoRecreateProxy
cvars.description: Optimization. If true, spline mesh proxies will not be recreated every time they are changed. They are simply updated.

cvars.name: r.SplineMesh.RenderNanite
cvars.description: When true, allows spline meshes to render as Nanite when enabled on the mesh (otherwise uses fallback mesh).

cvars.name: r.SplineMesh.SceneTextures
cvars.description: Whether to cache all spline mesh splines in the scene to textures (performance optimization).

cvars.name: r.SplineMesh.SceneTextures.CaptureNextUpdate
cvars.description: Set to 1 to perform a capture of the next spline mesh texture update. Set to > 1 to capture the next N updates.

cvars.name: r.SplineMesh.SceneTextures.ForceUpdate
cvars.description: When true, will force an update of the whole spline mesh scene texture each frame (for debugging).

cvars.name: r.SplineMesh.SceneTextures.InstanceIDUploadCopy
cvars.description: When true, will make a copy of the registered instance IDs on buffer upload.

cvars.name: r.SSGI.LeakFreeReprojection
cvars.description: Whether use a more expensive but leak free reprojection of previous frame's scene color.

cvars.name: r.SSGI.Quality
cvars.description: Quality setting to control number of ray shot with SSGI, between 1 and 4 (defaults to 4).

cvars.name: r.SSGI.RejectUncertainRays
cvars.description: Rejects the screen space ray if it was uncertain due to going behind screen geometry.

cvars.name: r.SSGI.SkyDistance
cvars.description: Distance of the sky in KM.

cvars.name: r.SSGI.TerminateCertainRay
cvars.description: Optimisations that if the screen space ray is certain and didn't find any geometry, don't fallback on otehr tracing technic.

cvars.name: r.SSProfiles.Transmission.UseLegacy
cvars.description: 0. Use more physically correct transmission profile. 1. Use legacy transmission profile (default).

cvars.name: r.SSProfilesPreIntegratedTextureForceUpdate
cvars.description: 0: Only update the preintegrated texture as needed. 1: Force to update the preintegrated texture for debugging.

cvars.name: r.SSProfilesPreIntegratedTextureResolution
cvars.description: The resolution of the subsurface profile preintegrated texture.

cvars.name: r.SSProfilesSamplingChannelSelection
cvars.description: 0. Select the sampling channel based on max DMFP. 1. based on max MFP.

cvars.name: r.SSR.ExperimentalDenoiser
cvars.description: Replace SSR's TAA pass with denoiser.

cvars.name: r.SSR.HalfResSceneColor
cvars.description: Use half res scene color as input for SSR. Improves performance without much of a visual quality loss.

cvars.name: r.SSR.MaxRoughness
cvars.description: Allows to override the post process setting ScreenSpaceReflectionMaxRoughness. It defines until what roughness we fade the screen space reflections, 0.8 works well, smaller can run faster. (Useful for testing, no scalability or project setting) 0..1: use specified max roughness (overrride PostprocessVolume setting) -1: no override (default)

cvars.name: r.SSR.Quality
cvars.description: Whether to use screen space reflections and at what quality setting. (limits the setting in the post process settings which has a different scale) (costs performance, adds more visual realism but the technique has limits) 0: off (default) 1: low (no glossy) 2: medium (no glossy) 3: high (glossy/using roughness, few samples) 4: very high (likely too slow for real-time)

cvars.name: r.SSR.Stencil
cvars.description: Defines if we use the stencil prepass for the screen space reflection 0 is off (default), 1 is on

cvars.name: r.SSR.Temporal
cvars.description: Defines if we use the temporal smoothing for the screen space reflection 0 is off (for debugging), 1 is on (default)

cvars.name: r.SSR.TiledComposite
cvars.description: Enable tiled optimization of the screen space reflection.

cvars.name: r.SSR.TiledComposite.MinSpecular
cvars.description: Ignore pixels with very small specular contribution in case max roughness cannot filter them out

cvars.name: r.SSR.TiledComposite.OverrideMaxRoughness
cvars.description: Ignore pixels with roughness larger than this value.<0: use derived value from ScreenSpaceReflectionMaxRoughness of FinalPostProcessSettings.

cvars.name: r.SSR.TiledComposite.TwoSidedFoliage
cvars.description: 0: diable SSR for foliage if tiling is enabled.

cvars.name: r.SSR.TiledComposite.Visualize
cvars.description: 1: Visualize the tiling region.

cvars.name: r.SSS.Burley.AlwaysUpdateParametersFromSeparable
cvars.description: 0: Will not update parameters when the program loads. (default)1: Always update from the separable when the program loads. (Correct only when Subsurface color is 1).

cvars.name: r.SSS.Burley.BilateralFilterKernelFunctionType
cvars.description: 0: Depth Only. It is more performant (x2 faster for close view).1: Depth and normal. It leads to better quality in regions like eyelids. (default)

cvars.name: r.SSS.Burley.EnableProfileIdCache
cvars.description: 0: Disable profile id cache using in the sampling pass. 1: Consumes 1 byte per pixel more memory to make Burley pass much faster. (default)

cvars.name: r.SSS.Burley.MinGenerateMipsTileCount
cvars.description: 4000. (default) The minimal number of tiles to trigger subsurface radiance mip generation. Set to zero to always generate mips (Experimental value)

cvars.name: r.SSS.Burley.NumSamplesOverride
cvars.description: When zero, Burley SSS adaptively determines the number of samples. When non-zero, this value overrides the sample count.

cvars.name: r.SSS.Burley.Quality
cvars.description: 0: Fallback mode. Burley falls back to run scattering in Separable with transmission in Burley for better performance. Separable parameters are automatically fitted.1: Automatic. The subsurface will only switch to separable in half resolution. (default)

cvars.name: r.SSS.Checkerboard
cvars.description: Enables or disables checkerboard rendering for subsurface profile rendering. This is necessary if SceneColor does not include a floating point alpha channel (e.g 32-bit formats) 0: Disabled (high quality). If the rendertarget format does not have an alpha channel (e.g., PF_FloatR11G11B10), it leads to over-washed SSS. 1: Enabled (low quality). Surface lighting will be at reduced resolution. 2: Automatic. Non-checkerboard lighting will be applied if we have a suitable rendertarget format

cvars.name: r.SSS.Checkerboard.NeighborSSSValidation
cvars.description: Enable or disable checkerboard neighbor subsurface scattering validation. This validation can remove border light leakage into subsurface scattering, creating a sharpe border with correct color 0: Disabled (default) 1: Enabled. Add 1 subsurface profile id query/pixel (low quality), 4 id query/pixel (high quality) at recombine pass

cvars.name: r.SSS.Filter
cvars.description: Defines the filter method for Screenspace Subsurface Scattering feature. 0: point filter (useful for testing, could be cleaner) 1: bilinear filter

cvars.name: r.SSS.HalfRes
cvars.description: 0: full quality (Combined Burley and Separable pass. Separable is not optimized, as reference) 1: parts of the algorithm runs in half resolution which is lower quality but faster (default, Separable only)

cvars.name: r.SSS.Quality
cvars.description: Defines the quality of the recombine pass when using the SubsurfaceScatteringProfile shading model 0: low (faster, default) 1: high (sharper details but slower) -1: auto, 1 if TemporalAA is disabled (without TemporalAA the quality is more noticable)

cvars.name: r.SSS.SampleSet
cvars.description: Defines how many samples we use for Separable Screenspace Subsurface Scattering feature. 0: lowest quality (62+1) 1: medium quality (92+1) 2: high quality (13*2+1) (default)

cvars.name: r.SSS.Scale
cvars.description: Affects the Screen space Separable subsurface scattering pass (use shadingmodel SubsurfaceProfile, get near to the object as the default) is human skin which only scatters about 1.2cm) 0: off (if there is no object on the screen using this pass it should automatically disable the post process pass) <1: scale scatter radius down (for testing) 1: use given radius form the Subsurface scattering asset (default) >1: scale scatter radius up (for testing)

cvars.name: r.SSS.SubSurfaceColorAsTansmittanceAtDistance
cvars.description: Normalized distance (0..1) at which the surface color is interpreted as transmittance color to compute extinction coefficients.

cvars.name: r.StaticMesh.DisableThreadedBuild
cvars.description: Activate to force static mesh building from a single thread.

cvars.name: r.StaticMesh.EnableSaveGeneratedLODsInPackage
cvars.description: Enables saving generated LODs in the Package. 0 - Do not save (and hide this menu option) [default]. 1 - Enable this option and save the LODs in the Package.

cvars.name: r.StaticMesh.KeepMobileMinLODSettingOnDesktop
cvars.description: If non-zero, mobile setting for MinLOD will be stored in the cooked data for desktop platforms

cvars.name: r.StaticMesh.MinLodQualityLevel
cvars.description: The quality level for the Min stripping LOD.

cvars.name: r.StaticMesh.StripDistanceFieldDataDuringLoad
cvars.description: If non-zero, data for distance fields will be discarded on load. TODO: change to discard during cook!.

cvars.name: r.StaticMesh.StripMinLodDataDuringCooking
cvars.description: If non-zero, data for Static Mesh LOD levels below MinLOD will be discarded at cook time

cvars.name: r.StaticMesh.UpdateMeshLODGroupSettingsAtLoad
cvars.description: If set, LODGroup settings for static meshes will be applied at load time.

cvars.name: r.StaticMeshLODDistanceScale
cvars.description: Scale factor for the distance used in computing discrete LOD for static meshes. (defaults to 1) (higher values make LODs transition earlier, e.g., 2 is twice as fast / half the distance)

cvars.name: r.Streaming.AllowFastForceResident
cvars.description: Whether it is allowed to load in missing mips for fast-force-resident assets ASAP. Useful to accelerate force-resident process but risks disturbing streaming metric calculation. Fast-force-resident mips can't be sacrificed even when overbudget so use with caution.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.AllowParallelStreamingRenderAssets
cvars.description: Whether it is allowed to do UpdateStreamingRenderAssets with a ParallelFor to use more cores.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.AllowUpdateResourceSize
cvars.description: AllowUpdateResourceSize

cvars.name: r.Streaming.AmortizeCPUToGPUCopy
cvars.description: If set and r.Streaming.MaxNumTexturesToStreamPerFrame > 0, limit the number of 2D textures streamed from CPU memory to GPU memory each frame

cvars.name: r.Streaming.Boost
cvars.description: =1.0: normal <1.0: decrease wanted mip levels >1.0: increase wanted mip levels

cvars.name: r.Streaming.CheckBuildStatus
cvars.description: If non-zero, the engine will check whether texture streaming needs rebuild.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.DefaultNoRefLODBias
cvars.description: The default LOD bias for no-ref meshes

cvars.name: r.Streaming.DeferredRemoveDynamicInstances
cvars.description: Whether to defer removing components from the dynamic instance manager to avoid stalling the game thread on component deregistration.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.DefragDynamicBounds
cvars.description: If non-zero, unused dynamic bounds will be removed from the update loop

cvars.name: r.Streaming.DropMips
cvars.description: 0: Drop No Mips 1: Drop Cached Mips 2: Drop Cached and Hidden Mips 3: Drop cached mips and non-inlined LODs of no-ref meshes

cvars.name: r.Streaming.EnableAutoDetectNoStreamableTextures
cvars.description: Enables auto-detection at cook time of primitive components with no streamable textures. Can also be turned-off at runtime to skip optimisation.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.FlushDeferredMipLevelChangeCallbacksBeforeGC
cvars.description: Whether to flush deferred mip level change callbacks before GC.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.FlushTimeOut
cvars.description: Time before we timeout when flushing streaming (default=3)

cvars.name: r.Streaming.FramesForFullUpdate
cvars.description: Texture streaming is time sliced per frame. This values gives the number of frames to visit all textures.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.FullyLoadMeshes
cvars.description: If non-zero, stream in all mesh LODs. This allows semi-disabling mesh LOD streaming without recook.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.FullyLoadUsedTextures
cvars.description: If non-zero, all used texture will be fully streamed in as fast as possible

cvars.name: r.Streaming.HiddenPrimitiveScale
cvars.description: Define the resolution scale to apply when not in range. .5: drop one mip 1: ignore visiblity

cvars.name: r.Streaming.HLODStrategy
cvars.description: Define the HLOD streaming strategy. 0: stream 1: stream only mip 0 2: disable streaming

cvars.name: r.Streaming.LimitPoolSizeToVRAM
cvars.description: If non-zero, texture pool size with be limited to how much GPU mem is available.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.LowResHandlingMode
cvars.description: How to handle assets with too many missing MIPs or LODs. 0 (default): do nothing, 1: load before regular streaming requests, 2: load before async loading precache requests.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MaxEffectiveScreenSize
cvars.description: 0: Use current actual vertical screen size > 0: Clamp wanted mip size calculation to this value for the vertical screen size component.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MaxHiddenPrimitiveViewBoost
cvars.description: Maximum view boost that can affect hidden primitive. This prevents temporary small FOV from streaming all textures to their highest mips.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MaxNumTexturesToStreamPerFrame
cvars.description: Maximum number of 2D textures allowed to stream from CPU memory to GPU memory each frame. <= 0 means no limit. This has no effect if r.Streaming.AmortizeCPUToGPUCopy is not set

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MaxReferenceChecksBeforeStreamOut
cvars.description: Number of times the engine wait for references to be released before forcing streamout. (default=2)

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MaxTempMemoryAllowed
cvars.description: Maximum temporary memory used when streaming in or out texture mips. This memory contains mips used for the new updated texture. The value must be high enough to not be a limiting streaming speed factor.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MaxTextureUVDensity
cvars.description: If non-zero, the max UV density a static entry can have. Used to improve level culling from MinLevelTextureScreenSize. Component with bigger entries become handled as dynamic component.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MinBoost
cvars.description: Minimum clamp for r.Streaming.Boost

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MinLevelRenderAssetScreenSize
cvars.description: If non-zero, levels only get handled if any of their referenced texture could be required of this size. Using conservative metrics on the level data.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MinMipForSplitRequest
cvars.description: If non-zero, the minimum hidden mip for which load requests will first load the visible mip

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MipBias
cvars.description: 0..x reduce texture quality for streaming by a floating point number. 0: use full resolution (default) 1: drop one mip 2: drop two mips

cvars.name: r.Streaming.MipCalculationEnablePerLevelList
cvars.description: If non-zero, Mip size computation for streamed texture will use levels referenced with it (instead of iterating thorugh every levels).

cvars.name: r.Streaming.NoRefLODBiasQualityLevel
cvars.description: The quality level for the no-ref mesh streaming LOD bias

cvars.name: r.Streaming.NumStaticComponentsProcessedPerFrame
cvars.description: If non-zero, the engine will incrementaly inserting levels by processing this amount of components per frame before they become visible

cvars.name: r.Streaming.OverlapAssetAndLevelTicks
cvars.description: Ticks render asset streaming info on a high priority task thread while ticking levels on GT

cvars.name: r.Streaming.ParallelRenderAssetsNumWorkgroups
cvars.description: How many workgroups we want to use for ParellelRenderAsset updates. Splits the work up a bit more so we don't get as many waits. Though adds overhead to GameThread if too high.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.PerTextureBiasViewBoostThreshold
cvars.description: Maximum view boost at which per texture bias will be increased. This prevents temporary small FOV from downgrading permanentely texture quality.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.PoolSize
cvars.description: -1: Default texture pool size, otherwise the size in MB

cvars.name: r.Streaming.PoolSize.VRAMPercentageClamp
cvars.description: When using PoolSizeVRAMPercentage, a maximum amout of memory to reserve in MB. This avoids reserving too much space for systems with a lot of VRAM. (default=1024)

cvars.name: r.Streaming.PoolSizeForMeshes
cvars.description: < 0: Mesh and texture share the same pool, otherwise the size of pool dedicated to meshes.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.PrioritizeMeshLODRetention
cvars.description: Whether to prioritize retaining mesh LODs

cvars.name: r.Streaming.ProcessAddedRenderAssetsAfterAsyncWork
cvars.description: Whether to call ProcessAddedRenderAssets in subsqequent UpdateResourceStreaming stages after Async work has completed.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.StressTest
cvars.description: Set to non zero to stress test the streaming update. Negative values also slow down the IO.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.StressTest.ExtaIOLatency
cvars.description: An extra latency in milliseconds for each stream-in requests when doing the stress test.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.StressTest.ExtraAsyncLatency
cvars.description: An extra latency in milliseconds for each async task when doing the stress test.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.StressTest.FramesForFullUpdate
cvars.description: Num frames to update texture states when doing the stress tests.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.SyncStatesWhenBlocking
cvars.description: If true, SyncStates will be called to fully update async states before flushing outstanding streaming requests. Used by Movie Render Queue to ensure all streaming requests are handled each frame to avoid pop-in.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseAllMips
cvars.description: If non-zero, all available mips will be used

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseAsyncRequestsForDDC
cvars.description: Whether to use async DDC requests in order to react quickly to cancel and suspend rendering requests (default=0)

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseBackgroundThreadPool
cvars.description: If true, use the background thread pool for mip calculations.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseFixedPoolSize
cvars.description: If non-zero, do not allow the pool size to change at run time.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseGenericStreamingPath
cvars.description: Control when to use the mip data provider implementation: (default=0) 0 to use it when there is a custom asset override. 1 to always use it. 2 to never use it.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseMaterialData
cvars.description: If non-zero, material texture scales and coord will be used

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseMobileLODBiasOnDesktopES31
cvars.description: If set apply mobile Min LOD bias on desktop platforms when running in ES31 mode

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseNewMetrics
cvars.description: If non-zero, will use improved set of metrics and heuristics.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UsePerTextureBias
cvars.description: If non-zero, each texture will be assigned a mip bias between 0 and MipBias as required to fit in budget.

cvars.name: r.Streaming.UseTextureStreamingBuiltData
cvars.description: Turn on/off usage of texture streaming built data (0 to turn off).

cvars.name: r.Substrate
cvars.description: Enable Substrate materials (Beta).

cvars.name: r.Substrate.AllocationMode
cvars.description: Substrate resource allocation mode. 0: Allocate resources based on view requirement, 1: Allocate resources based on view requirement, but can only grow over frame to minimize resources reallocation and hitches, 2: Allocate resources based on platform settings.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.AsyncClassification
cvars.description: Run Substrate material classification in async (with shadow).

cvars.name: r.Substrate.BytesPerPixel
cvars.description: Substrate allocated byte per pixel to store materials data. Higher value means more complex material can be represented.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.ClosuresPerPixel
cvars.description: Substrate closure count per pixel can be constrained. That is usefull to reduce the number of byte written durting the base pass, but also to limit the number of closures in Forward. Higher value means more complex material can be represented.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.DBufferPass
cvars.description: Apply DBuffer after the base-pass as a separate pass. Read only because when this is changed, it will require the recompilation of all shaders.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.DBufferPass.DedicatedTiles
cvars.description: Use dedicated tile for DBuffer application when DBuffer pass is enabled.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.Debug.AdvancedVisualizationShaders
cvars.description: Enable advanced Substrate material debug visualization shaders. Base pass shaders can output such advanced data.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.Debug.PeelLayersAboveDepth
cvars.description: Substrate debug control to progressively peel off materials layer by layer.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.Debug.RoughnessTracking
cvars.description: Substrate debug control to disable roughness tracking, e.g. top layer roughness affecting bottom layer roughness to simulate light scattering.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.Glints
cvars.description: Enable Glint support for Substrate slabs. If changed, shaders needs to be recompiled.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.Glints.LevelBias
cvars.description: Constantly bias the glint level. A negative value will reinforce the glint effect while a positive value will attenuate it. Be careful as this might cause aliasing.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.Glints.LevelMin
cvars.description: The minimum glint level used to render glints. Mostly affect the look of distance surfaces. A value greater than 0 will force glints to always be visible instead of using the analytic BSDF. Be careful as this might cause aliasing.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.Glints.LUT
cvars.description: Select one of the glint rendering LUT for testing.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.OpaqueMaterialRoughRefraction
cvars.description: Enable Substrate opaque material rough refractions effect from top layers over layers below.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.OpaqueMaterialRoughRefraction.BlurScale
cvars.description: Scale opaque rough refraction strengh for debug purposes.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.RoughDiffuse
cvars.description: Enable Substrate rough diffuse model (works only if r.Material.RoughDiffuse is enabled in the project settings). Togglable at runtime

cvars.name: r.Substrate.ShadingQuality
cvars.description: Define Substrate shading quality (1: accurate lighting, 2: approximate lighting). This variable is read-only.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.SheenQuality
cvars.description: Define Substrate sheen quality (1: Disney-based sheen, 2: Charlie-based sheen, ). r.Substrate.ShadingQuality=2 forces SheenQuality to 2. This variable is read-only.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.SpecularProfile
cvars.description: Enable Specular Profile support for Substrate slabs. If changed, shaders needs to be recompiled.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.SpecularProfile.ForceUpdate
cvars.description: 0: Only update the specular profile as needed. 1: Force to update the specular profile every frame for debugging.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.SpecularProfile.Resolution
cvars.description: The resolution of the specular profile texture.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.TileCoord8bits
cvars.description: Format of tile coord. This variable is read-only.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.UseClosureCountFromMaterial
cvars.description: When enable, scale the number of Lumen's layers for multi-closures pixels based on material data. Otherwise use r.Substrate.ClosuresPerPixel.

cvars.name: r.Substrate.UseCmaskClear
cvars.description: TEST.

cvars.name: r.SubstrateBackCompatibility
cvars.description: Disables Substrate multiple scattering and replaces Chan diffuse by Lambert.

cvars.name: r.SubsurfaceScattering
cvars.description: 0: disabled 1: enabled (default)

cvars.name: r.SupportAllShaderPermutations
cvars.description: Local user config override to force all shader permutation features on.

cvars.name: r.SupportCloudShadowOnForwardLitTranslucent
cvars.description: Enables cloud shadow to affect all translucenct surface not relying on the translucent lighting volume.

cvars.name: r.SupportDepthOnlyIndexBuffers
cvars.description: Enables depth-only index buffers. Saves a little time at the expense of doubling the size of index buffers.

cvars.name: r.SupportLocalFogVolumes
cvars.description: Enables local fog volume rendering and shader code.

cvars.name: r.SupportLowQualityLightmaps
cvars.description: Support low quality lightmap shader permutations

cvars.name: r.SupportPointLightWholeSceneShadows
cvars.description: Enables shadowcasting point lights.

cvars.name: r.SupportReversedIndexBuffers
cvars.description: Enables reversed index buffers. Saves a little time at the expense of doubling the size of index buffers.

cvars.name: r.SupportSkyAtmosphere
cvars.description: Enables SkyAtmosphere rendering and shader code.

cvars.name: r.SupportSkyAtmosphereAffectsHeightFog
cvars.description: Enables SkyAtmosphere affecting height fog. It requires r.SupportSkyAtmosphere to be true.

cvars.name: r.SupportStationarySkylight
cvars.description: Enables Stationary and Dynamic Skylight shader permutations.

cvars.name: r.SupportsTexture2DArrayStreaming
cvars.description: Enable Support of Texture2DArray Streaming

cvars.name: r.SupportsVolumeTextureStreaming
cvars.description: Enable Support of VolumeTexture Streaming

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.Debug.OverrideTemporalIndex
cvars.description: Override the temporal index for debugging purposes.

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.HistoryScreenPercentage
cvars.description: Size of temporal AA's history.

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.Mobile.UseCompute
cvars.description: 0: Uses pixel shader to save bandwidth with FBC on tiled gpu; 1: Uses compute shader (default);

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.Quality
cvars.description: Quality of the main Temporal AA pass. 0: Disable input filtering; 1: Enable input filtering; 2: Enable more input filtering, enable mobility based anti-ghosting (Default) 3: Quality 1 input filtering, enable anti-ghosting

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.R11G11B10History
cvars.description: Select the bitdepth of the history.

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.Upsampling
cvars.description: Whether to do primary screen percentage with temporal AA or not. 0: use spatial upscale pass independently of TAA; 1: TemporalAA performs spatial and temporal upscale as screen percentage method (default).

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.Upscaler
cvars.description: Choose the upscaling algorithm. 0: Forces the default temporal upscaler of the renderer; 1: GTemporalUpscaler which may be overridden by a third party plugin (default).

cvars.name: r.TemporalAA.UseMobileConfig
cvars.description: 1 to use mobile TAA config. This will disable groupshared caching of the color and depth buffers. 0: disabled (default); 1: enabled;

cvars.name: r.TemporalAACatmullRom
cvars.description: Whether to use a Catmull-Rom filter kernel. Should be a bit sharper than Gaussian.

cvars.name: r.TemporalAACurrentFrameWeight
cvars.description: Weight of current frame's contribution to the history. Low values cause blurriness and ghosting, high values fail to hide jittering.

cvars.name: r.TemporalAAFilterSize
cvars.description: Size of the filter kernel. (1.0 = smoother, 0.0 = sharper but aliased).

cvars.name: r.TemporalAAPauseCorrect
cvars.description: Correct temporal AA in pause. This holds onto render targets longer preventing reuse and consumes more memory.

cvars.name: r.TemporalAASamples
cvars.description: Number of jittered positions for temporal AA (4, 8=default, 16, 32, 64).

cvars.name: r.Test.Aplha.OpaqueLerpWorldRange
cvars.description: Sets the gradient length in world unit on which opaque pixel are lerped to translucent for testing purposes.

cvars.name: r.Test.Aplha.OpaqueWorldDistance
cvars.description: Sets the world distance beyond which the opaque pixel are lerped to translucent for testing purposes.

cvars.name: r.Test.CameraCut
cvars.description: Force enabling camera cut for testing purposes. 0: disabled (default); 1: enabled.

cvars.name: r.Test.ConstrainedView
cvars.description: Allows to test different viewport rectangle configuations (in game only) as they can happen when using cinematics/Editor. 0: off(default) 1..7: Various Configuations

cvars.name: r.Test.DynamicResolutionHell
cvars.description: Override the screen percentage interface for all view family with dynamic resolution hell. 0: off (default); 1: Dynamic resolution hell.

cvars.name: r.Test.EditorConstrainedView
cvars.description: Allows to test different viewport rectangle configuations (in game only) as they can happen when using cinematics/Editor. 0: off(default) 1..7: Various Configuations

cvars.name: r.Test.ForceBlackVelocityBuffer
cvars.description: Force the velocity buffer to have no motion vector for debugging purpose.

cvars.name: r.Test.FreezeTemporalHistories
cvars.description: Freezes all temporal histories as well as the temporal sequence.

cvars.name: r.Test.FreezeTemporalHistories.Progress
cvars.description: Progress the temporal histories by one frame when modified.

cvars.name: r.Test.FreezeTemporalSequences
cvars.description: Freezes all temporal sequences.

cvars.name: r.Test.OverrideTimeMaterialExpressions
cvars.description: Value to freeze time material expressions with.

cvars.name: r.Test.PrimaryScreenPercentageMethodOverride
cvars.description: Override the screen percentage method for all view family. 0: view family's screen percentage interface choose; (default) 1: old fashion upscaling pass at the very end right before before UI; 2: TemporalAA upsample.

cvars.name: r.Test.SecondaryUpscaleOverride
cvars.description: Override the secondary upscale. 0: disabled; (default) 1: use secondary view fraction = 0.5 with nearest secondary upscale.

cvars.name: r.Test.ViewRectOffset
cvars.description: Moves the view rect within the renderer's internal render target. 0: disabled (default);

cvars.name: r.TexelDebugging
cvars.description: Whether T + Left mouse click in the editor selects lightmap texels for debugging Lightmass. Lightmass must be recompiled with ALLOW_LIGHTMAP_SAMPLE_DEBUGGING enabled for this to work.

cvars.name: r.TextureProfiler.EnableRenderTargetCSV
cvars.description: True to enable csv profiler output for all Render Targets.

cvars.name: r.TextureProfiler.EnableTextureCSV
cvars.description: True to enable csv profiler output for all textures. Does not include render targets.

cvars.name: r.TextureProfiler.MinRenderTargetSizeMB
cvars.description: The minimum combined size for render targets to be reported. All combined sizes less than this threshold will be reported as Other.

cvars.name: r.TextureProfiler.MinTextureSizeMB
cvars.description: The minimum size for any texture to be reported. All textures below this threshold will be reported as Other.

cvars.name: r.TexturesComputeChannelMinMaxDuringSave
cvars.description: Whether textures determine per channel min/max on save for early format computation.

cvars.name: r.TexturesCookToDerivedDataReferences
cvars.description: Whether cooked textures are serialized using Derived Data References.

cvars.name: r.TextureStreaming
cvars.description: Allows to define if texture streaming is enabled, can be changed at run time. 0: off 1: on (default)

cvars.name: r.Tonemapper.Quality
cvars.description: Defines the Tonemapper Quality in the range 0..5 Depending on the used settings we might pick a faster shader permutation 0: basic tonemapper only, lowest quality 2: + Vignette 4: + Grain 5: + GrainJitter = full quality (default)

cvars.name: r.Tonemapper.Sharpen
cvars.description: Sharpening in the tonemapper (not for mobile), actual implementation is work in progress, clamped at 10 <0: inherit from PostProcessVolume settings (default) 0: off 0.5: half strength 1: full strength

cvars.name: r.TonemapperGamma
cvars.description: 0: Default behavior #: Use fixed gamma # instead of sRGB or Rec709 transform

cvars.name: r.Translucency.AutoBeforeDOF
cvars.description: Automatically bin After DOF translucency before DOF if behind focus distance (Experimental)

cvars.name: r.Translucency.DynamicRes.ChangePercentageThreshold
cvars.description: Minimal increase percentage threshold to alow when changing resolution of translucency.

cvars.name: r.Translucency.DynamicRes.MaxScreenPercentage
cvars.description: Maximal screen percentage for translucency.

cvars.name: r.Translucency.DynamicRes.MinScreenPercentage
cvars.description: Minimal screen percentage for translucency.

cvars.name: r.Translucency.DynamicRes.TargetedHeadRoomPercentage
cvars.description: Targeted GPU headroom for translucency (in percent from r.DynamicRes.DynamicRes.TimeBudget).

cvars.name: r.Translucency.DynamicRes.TimeBudget
cvars.description: Frame's time budget for translucency rendering in milliseconds.

cvars.name: r.Translucency.DynamicRes.UpperBoundQuantization
cvars.description: Quantization step count to use for upper bound screen percentage. If non-zero, rendertargets will be resized based on the dynamic resolution fraction, saving GPU time during clears and resolves. Only recommended for use with the transient allocator (on supported platforms) with a large transient texture cache (e.g RHI.TransientAllocator.TextureCacheSize=512)

cvars.name: r.Translucency.HeterogeneousVolumes
cvars.description: Enables composting with heterogeneous volumes when rendering translucency (Default = 0)

cvars.name: r.Translucency.ScreenPercentage.Basis
cvars.description: Basis of the translucency's screen percentage (Experimental). 0: Uses the primary view's resolution (notably scaling with r.ScreenPercentage and r.DynamicRes.*) 1: Uses the secondary view's resolution (temporal upscale's output resolution)

cvars.name: r.Translucency.StandardSeparated
cvars.description: Render translucent meshes in separate buffer from the scene color. This prevent those meshes from self refracting and leaking scnee color behind over edges when it should be affect by colored transmittance. Forced disabled when r.SeparateTranslucency is 0.

cvars.name: r.Translucency.Velocity
cvars.description: Whether translucency can draws depth/velocity (enabled by default)

cvars.name: r.TranslucencyLightingVolumeDim
cvars.description: Dimensions of the volume textures used for translucency lighting. Larger textures result in higher resolution but lower performance.

cvars.name: r.TranslucencyLightingVolumeInnerDistance
cvars.description: Distance from the camera that the first volume cascade should end

cvars.name: r.TranslucencyLightingVolumeOuterDistance
cvars.description: Distance from the camera that the second volume cascade should end

cvars.name: r.TranslucencyVolumeBlur
cvars.description: Whether to blur the translucent lighting volumes. 0:off, otherwise on, default is 1

cvars.name: r.Translucent.UsesIESProfiles
cvars.description: Enable IES profiles support for translucent surfaces. When enabled, it will add an extrat sampler to the pixel shader (limited to 16 on dx11 based system)

cvars.name: r.Translucent.UsesLightFunctionAtlas
cvars.description: Enable sampling of the light function atlas on translucent materials using forward shading.

cvars.name: r.Translucent.UsesRectLights
cvars.description: Enable rect light support for translucent surfaces. When enabled, it will add an extrat sampler to the pixel shader (limited to 16 on dx11 based system)

cvars.name: r.TranslucentLightingVolume
cvars.description: Whether to allow updating the translucent lighting volumes. 0:off, otherwise on, default is 1

cvars.name: r.TranslucentSortPolicy
cvars.description: 0: Sort based on distance from camera centerpoint to bounding sphere centerpoint. (default, best for 3D games) 1: Sort based on projected distance to camera. 2: Sort based on the projection onto a fixed axis. (best for 2D games)

cvars.name: r.TranslucentVolumeFOVSnapFactor
cvars.description: FOV will be snapped to a factor of this before computing volume bounds.

cvars.name: r.TranslucentVolumeMinFOV
cvars.description: Minimum FOV for translucent lighting volume. Prevents popping in lighting when zooming in.

cvars.name: r.TSR.16BitVALU
cvars.description: Whether to use 16bit VALU on platform that have bSupportsRealTypes=RuntimeDependent

cvars.name: r.TSR.16BitVALU.AMD
cvars.description: Overrides whether to use 16bit VALU on AMD desktop GPUs

cvars.name: r.TSR.16BitVALU.Intel
cvars.description: Overrides whether to use 16bit VALU on Intel desktop GPUs

cvars.name: r.TSR.16BitVALU.Nvidia
cvars.description: Overrides whether to use 16bit VALU on Nvidia desktop GPUs

cvars.name: r.TSR.AplhaChannel
cvars.description: Controls whether TSR should process the scene color's alpha channel. -1: based of r.PostProcessing.PropagateAlpha (default); 0: disabled; 1: enabled.

cvars.name: r.TSR.AsyncCompute
cvars.description: Controls how TSR run on async compute. Some TSR passes can overlap with previous passes. 0: Disabled; 1: Run on async compute only passes that are completly independent from any intermediary resource of this frame, namely ClearPrevTextures and ForwardScatterDepth passes; 2: Run on async compute only passes that are completly independent or only dependent on the depth and velocity buffer which can overlap for instance with translucency or DOF. Any passes on critical path remains on the graphics queue (default); 3: Run all passes on async compute;

cvars.name: r.TSR.Debug.ArraySize
cvars.description: Size of array for the TSR.Debug.* RDG textures

cvars.name: r.TSR.ForceSeparateTranslucency
cvars.description: Overrides r.SeparateTranslucency whenever TSR is enabled (enabled by default).

cvars.name: r.TSR.History.R11G11B10
cvars.description: Select the bitdepth of the history. r.TSR.History.R11G11B10=1 Saves memory bandwidth that is of particular interest of the TSR's UpdateHistory's runtime performance by saving memory both at previous frame's history reprojection and write out of the output and new history. This optimisation is unsupported with r.PostProcessing.PropagateAlpha=1. Please also not that increasing r.TSR.History.ScreenPercentage=200 adds 2 additional implicit encoding bits in the history compared to the TSR.Output's bitdepth thanks to the downscaling pass from TSR history resolution to TSR output resolution.

cvars.name: r.TSR.History.SampleCount
cvars.description: Maximum number sample for each output pixel in the history. Higher values means more stability on highlights on static images, but may introduce additional ghosting on firefliers style of VFX. Minimum value supported is 8.0 as TSR was in 5.0 and 5.1. Maximum value possible due to the encoding of the TSR.History.Metadata is 32.0. Defaults to 16.0. Use r.TSR.Visualize 0" command to see how many samples where accumulated in TSR history on areas of the screen."

cvars.name: r.TSR.History.ScreenPercentage
cvars.description: Resolution multiplier of the history of TSR based of output resolution. While increasing the resolution adds runtime cost to TSR, it allows to maintain a better sharpness and stability of the details stored in history through out the reprojection. Setting to 200 brings on a very particular property relying on NyQuist-Shannon sampling theorem that establishes a sufficient condition for the sample rate of the accumulated details in the history. As a result only values between 100 and 200 are supported. It is controlled by default in the anti-aliasing scalability group set to 200 on Epic and Cinematic, 100 otherwise.

cvars.name: r.TSR.History.UpdateQuality
cvars.description: Selects shader permutation of the quality of the update of the history in the TSR HistoryUpdate pass currently driven by the sg.AntiAliasingQuality scalability group. For further details about what each offers, you are invited to look at DIM_UPDATE_QUALITY in TSRUpdateHistory.usf and customise to your need.

cvars.name: r.TSR.RejectionAntiAliasingQuality
cvars.description: Controls the quality of TSR's built-in spatial anti-aliasing technology when the history needs to be rejected. While this may not be critical when the rendering resolution is not much lowered than display resolution, this technic however becomes essential to hide lower rendering resolution rendering because of two reasons: - the screen space size of aliasing is inverse proportional to rendering resolution; - rendering at lower resolution means need more frame to reach at least 1 rendered pixel per display pixel. Use r.TSR.Visualize 6" command to see on screen where the spatial anti-aliaser quicks in green. By default, it is only disabled by default in the low anti-aliasing scalability group."

cvars.name: r.TSR.Resurrection
cvars.description: Allows TSR to resurrect previously discarded details from many frames ago. When enabled, the entire frames of the TSR are stored in a same unique Texture2DArray including a configurable number of persistent frame (defined by r.TSR.Resurrection.PersistentFrameCount) that are occasionally recorded (defined by r.TSR.Resurrection.PersistentFrameInterval). Then every frame, TSR will attempt to reproject either previous frame, or the oldest persistent frame available based which matches best the current frames. The later option will happen when something previously seen by TSR shows up again (no matter through parallax disocclusion, shading changes, translucent VFX moving) which will have the advantage bypass the need to newly accumulate a second time by simply resurrected the previously accumulated details. Command r.TSR.Visualize 4" too see parts of the screen is being resurrected by TSR in green. Command "r.TSR.Visualize 5" too see the oldest frame being possibly resurrected. Currently experimental and disabled by default."

cvars.name: r.TSR.Resurrection.PersistentFrameCount
cvars.description: Configures the number of persistent frame to record in history for futur history resurrection. This will increase the memory footprint of the entire TSR history. Must be an even number greater or equal to 2. (default=2)

cvars.name: r.TSR.Resurrection.PersistentFrameInterval
cvars.description: Configures in number of frames how often persistent frame should be recorded in history for futur history resurrection. This has no implication on memory footprint of the TSR history. Must be an odd number greater or equal to 1. Uses the VisualizeTSR show flag and r.TSR.Visualize=5 to tune this parameter to your content. (default=31)

cvars.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.ExposureOffset
cvars.description: The shading rejection needs to have a representative idea how bright a linear color pixel ends up displayed to the user. And the shading rejection detect if a color become to changed to be visible in the back buffer by comparing to MeasureBackbufferLDRQuantizationError(). It is important to have TSR's MeasureBackbufferLDRQuantizationError() ends up distributed uniformly across the range of color intensity or it could otherwise disregard some subtle VFX causing ghostin. This controls adjusts the exposure of the linear color space solely in the TSR's rejection heuristic, such that higher value lifts the shadows's LDR intensity, meaning MeasureBackbufferLDRQuantizationError() is decreased in these shadows and increased in the highlights, control directly. The best TSR internal buffer to verify this is TSR.Flickering.Luminance, either with the show VisualizeTemporalUpscaler" command or in DumpGPU with the RGB Linear[0;1] source color space against the Tonemaper's output in sRGB source color space."

cvars.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering
cvars.description: Instability in TSR output 99% of the time coming from instability of the shading rejection, for different reasons: - One first source of instability is most famously moire pattern between structured geometry and the rendering pixel grid changing every frame due to the offset of the jittering pixel grid offset; - Another source of instability can happen on extrem geometric complexity due to temporal history's chicken-and-egg problem that can not be overcome by other mechanisms in place in TSR's RejectHistory pass: how can the history be identical to rendered frame if the amount of details you have in the rendered frame is not in history? how can the history accumulate details if the history is too different from the rendered frame? When enabled, this flickering temporal analysis monitor how the luminance of the scene right before any translucency drawing stored in the TSR.Flickering.Luminance resource how it involves over successive frames. And if it is detected to constantly flicker regularily above a certain threshold defined with this r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.* cvars, the heuristic attempts to stabilize the image by letting ghost within luminance boundary tied to the amplititude of flickering. Use r.TSR.Visualize 7 command to see on screen where this heuristic quicks in orange and red. Pink is where it is disabled. One particular caveat of this heuristic is that any opaque geometry with incorrect motion vector can make a pixel look identically flickery quicking this heuristic in and leaving undesired ghosting effects on the said geometry. When that happens, it is highly encourage to verify the motion vector through the VisualizeMotionBlur show flag and how these motion vectors are able to reproject previous frame with the VisualizeReprojection show flag. The variable to countrol the frame frequency at which a pixel is considered flickery and needs to be stabilized with this heuristic is defined with the r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.Period in frames. For instance, a value r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.Period=3, it means any pixel that have its luminance changing of variation every more often than every frames is considered flickering. However another caveats on this boundary between flickering pixel versus animated pixel is that: flickering happens regardless of frame rate whereas a visual effects that are or should be based on time and are therefore independent of the frame rate. This mean that a visual effect that looks smooth at 60hz might appear to 'flicker' at lower frame rates, like 24hz for instance. To make sure a visual effect authored by an artists doesn't start to ghost of frame rate, r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.AdjustToFrameRate is enabled by default such that this frame frequency boundary is automatically when the frame rate drops below a refresh rate below r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.FrameRateCap. While r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering is controled based of scalability settings turn on/off this heuristic on lower or high-end GPU the other r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.* can be set orthogonally in the Project's DefaultEngine.ini for a consistent behavior across all platforms.It is enabled by default in the anti-aliasing scalability group High, Epic, and Cinematic.

cvars.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.AdjustToFrameRate
cvars.description: Whether r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.Period settings should adjust to frame rate when below r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.FrameRateCap. Please read r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering's help for further details. (Enabled by default).

cvars.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.FrameRateCap
cvars.description: Framerate cap in hertz at which point there is automatic adjustment of r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.Period when the rendering frame rate is lower. Please read r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering's help for further details. (Default to 60hz)

cvars.name: r.TSR.ShadingRejection.Flickering.MaxParallaxVelocity
cvars.description: Some material might for instance might do something like parallax occlusion mapping such as CitySample's buildings' window's interiors.
