cvars.name: SigMan.FilterTag
cvars.description: Only display objects with the specified filter tag. If None objects with any will be displayed.

cvars.name: SigMan.ObjectsToShow
cvars.description: How many objects to display when ShowDebug SignificanceManager is enabled.

cvars.name: SkeletalMesh.UseExperimentalChunking
cvars.description: Whether skeletal mesh will use a experimental chunking algorithm when building LODModel.

cvars.name: SkinWeightProfileManager.AllowCPU
cvars.description: Whether or not to allow cpu buffer generation

cvars.name: Slate.AbsoluteIndices
cvars.description: 0: Each element first vertex index starts at 0 (default), 1: Use absolute indices, simplifies draw call setup on RHIs that do not support BaseVertex

cvars.name: Slate.AccessibleWidgetsProcessedPerTick
cvars.description: To reduce performance spikes, generating the accessible widget tree is limited to this many widgets per tick to update.

cvars.name: Slate.AllowBackgroundBlurWidgets
cvars.description: If 0, no background blur widgets will be rendered

cvars.name: Slate.AllowPerUserHitTesting
cvars.description: Toggles between widgets mapping to a user id and requring a matching user id from an input event or allowing all users to interact with widget

cvars.name: Slate.AllowSlateToSleep
cvars.description: Whether Slate should go to sleep when there are no active timers and the user is idle

cvars.name: Slate.AlwaysInvalidate
cvars.description: Forces invalidation panels to cache, but to always invalidate.

cvars.name: Slate.ApplyDisabledEffectOnWidgets
cvars.description: If true, disabled game-layer widgets will have alpha multiplied by 0.45.

cvars.name: Slate.BackgroundBlurMaxKernelSize
cvars.description: The maximum allowed kernel size. Note: Very large numbers can cause a huge decrease in performance

cvars.name: Slate.bAllowNotifications
cvars.description: If false, then notifications will not be displayed to the user.

cvars.name: Slate.bAllowThrottling
cvars.description: Allow Slate to throttle parts of the engine to ensure the UI is responsive

cvars.name: Slate.Contrast
cvars.description: The amount of contrast to apply to the UI (default 1).

cvars.name: Slate.CopyBackbufferToSlatePostRenderTargets
cvars.description: Experimental. Set true to copy final backbuffer into slate RTs for slate post processing / material usage

cvars.name: Slate.CullingSlackFillPercent
cvars.description: Scales the culling rect by the amount to provide extra slack/wiggle room for widgets that have a true bounds larger than the root child widget in a container.

cvars.name: Slate.CursorSignificantMoveDetectionThreshold
cvars.description: The distance from previous cursor position above which the move will be considered significant (used to trigger the display of the tooltips).

cvars.name: Slate.Debug.TraceNavigationConfig
cvars.description: True enables tracing of navigation config & callstack to log.

cvars.name: Slate.DebugCulling
cvars.description: Controls whether we should ignore clip rects, and just use culling.

cvars.name: Slate.DefaultEnablePostRenderTarget_0
cvars.description: Experimental. Set true to enable slate post render target 0

cvars.name: Slate.DefaultTextFlowDirection
cvars.description: 0: Auto (default), 1: LeftToRight, 2: RightToLeft.

cvars.name: Slate.DefaultTextShapingMethod
cvars.description: 0: Auto (default), 1: KerningOnly, 2: FullShaping.

cvars.name: Slate.DeferRetainedRenderingRenderThread
cvars.description: Whether or not to defer retained rendering to happen at the same time as the rest of slate render thread work

cvars.name: Slate.DeferWindowsMessageProcessing
cvars.description: Whether windows message processing is deferred until tick or if they are processed immediately

cvars.name: Slate.DemoMode.KeyEvent
cvars.description: Visualize any pressed keys for demo-recording purposes.

cvars.name: Slate.DemoMode.MouseEvent
cvars.description: Visualize the cursor events for demo-recording purposes.

cvars.name: Slate.DrawToVRRenderTarget
cvars.description: If enabled while in VR. Slate UI will be drawn into the render target texture where the VR imagery for either eye was rendered, allow the viewer of the HMD to see the UI (for better or worse.) This render target will then be cropped/scaled into the back buffer, if mirroring is enabled. When disabled, Slate UI will be drawn on top of the backbuffer (not to the HMD) after the mirror texture has been cropped/scaled into the backbuffer.

cvars.name: Slate.DumpFontCacheStats
cvars.description: Dump statistics about font cache usage.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableCrashHandler
cvars.description: Slate will add states into the crash reporter.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableDeleteUnusedPostProcess
cvars.description: Greater than zero implies that post process render targets will be deleted when they are not used after n frames.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableDesignerRetainedRendering
cvars.description: Controls if retainer renders in designer; 0 - Never; 1 - Per Widget Properties

cvars.name: Slate.EnableFastWidgetPath
cvars.description: Whether or not we enable fast widget pathing. This mode relies on parent pointers to work correctly.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableFontAntiAliasing
cvars.description: Enable or disable anti-aliasing for font rendering (0 = off, 1 = on). Enabled by default.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableGamepadEditorNavigation
cvars.description: True implies we allow gamepad navigation outside of the game viewport.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableInvalidationPanels
cvars.description: Whether to attempt to cache any widgets through invalidation panels.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableLayoutLocalization
cvars.description: Controls if we enable or disable localized layout, which affects left to right or right to left detection for cultures.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableLegacyFontHinting
cvars.description: Enable the legacy font hinting? (0/1).

cvars.name: Slate.EnableRetainedRendering
cvars.description: Whether to attempt to render things in SRetainerWidgets to render targets first.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableRetainedRenderingWithLocalTransform
cvars.description: This console variable is no longer useful, as the SRetainerWidget render bugs workedaround by it have been fixed. It will be removed.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableSlateWidgetTracker
cvars.description: Whether or not we enable the tracking of widgets via the Slate Widget Tracker.

cvars.name: Slate.EnableTooltips
cvars.description: Whether to allow tooltips to spawn at all.

cvars.name: Slate.EnsureAllVisibleWidgetsPaint
cvars.description: Ensures that if a child widget is visible before OnPaint, that it was painted this frame after OnPaint, if still marked as visible. Only works if we're on the FastPaintPath.

cvars.name: Slate.EnsureOutgoingLayerId
cvars.description: Ensures that child widget returns the correct layer id with OnPaint.

cvars.name: Slate.FlushFontCache
cvars.description: Flush the font cache.

cvars.name: Slate.Font.AsyncLazyLoad
cvars.description: Causes unloaded font faces that are lazily loaded, to be loaded asynchronusly, until then the font won't measure correctly. Once complete the UI will invalidate.

cvars.name: Slate.ForceBackgroundBlurLowQualityOverride
cvars.description: Whether or not to force a slate brush to be used instead of actually blurring the background

cvars.name: Slate.GameLayer.AllCanvasesVisible
cvars.description: Show/Hide the viewport slot, player canvas, and debug canvas.

cvars.name: Slate.GameLayer.DebugCanvasVisible
cvars.description: Show/Hide the debug canvas.

cvars.name: Slate.GameLayer.PlayerCanvasVisible
cvars.description: Show/Hide the player canvas.

cvars.name: Slate.GameLayer.ViewportSlotVisible
cvars.description: Show/Hide the slot on viewport

cvars.name: Slate.GlobalScrollAmount
cvars.description: How much to scroll for each click of the mouse wheel (in Slate Screen Units).

cvars.name: Slate.GrowFontAtlasFrameWindow
cvars.description: The number of frames within the font atlas will resize rather than flush.

cvars.name: Slate.GrowFontNonAtlasFrameWindow
cvars.description: The number of frames within the large font glyph pool will resize rather than flush.

cvars.name: Slate.GrowSVGAtlasFrameWindow
cvars.description: The number of frames within the atlas will resize rather than flush.

cvars.name: Slate.GrowSVGNonAtlasFrameWindow
cvars.description: The number of frames within the large pool will resize rather than flush.

cvars.name: Slate.HitTestGridDebugging
cvars.description: Whether to show a visualization of everything in the hit teest grid

cvars.name: Slate.Input.MotionFiresUserInteractionEvents
cvars.description: If this is false, LastUserInteractionTimeUpdateEvent events won't be fired based on motion input, and LastInteractionTime won't be updated Some motion devices report small tiny changes constantly without filtering, so motion input is unhelpful for determining user activity

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationDebugging
cvars.description: Deprecated - Use SlateDebugger.Invalidate.Enable

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationList.EnableReindexLayerId
cvars.description: With invalidation system, when a painted widget returns a bigger LayerId that it used to, re-index the other widgets.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationList.MaxArrayElements
cvars.description: With the invalidation system, the preferred size of the elements array.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationList.NumberElementLeftBeforeSplitting
cvars.description: With the invalidation system, when splitting, only split the array when the number of element left is under X.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.DumpPostInvalidationList
cvars.description: Each frame, log the widgets that are processed in the post update phase.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.DumpPreInvalidationList
cvars.description: Each frame, log the widgets that are processed in the pre update phase.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.DumpPrepassInvalidationList
cvars.description: Each frame, log the widgets that are processed in the prepass update phase.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.DumpUpdateList
cvars.description: Each frame, log the widgets that will be updated.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.DumpUpdateListOnce
cvars.description: Log the widgets that will be updated this frame.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyHittestGrid
cvars.description: Every tick, verify the hittest grid.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifySlateAttribute
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that the widgets that have registered attribute are correctly updated once and the list contains all the widgets.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyValidWidgets
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that every WidgetProxy has a valid SWidget.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyWidgetHeapContains
cvars.description: Verify that the widget is not already in the list before adding it.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyWidgetList
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that the updated list does match a newly created list.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyWidgetsAreUpdatedOnce
cvars.description: Verify that the widgets are painted only once per tick.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyWidgetsIndex
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that every widgets has the correct corresponding index.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyWidgetUpdateList
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that pre and post update list contains the correct information and they are sorted.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyWidgetVisibility
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that the cached visibility of the widgets is properly set.

cvars.name: Slate.InvalidationRoot.VerifyWidgetVolatile
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that volatile widgets are mark properly and are in the correct list.

cvars.name: Slate.LogPaintedText
cvars.description: If true, all text that is visible to the user will be logged when it is painted. This will log the full text to be painted, not the truncated or clipped version based on UI constraints.

cvars.name: Slate.MaxFontAtlasPagesBeforeFlush
cvars.description: The number of font atlas textures created and used before we flush the font cache if a texture atlas is full

cvars.name: Slate.MaxFontNonAtlasTexturesBeforeFlush
cvars.description: The number of large glyph font textures initially.

cvars.name: Slate.MaxSVGAtlasPagesBeforeFlush
cvars.description: The number of atlas textures created and used before we flush the cache if a texture atlas is full

cvars.name: Slate.MaxSVGNonAtlasTexturesBeforeFlush
cvars.description: The number of large textures initially.

cvars.name: Slate.MemorylessDepthStencil
cvars.description: Whether to use memoryless DepthStencil target for Slate. Reduces memory usage and implies that DepthStencil state can't be preserved between Slate renderpasses

cvars.name: Slate.OutlineFontRenderMethod
cvars.description: Changes the render method for outline fonts. 0 = freetype does everything and generates a bitmap for the base glyph (default). 1 = We override the freetype rasterizer. Can help with some rendering anomalies on complicated fonts.

cvars.name: Slate.OverrideScissorRect
cvars.description: Whether to allow Slate to apply a scissor clip to UI elements to prevent certain artifacts. You might need to disable this if you are drawing UI over a mirror backbuffer while in VR.

cvars.name: Slate.PreventDuplicateMouseEventsForTouchForWindows7
cvars.description: Hack to get around multiple mouse events being triggered for touch events on Windows 7 and lower. Enabling this will prevent pen tablets from working on windows 7 since until we switch to the windows 8 sdk (and can use WM_POINTER* events) we cannot detect the difference

cvars.name: Slate.RequireFocusForGamepadInput
cvars.description: Whether gamepad input should be ignored by the engine if the application is not currently active

cvars.name: Slate.ResourceManager.LockResourceDuringGC
cvars.description: Lock the Slate RHI Resource Manager when GCing and when the loading screen has ownership to prevent multithreaded access to the resources.

cvars.name: Slate.ShouldFollowCultureByDefault
cvars.description: Should we initially follow the culture's flow direction at the window level.

cvars.name: Slate.ShowBatching
cvars.description: 0: Don't show batching, 1: Show Batching

cvars.name: Slate.ShowClipping
cvars.description: Controls whether we should render a clipping zone outline. Yellow = Axis Scissor Rect Clipping (cheap). Red = Stencil Clipping (expensive).

cvars.name: Slate.ShowOverdraw
cvars.description: 0: Don't show overdraw, 1: Show Overdraw

cvars.name: Slate.ShowTextDebugging
cvars.description: Show debugging painting for text rendering.

cvars.name: Slate.SleepBufferPostInput
cvars.description: The amount of time that must pass without any user action before Slate is put to sleep (provided that there are no active timers).

cvars.name: Slate.SoftwareCursorScale
cvars.description: Scale factor applied to the software cursor. Requires the cursor widget to be scale-aware.

cvars.name: Slate.TargetFrameRateForResponsiveness
cvars.description: Minimum sustained average frame rate required before we consider the editor to be responsive" for a smooth UI experience"

cvars.name: Slate.ThrottleWhenMouseIsMoving
cvars.description: Whether to attempt to increase UI responsiveness based on mouse cursor movement.

cvars.name: Slate.TooltipIntroDuration
cvars.description: How long it takes for a tooltip to animate into view, in seconds.

cvars.name: Slate.TooltipSummonDelay
cvars.description: Delay in seconds before a tooltip is displayed near the mouse cursor when hovering over widgets that supply tooltip data.

cvars.name: Slate.ToolTipWrapWidth
cvars.description: Width of Slate tool-tips before we wrap the tool-tip text

cvars.name: Slate.Transform.FullscreenMouseInput
cvars.description: Set true to transform mouse input to account for viewport stretching at fullscreen resolutions not natively supported by the monitor.

cvars.name: Slate.UnloadFreeTypeDataOnFlush
cvars.description: Releases the free type data when the font cache is flushed

cvars.name: Slate.UseFixedDeltaTime
cvars.description: True means we use a constant delta time on every widget tick.

cvars.name: Slate.VerifyParentChildrenRelationship
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that a widget has only one parent.

cvars.name: Slate.VerifyWidgetLayerId
cvars.description: Every tick, verify that widgets have a LayerId range that fits with their siblings and their parent.

cvars.name: Slate.WorldWidgetIgnoreNotVisibleWidgets
cvars.description: Whether to not update the position of world widgets if they are not visible - to prevent invalidating the whole layer unnecessarily

cvars.name: Slate.WorldWidgetZOrder
cvars.description: Whether to re-order world widgets projected to screen by their view point distance 0: Disable re-ordering 1: Re-order by distance (default, less batching, less artifacts when widgets overlap)

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.bCaptureRootInvalidationCallstacks
cvars.description: Whenever a widget is the root cause of an invalidation, capture the callstack for slate insights.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.CaptureStack
cvars.description: Should we capture the stack when there are events?

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.InputRoutingModeEnabled
cvars.description: Should we output the route that an input event took?

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.LogAttemptNavigationEvent
cvars.description: Log attempt navigation events

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.LogCaptureStateChangeEvent
cvars.description: Log cursor state change events

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.LogCursorChangeEvent
cvars.description: Log cursor change events

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.LogExecuteNavigationEvent
cvars.description: Log execute navigation events

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.LogFocusEvent
cvars.description: Log focus events

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.LogInputEvent
cvars.description: Log input events

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Event.LogWarning
cvars.description: Log warning events

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Invalidate.bLogInvalidatedWidget
cvars.description: Option to log the invalidated widget to the console.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Invalidate.bShowLegend
cvars.description: Option to display the color legend.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Invalidate.bShowWidgetList
cvars.description: Option to display the names of invalidated widgets.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Invalidate.bUsePerformanceThreshold
cvars.description: Only display the invalidated widgets and/or log them if the performance are worst than the threshold (in millisecond).

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Invalidate.Enabled
cvars.description: Start/Stop the Invalidation widget debug tool. It shows widgets that are invalidated.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Invalidate.ThresholdPerformanceMS
cvars.description: For bUsePerformanceThreshold, threshold in milliseconds to reach before logging and/or displaying the invalidated widgets.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.InvalidationRoot.Enable
cvars.description: Start/Stop the Invalidation Root widget debug tool. It shows when Invalidation Roots are using the slow or the fast path.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Paint.Enable
cvars.description: Start/Stop the painted widget debug tool. It shows when widgets are painted.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Paint.LogWarningIfWidgetIsPaintedMoreThanOnce
cvars.description: Option to log a warning if a widget is painted more than once in a single frame.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Paint.MaxNumberOfWidgetDisplayedInList
cvars.description: The max number of widgets that will be displayed when DisplayWidgetNameList is active.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Paint.OnlyGameWindow
cvars.description: Option to only the debug the game window

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Update.Enable
cvars.description: Start/Stop the painted widget debug tool. It shows when widgets are updated.

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Update.OnlyGameWindow
cvars.description: Option to only the debug the game window

cvars.name: SlateDebugger.Update.SetInvalidationRootIdFilter
cvars.description: Option to show only the widgets that are part of an invalidation root.

cvars.name: SlateSdfText.Enable
cvars.description: Enables MSDF-based text rendering in Slate

cvars.name: SlateSdfText.GeneratorPoolSize
cvars.description: Sets the maximum number of concurrent tasks when generating multi-channel distance fields for Slate text glyphs

cvars.name: SourceControl.LoginDialog.ForceModal
cvars.description: Forces the SourceControl 'Login Dialog' to always be a modal dialog.

cvars.name: SourceControl.P4.AllowNonTicketLogins
cvars.description: Whether or not to allow logging in with a password directly from the perforce dialog. This is off by default because it is not a secure option. Perforce often your password as plain text in their enviroment variables

cvars.name: SourceControl.Perforce.IdleConnectionDisconnectSeconds
cvars.description: The number of seconds a perforce connection will be kept open without activity before being automatically disconnected

cvars.name: SourceControl.Revert.EnableFromSceneOutliner
cvars.description: Allows a SourceControl 'Revert' operation to be triggered from the SceneOutliner.

cvars.name: SourceControl.Revert.EnableFromSubmitWidget
cvars.description: Allows a SourceControl 'Revert' operation to be triggered from the SubmitWidget.

cvars.name: SourceControl.RevertUnsaved.Enable
cvars.description: Allows a SourceControl 'Revert' operation to be triggered on an unsaved asset.

cvars.name: SourceControlAssetDataCache.MaxAsyncTask
cvars.description: Maximum number of task running in parallel to fetch AssetData information.

cvars.name: spectatorbeacon.DelayCancellationResponse
cvars.description: Delay time between received cancel response and notification Time in secs

cvars.name: spectatorbeacon.DelayFullResponse
cvars.description: Delay time between received full response and notification Time in secs

cvars.name: spectatorbeacon.DelayReservationResponse
cvars.description: Delay time between received response and notification Time in secs

cvars.name: spectatorbeacon.DelayUpdateResponse
cvars.description: Delay time between received update response and notification Time in secs

cvars.name: SpewAnimRateOptimization
cvars.description: True to spew overall anim rate optimization tick rates.

cvars.name: splines.blockall
cvars.description: Force splines to always use the BlockAll collision profile instead of whatever is stored in the CollisionProfileName property

cvars.name: StallDetector.ForceLogOnStall
cvars.description: Forces StallDetector to make a log to LogStall at verbosity=Log even if reporting mode is disabled

cvars.name: stats.AutoEnableNamedEventsWhenProfiling
cvars.description: If 1, toggles named events on when a profiler is detected and capturing. Toggles named events off if 0 or when profiling stops.

cvars.name: stats.MaxPerGroup
cvars.description: The max number of lines of stats to show in a group

cvars.name: stats.SpewSpam
cvars.description: If set to 1, periodically prints a profile of messages coming into the stats system. Messages should be minimized to cut down on overhead.

cvars.name: t.DumpHitches.AllThreads
cvars.description: Dump all Threads when doing stat dumphitches 0: Only Game and Render Threads (default) 1: All threads

cvars.name: t.FPSChart.DoCsvProfile
cvars.description: Whether to record a CSV profile when recording FPSChart data default: 0

cvars.name: t.FPSChart.ExcludeIdleTime
cvars.description: Should we exclude idle time (i.e. one which we spent sleeping) when doing a FPS chart? default: 0

cvars.name: t.FPSChart.InterestingFramerates
cvars.description: Comma separated list of interesting frame rates default: 30,60,120

cvars.name: t.FPSChart.MaxFrameDeltaSecsBeforeDiscarding
cvars.description: The maximum length a frame can be (in seconds) to be considered for FPS chart binning (default 1.0s; no maximum length if <= 0.0)

cvars.name: t.FPSChart.OpenFolderOnDump
cvars.description: Should we explore to the folder that contains the .log / etc... when a dump is finished? This can be disabled for automated testing default: 1

cvars.name: t.FPSChart.RoundFPSBeforeBinning
cvars.description: Should we round raw FPS values before thresholding them into bins when doing a FPS chart? default: 0

cvars.name: t.HitchDeadTimeWindow
cvars.description: Minimum time passed before we'll record a new hitch (in ms) default: 200.0 ms

cvars.name: t.HitchFrameTimeThreshold
cvars.description: Definition of a hitchy frame (in ms) default: 60.0 ms

cvars.name: t.HitchVersusNonHitchRatio
cvars.description: For the current frame to be considered a hitch, it must have run at least this many times slower than the previous frame. The actual ratio is clamped to be between this and t.HitchFrameTimeThreshold/t.TargetFrameTimeThreshold default: 1.5

cvars.name: t.IdleWhenNotForeground
cvars.description: Prevents the engine from taking any CPU or GPU time while not the foreground app.

cvars.name: t.MaxFPS
cvars.description: Caps FPS to the given value. Set to <= 0 to be uncapped.

cvars.name: t.OverrideFPS
cvars.description: This allows to override the frame time measurement with a fixed fps number (game can run faster or slower). <=0:off, in frames per second, e.g. 60

cvars.name: t.SlowFrameLoggingThreshold
cvars.description: How slow must a frame be (in seconds) to be logged out (<= 0 to disable).

cvars.name: t.TargetFrameTimeThreshold
cvars.description: The target frame time (in ms); values below this will be drawn in green, values above will be yellow or red depending on the severity default: 33.9 ms

cvars.name: t.TickComponentLatentActionsWithTheComponent
cvars.description: Should we tick latent actions fired for a component at the same time as the component? 0: Tick component latent actions later on in the frame (behavior prior to 4.16, provided for games relying on the old behavior but will be removed in the future) 1: Tick component latent actions at the same time as the component (default)

cvars.name: t.UnacceptableFrameTimeThreshold
cvars.description: The frame time theshold for what is considered completely unacceptable (in ms); values above this will be drawn as red default: 50.0 ms

cvars.name: t.UnsteadyFPS
cvars.description: Causes FPS to bounce around randomly in the 8-32 range.

cvars.name: TakeRecorder.SaveRecordedAssetsOverride
cvars.description: 0: Save recorded assets is based on user settings 1: Override save recorded assets to always start on

cvars.name: TaskGraph.EnableForkedMultithreading
cvars.description: When false will prevent the task graph from running multithreaded on forked processes.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.ForkedProcessMaxWorkerThreads
cvars.description: Configures the number of worker threads a forked process should spawn if it allows multithreading.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.IgnoreThreadToDoGatherOn
cvars.description: DEPRECATED! If 1, then we ignore the hint provided with SetGatherThreadForDontCompleteUntil and just run it on AnyHiPriThreadHiPriTask.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.NumForegroundWorkers
cvars.description: Configures the number of foreground worker threads. Requires the scheduler to be restarted to have an affect

cvars.name: TaskGraph.PrintBroadcastWarnings
cvars.description: If > 0 taskgraph will emit warnings when waiting on broadcasts

cvars.name: TaskGraph.RenderThreadPollPeriodMs
cvars.description: Render thread polling period in milliseconds. If value < 0, task graph tasks explicitly wake up RT, otherwise RT polls for tasks.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.AsyncEndOfFrameGameTasks
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for the experiemntal async end of frame tasks. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.AsyncEndOfFrameGameTasks bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.AsyncTraceTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for async traces. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.AsyncTraceTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ClearAudioChunkCacheReadRequest
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for an async task that clears FCacheElement::ReadRequest Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ClearAudioChunkCacheReadRequest bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.CompilePipelineStateTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FCompilePipelineStateTask. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.CompilePipelineStateTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.FMeshDrawCommandPassSetupTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FMeshDrawCommandPassSetupTask. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.FMeshDrawCommandPassSetupTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.GatherShadowPrimitives
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for GatherShadowPrimitives tasks. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.GatherShadowPrimitives bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.HiPriAsyncTickTaskPriority
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for async ticks that are high priority. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.HiPriAsyncTickTaskPriority bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.IoDispatcherAsyncTasks
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for IoDispatcher decompression. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.IoDispatcherAsyncTasks bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.NavTriggerAsyncQueries
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for UNavigationSystemV1::PerformAsyncQueries. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.NavTriggerAsyncQueries bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.NormalAsyncTickTaskPriority
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for async ticks that are not high priority. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.NormalAsyncTickTaskPriority bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelAnimationEvaluationTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FParallelAnimationEvaluationTask Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelAnimationEvaluationTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelAnimCompletionTaskHighPriority
cvars.description: Allows parallel anim completion tasks to take priority on the GT so further work (if needed) can be kicked off earlier.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelBlendPhysicsTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FParallelBlendPhysicsTask. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelBlendPhysicsTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelClothTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for parallel cloth. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelClothTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelTranslateCommandList
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FParallelTranslateCommandList. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelTranslateCommandList bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelTranslateCommandListPrepass
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FParallelTranslateCommandList for the prepass, which we would like to get to the GPU asap. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParallelTranslateCommandListPrepass bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParticleAsyncTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FParticleAsyncTask. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParticleAsyncTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParticleManagerAsyncTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FParticleManagerAsyncTask. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.ParticleManagerAsyncTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.PhysicsTickTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priotiry for Chaos physics tick Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.PhysicsTickTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.RHIThreadOnTaskThreads
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for when we are running 'RHI thread' tasks on any thread. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.RHIThreadOnTaskThreads bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.SceneRenderingTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for various scene rendering tasks. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.SceneRenderingTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.TickCleanupTaskPriority
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for tick cleanup. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.TickCleanupTaskPriority bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.TickDispatchTaskPriority
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for tick tasks dispatch. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.TickDispatchTaskPriority bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.UpdateCachePrimitivesTask
cvars.description: Task and thread priority for FUpdateCachePrimitivesTask. Arguments are three characters: [ThreadPriority][TaskPriority][TaskPriorityIfForcedToNormalThreadPriority] where ThreadPriority is 'h' or 'n' or 'b' (high/normal/background) and TaskPriority is 'h' or 'n' (high/normal). Example: TaskGraph.TaskPriorities.UpdateCachePrimitivesTask bnh

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TestCriticalLockFree
cvars.description: If > 0, then we sleep periodically at critical points in the lock free lists. Threads must not starve...this will encourage them to starve at the right place to find livelocks.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.TestDontCompleteUntilForAlreadyComplete
cvars.description: If 1, then we before spawning a gather task, we just check if all of the subtasks are complete, and in that case we can skip the gather.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.UseBackgroundThreads
cvars.description: If > 0, then use background threads, otherwise run background tasks on normal priority task threads. Used for performance tuning.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.UseDynamicPrioritization
cvars.description: Adjust thread priority per-task so that higher priority tasks running on background threads can't be preempted as easily. Helps a lot under high load.

cvars.name: TaskGraph.UseHiPriThreads
cvars.description: If > 0, then use hi priority task threads, otherwise run background tasks on normal priority task threads. Used for performance tuning.

cvars.name: Tex.AsyncDXTBlocksPerBatch
cvars.description: The number of blocks to compress in parallel for DXT compression.

cvars.name: tick.AllowAsyncComponentTicks
cvars.description: Used to control async component ticks.

cvars.name: tick.AllowAsyncTickCleanup
cvars.description: If true, ticks are cleaned up in a task thread.

cvars.name: tick.AllowAsyncTickDispatch
cvars.description: If true, ticks are dispatched in a task thread.

cvars.name: tick.AllowConcurrentTickQueue
cvars.description: If true, queue ticks concurrently.

cvars.name: tick.AnimationDelaysEndGroup
cvars.description: If > 0, then skeletal meshes that do not rely on physics simulation will set their animation end tick group to TG_PostPhysics.

cvars.name: tick.DoAsyncEndOfFrameTasks
cvars.description: Experimental option to run various things concurrently with the HUD render.

cvars.name: tick.DoAsyncEndOfFrameTasks.Randomize
cvars.description: Used to add random sleeps to tick.DoAsyncEndOfFrameTasks to shake loose bugs on either thread. Also does random render thread flushes from the game thread.

cvars.name: tick.DoAsyncEndOfFrameTasks.ValidateReplicatedProperties
cvars.description: If true, validates that replicated properties haven't changed during the Slate tick. Results will not be valid if demo.ClientRecordAsyncEndOfFrame is also enabled.

cvars.name: tick.HiPriSkinnedMeshes
cvars.description: If > 0, then schedule the skinned component ticks in a tick group before other ticks.

cvars.name: tick.LightweightTimeguardThresholdMS
cvars.description: Threshold in milliseconds for the tick timeguard

cvars.name: tick.LogTicks
cvars.description: Spew ticks for debugging.

cvars.name: tick.MinimizedSyncDrawToGPU
cvars.description: True means we will wait for GPU idle when minimized. Prevents mem leaks due to CPU issuing draws faster than GPU processes when minimized.

cvars.name: tick.SecondsBeforeEmbeddedAppSleeps
cvars.description: When built as embedded, how many ticks to perform before sleeping

cvars.name: tick.ShowPrerequistes
cvars.description: When logging ticks, show the prerequistes; debugging.

cvars.name: timecode.UseDropFormatTimecodeByDefaultWhenSupported
cvars.description: By default, should we generate a timecode in drop frame format when the frame rate does support it.

cvars.name: TimerManager.BuildTimerSourceList
cvars.description: When non-zero, tracks which timers expire each frame, dumping them during shutdown or when the flag is changed back to 0. 0: Off 1: On - Group timers by class (useful to focus on entire systems of things, especially bad spikey frames where we care about aggregates) 2: On - Do not group timers by class (useful if individual instances are problematic)

cvars.name: TimerManager.DumpAllTimerLogsThreshold
cvars.description: Threshold (in count of active timers) at which to dump info about all active timers to logs. -1 means this is disabled. NOTE: This will only be dumped once per process launch.

cvars.name: TimerManager.DumpTimerLogResolveVirtualFunctions
cvars.description: When logging timer info virtual functions will be resolved, if possible.

cvars.name: TimerManager.DumpTimerLogsThreshold
cvars.description: Threshold (in milliseconds) after which we log timer info to try and help track down spikes in the timer code. Disabled when set to 0

cvars.name: TimerManager.DumpTimerLogSymbolNames
cvars.description: When logging timer info, symbol names will be included if set to 1.

cvars.name: TimerManager.MaxExpiredTimersToLog
cvars.description: Maximum number of TimerData exceeding the threshold to log in a single frame.

cvars.name: Trace.SlowTaskMaxRegionDepth
cvars.description: Maximum depth of nested slow tasks to create as trace regions in Insights

cvars.name: TrackAsyncLoadRequests.Dedupe
cvars.description: If > 0 then deduplicate requests to async load the same package in the report.

cvars.name: TrackAsyncLoadRequests.DumpAfterCsvProfiling
cvars.description: If > 0, dumps tracked async load requests to a file when csv profiling ends.

cvars.name: TrackAsyncLoadRequests.Enable
cvars.description: If > 0 then remove aliases from the counting process. This essentialy merges addresses that have the same human readable string. It is slower.

cvars.name: TrackAsyncLoadRequests.RemoveAliases
cvars.description: If > 0 then remove aliases from the counting process. This essentialy merges addresses that have the same human readable string. It is slower.

cvars.name: TrackAsyncLoadRequests.StackIgnore
cvars.description: Number of items to discard from the top of a stack frame.

cvars.name: TrackAsyncLoadRequests.StackLen
cvars.description: Maximum number of stack frame items to keep. This improves aggregation because calls that originate from multiple places but end up in the same place will be accounted together.

cvars.name: TrackAsyncLoadRequests.Threshhold
cvars.description: Minimum number of hits to include in the report.

cvars.name: TransBuffer.DumpObjectMap
cvars.description: Whether to dump the object map each time a transaction is written for debugging purposes.

cvars.name: TypedElements.EnableElementsCopyAndPaste
cvars.description: Is support for elements copy and paste enabled?

cvars.name: TypedElements.EnableFoliageInstanceElements
cvars.description: Is support for static mesh instance elements enabled for foliage owned instances?

cvars.name: TypedElements.EnableReferenceTracking
cvars.description: Is support for element reference tracking enabled?

cvars.name: TypedElements.EnableSMInstanceElements
cvars.description: Is support for static mesh instance elements enabled?

cvars.name: TypedElements.EnableViewportSMInstanceSelection
cvars.description: Enable direct selection of Instanced Static Mesh Component (ISMC) Instances in the Level Editor Viewport

cvars.name: UMG.AnimationBudgetMs
cvars.description: (Default: 0.0) EXPERIMENTAL: A per-frame animation budget to use for evaluation of all UMG animations this frame.

cvars.name: UMG.AnimationMarkers
cvars.description: (Default: false) Whether to emit profiling frame markers for starting and stopping UMG animations.

cvars.name: UMG.AsyncAnimationControlFlow
cvars.description: (Default: true) Whether to perform animation control flow functions (Play, Pause, Stop etc) asynchronously.

cvars.name: UMG.Editor.EnableWidgetDesignerTools
cvars.description: Toggles processing of widget designer tools. Must be set before opening widget designer.

cvars.name: UMG.EnablePreviewMode
cvars.description: Whether or not to enable the UMG Preview mode.

cvars.name: UMG.FlushAnimationsAtEndOfFrame
cvars.description: Whether to automatically flush any outstanding animations at the end of the frame, or just wait until next frame.

cvars.name: UMG.ThumbnailRenderer.Enable
cvars.description: Option to enable/disable thumbnail rendering.

cvars.name: VA.AllowPkgVirtualization
cvars.description: When true submitting packages in the editor will no longer trigger the virtualization process

cvars.name: VA.DisableSystem
cvars.description: When true the VA system will be disabled as though 'SystemName' was 'None'

cvars.name: VA.LazyInitConnections
cvars.description: When true the VA backends will defer creating their connections until first use

cvars.name: VA.LazyInitSystem
cvars.description: When true the VA system will be lazy initialized on first use

cvars.name: VI.ActorSnap
cvars.description: Whether or not to snap to Actors in the scene. Off by default, set to 1 to enable.

cvars.name: VI.AlignCandidateDistance
cvars.description: The distance candidate actors can be from our transformable (in multiples of our transformable's size

cvars.name: VI.AllowCarryingCertainObjects
cvars.description: When enabled, allows the user to freely move and rotate certain selected objects with a one-hand drag.

cvars.name: VI.AllowLaserSmooth
cvars.description: Allow laser smoothing using one euro

cvars.name: VI.AllowVerticalWorldMovement
cvars.description: Whether you can move your tracking space away from the origin or not

cvars.name: VI.AllowWorldRotationPitchAndRoll
cvars.description: When enabled, you'll not only be able to yaw, but also pitch and roll the world when rotating by gripping with two hands

cvars.name: VI.CarrySmoothingLerpAlpha
cvars.description: How much to smooth out movement of the object you're carrying.

cvars.name: VI.DragAtLaserImpactInterpolationDuration
cvars.description: How long we should interpolate objects between positions when dragging under the laser's impact point

cvars.name: VI.DragAtLaserImpactInterpolationThreshold
cvars.description: Minimum distance jumped between frames before we'll force interpolation mode to activated

cvars.name: VI.DragHapticFeedbackStrength
cvars.description: Default strength for haptic feedback when starting to drag objects

cvars.name: VI.DragScale
cvars.description: Scales the translation when dragging yourself through the world

cvars.name: VI.DragTranslationVelocityStopEpsilon
cvars.description: When dragging inertia falls below this value (cm/frame), we'll stop inertia and finalize the drag

cvars.name: VI.ElasticSnap
cvars.description: When enabled with grid snap, you can 'pull' objects slightly away from their snapped position

cvars.name: VI.ElasticSnapStrength
cvars.description: How much objects should 'reach' toward their unsnapped position when elastic snapping is enabled with grid snap

cvars.name: VI.ForceGizmoPivotToCenterOfObjectsBounds
cvars.description: When enabled, the gizmo's pivot will always be centered on the selected objects. Otherwise, we use the pivot of the last selected object.

cvars.name: VI.ForceShowCursor
cvars.description: Whether or not the mirror window's cursor should be enabled. Off by default, set to 1 to enable.

cvars.name: VI.ForceSnapDistance
cvars.description: The distance (in % of transformable size) where guide lines indicate that actors are aligned

cvars.name: VI.GizmoHandleHoverAnimationDuration
cvars.description: How quickly to animate gizmo handle hover state

cvars.name: VI.GizmoHandleHoverScale
cvars.description: How much to scale up transform gizmo handles when hovered over

cvars.name: VI.GizmoScaleInDesktop
cvars.description: How big the transform gizmo should be when used in desktop mode

cvars.name: VI.GizmoSelectionAnimationCurvePower
cvars.description: Controls the animation curve for the gizmo after objects are selected

cvars.name: VI.GizmoSelectionAnimationDuration
cvars.description: How long to animate the gizmo after objects are selected

cvars.name: VI.GizmoShowMeasurementText
cvars.description: When enabled, gizmo measurements will always be visible. Otherwise, only when hovering over a scale/stretch gizmo handle

cvars.name: VI.GrabberSphereOffset
cvars.description: Offset from the controller origin that the grabber sphere should be centered at

cvars.name: VI.GrabberSphereRadius
cvars.description: In radial mode, the radius of the sphere used to select and interact

cvars.name: VI.GridHapticFeedbackStrength
cvars.description: Default strength for haptic feedback when moving across grid points

cvars.name: VI.HighSpeedInertiaDamping
cvars.description: Hight Speed Inertia Damping multiplier

cvars.name: VI.InertiaVelocityBoost
cvars.description: How much to scale object velocity when releasing dragged simulating objects in Simulate mode

cvars.name: VI.LaserPointerMaxLength
cvars.description: Maximum length of the laser pointer line

cvars.name: VI.LaserPointerRetractDuration
cvars.description: How fast the laser pointer should extend or retract

cvars.name: VI.LaserSmoothLag
cvars.description: Laser smooth lag

cvars.name: VI.LaserSmoothMinimumCutoff
cvars.description: Laser smooth lag

cvars.name: VI.LowSpeedInertiaDamping
cvars.description: Low Speed Inertia Damping multiplier

cvars.name: VI.MaxFlightSpeed
cvars.description: Maximum Superman speed

cvars.name: VI.MinVelocityForInertia
cvars.description: Minimum velocity (in cm/frame in unscaled room space) before inertia will kick in when releasing objects (or the world)

cvars.name: VI.NavigationMode
cvars.description: VR NavigationMode

cvars.name: VI.OculusLaserPointerRotationOffset
cvars.description: How much to rotate the laser pointer (pitch) relative to the forward vector of the controller (Oculus)

cvars.name: VI.OculusLaserPointerStartOffset
cvars.description: How far to offset the start of the laser pointer to avoid overlapping the hand mesh geometry (Oculus)

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoAimAtAnimationSpeed
cvars.description: The speed to animate to the gizmo full size when aiming at it

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoAimAtShrinkSize
cvars.description: The minimum size when not aiming at the gizmo (0 to 1)

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoDistanceScaleFactor
cvars.description: How much the gizmo handles should increase in size with distance from the camera, to make it easier to select

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoMinDistanceForScaling
cvars.description: How far away the camera needs to be from an object before we'll start scaling it based on distance

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoPlaneTranslationPivotOffsetYZ
cvars.description: How much the plane translation is offsetted from the pivot

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoScalePivotOffsetX
cvars.description: How much the non-uniform scale is offsetted from the pivot

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoTranslationHoverScaleMultiply
cvars.description: Multiplies translation handles hover scale

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoTranslationPivotOffsetX
cvars.description: How much the translation cylinder is offsetted from the pivot

cvars.name: VI.PivotGizmoTranslationScaleMultiply
cvars.description: Multiplies translation handles scale

cvars.name: VI.PlacementInterpolationDuration
cvars.description: How long we should interpolate newly-placed objects to their target location.

cvars.name: VI.PlacementOffsetScaleWhileSimulating
cvars.description: How far to additionally offset objects (as a scalar percentage of the gizmo bounds) from the placement impact point while simulate mode is active

cvars.name: VI.ScaleMax
cvars.description: Maximum world scale in centimeters

cvars.name: VI.ScaleMin
cvars.description: Minimum world scale in centimeters

cvars.name: VI.ScaleSensitivity
cvars.description: Sensitivity for scaling

cvars.name: VI.SelectionHapticFeedbackStrength
cvars.description: Default strength for haptic feedback when selecting objects

cvars.name: VI.SFXMultiplier
cvars.description: Default Sound Effect Volume Multiplier

cvars.name: VI.ShowTransformGizmo
cvars.description: Whether the transform gizmo should be shown for selected objects

cvars.name: VI.SmoothSnap
cvars.description: When enabled with grid snap, transformed objects will smoothly blend to their new location (instead of teleporting instantly)

cvars.name: VI.SmoothSnapSpeed
cvars.description: How quickly objects should interpolate to their new position when grid snapping is enabled

cvars.name: VI.SnapGridLineWidth
cvars.description: Width of the grid lines on the snap grid

cvars.name: VI.SnapGridSize
cvars.description: How big the snap grid should be. At 1.0, this will be the maximum of the gizmo's bounding box and a multiple of the current grid snap size

cvars.name: VI.SweepPhysicsWhileSimulating
cvars.description: If enabled, simulated objects won't be able to penetrate other objects while being dragged in Simulate mode

cvars.name: VI.TriggerDeadZone_Rift
cvars.description: Trigger dead zone. The trigger must be fully released before we'll trigger a new 'light press'

cvars.name: VI.TriggerDeadZone_Vive
cvars.description: Trigger dead zone. The trigger must be fully released before we'll trigger a new 'light press'

cvars.name: VI.TriggerFullyPressedThreshold_Rift
cvars.description: Minimum trigger threshold before we consider the trigger 'fully pressed'

cvars.name: VI.TriggerFullyPressedThreshold_Vive
cvars.description: Minimum trigger threshold before we consider the trigger 'fully pressed'

cvars.name: VI.TriggerTouchThreshold_Rift
cvars.description: Minimum trigger threshold before we consider the trigger 'touched'

cvars.name: VI.TriggerTouchThreshold_Vive
cvars.description: Minimum trigger threshold before we consider the trigger 'touched'

cvars.name: VI.ViveLaserPointerRotationOffset
cvars.description: How much to rotate the laser pointer (pitch) relative to the forward vector of the controller (Vive)

cvars.name: VI.ViveLaserPointerStartOffset
cvars.description: How far to offset the start of the laser pointer to avoid overlapping the hand mesh geometry (Vive)

cvars.name: VI.WorldRotationDragThreshold
cvars.description: How much (degrees) you need to perform a rotation gesture before world rotation starts to happen.

cvars.name: VI.WorldScalingDragThreshold
cvars.description: How much you need to perform a scale gesture before world scaling starts to happen.

cvars.name: vm.BatchPackedVMOutput
cvars.description: If > 0 output elements will be packed and batched branch free.

cvars.name: vm.BatchVMInput
cvars.description: If > 0 input elements will be batched.

cvars.name: vm.BatchVMOutput
cvars.description: If > 0 output elements will be batched.

cvars.name: vm.ChunkSizeInBytes
cvars.description: Number of bytes per VM chunk Ideally <= L1 size. (default=32768)

cvars.name: vm.DetailedVMScriptStats
cvars.description: If > 0 the vector VM will emit stats for it's internal module calls.

cvars.name: vm.FreeUnoptimizedByteCode
cvars.description: When we have optimized the VM byte code should we free the original unoptimized byte code?

cvars.name: vm.InstancesPerChunk
cvars.description: Number of instances per VM chunk. (default=128)

cvars.name: vm.MaxThreadsPerScript
cvars.description: Maximum number of threads per script. Set 0 to mean 'as many as necessary'

cvars.name: vm.OptimizeVMByteCode
cvars.description: If > 0 vector VM code optimization will be enabled at runtime.

cvars.name: vm.PageSizeInKB
cvars.description: Minimum allocation per VM instance. There are 64 of these, so multiply GVVMPageSizeInKB x 64 x 1024 to get total number of bytes used by the VVM

cvars.name: vm.Parallel
cvars.description: If > 0 vector VM chunk level paralellism will be enabled.

cvars.name: vm.ParallelChunksPerBatch
cvars.description: Number of chunks to process per task when running in parallel.

cvars.name: vm.SafeOptimizedKernels
cvars.description: If > 0 optimized vector VM byte code will use safe versions of the kernels.

cvars.name: vm.UseOptimizedVMByteCode
cvars.description: If > 0 optimized vector VM code will be excuted at runtime.

cvars.name: voice.debug.PrintAmplitude
cvars.description: when set to 1, the current incoming amplitude of the VOIP engine will be displayed on screen. 0: disabled, 1: enabled.

cvars.name: voice.DefaultPatchBufferSize
cvars.description: Changes the amount of audio we buffer for VOIP patching, in samples.

cvars.name: voice.DefaultPatchGain
cvars.description: Changes the default gain of audio patches, in linear gain.

cvars.name: voice.JitterBufferDelay
cvars.description: The default amount of audio we buffer, in seconds, before we play back audio. Decreasing this value will decrease latency but increase the potential for underruns. Value: Number of seconds of audio we buffer.

cvars.name: voice.MicInputGain
cvars.description: The default gain amount in linear amplitude. Value: Gain multiplier.

cvars.name: voice.MicNoiseAttackTime
cvars.description: Sets the fade-in time for our noise gate. Value: Number of seconds we fade in over.

cvars.name: voice.MicNoiseGateThreshold
cvars.description: Our threshold, in linear amplitude, for our noise gate on input. Similar to voice.SilenceDetectionThreshold, except that audio quieter than our noise gate threshold will still output silence. Value: Number of seconds of audio we buffer.

cvars.name: voice.MicNoiseReleaseTime
cvars.description: Sets the fade out time for our noise gate. Value: Number of seconds we fade out over.

cvars.name: voice.MicStereoBias
cvars.description: This will attenuate the left or right channel. 0.0: Centered. 1.0: right channel only. -1.0: Left channel only.

cvars.name: voice.MuteAudioEngineOutput
cvars.description: When set to a nonzero value, the output for the audio engine will be muted..

cvars.name: voice.NumChannels
cvars.description: Default number of channels to capture from mic input, encode to Opus, and output. Can be set to 1 or 2. Value: Number of channels to use for VOIP input and output.

cvars.name: voice.playback.ResyncThreshold
cvars.description: If the amount of audio we have buffered is greater than this value, we drop the oldest audio we have and sync to have voice.JitterDelay worth of buffered audio.

cvars.name: voice.playback.ShouldResync
cvars.description: If set to 1, we will resync VOIP audio once it's latency goes beyond voice.playback.ResyncThreshold.

cvars.name: voice.SilenceDetectionAttackTime
cvars.description: Attack time to be set for the VOIP microphone's silence detection algorithm in milliseconds.

cvars.name: voice.SilenceDetectionReleaseTime
cvars.description: Release time to be set for the VOIP microphone's silence detection algorithm in milliseconds.

cvars.name: voice.SilenceDetectionThreshold
cvars.description: Threshold to be set for the VOIP microphone's silence detection algorithm.

cvars.name: vr.AllowMotionBlurInVR
cvars.description: For projects with motion blur enabled, this allows motion blur to be enabled even while in VR.

cvars.name: vr.EnableMotionControllerLateUpdate
cvars.description: This command allows you to specify whether the motion controller late update is applied. 0: don't use late update 1: use late update (default)

cvars.name: vr.InstancedStereo
cvars.description: 0 to disable instanced stereo (default), 1 to enable.

cvars.name: vr.MobileMultiView
cvars.description: 0 to disable mobile multi-view, 1 to enable.

cvars.name: vr.RoundRobinOcclusion
cvars.description: 0 to disable round-robin occlusion queries for stereo rendering (default), 1 to enable.

cvars.name: Widget.MaxAnimationLatentActions
cvars.description: Defines the maximum number of latent actions that can be run in one frame.

cvars.name: Widget.TemplatePreviewInEditor
cvars.description: Should a dynamic template be generated at runtime for the editor for widgets? Useful for debugging templates.

cvars.name: Widget.UseParallelAnimation
cvars.description: Use multi-threaded evaluation for widget animations.

cvars.name: WidgetComponent.MaximumRenderTargetHeight
cvars.description: Sets the maximum height of the render target used by a Widget Component.

cvars.name: WidgetComponent.MaximumRenderTargetWidth
cvars.description: Sets the maximum width of the render target used by a Widget Component.

cvars.name: WidgetComponent.UseAutomaticTickModeByDefault
cvars.description: Sets to true to Disable Tick by default on Widget Components when set to false, the tick will enabled by default.

cvars.name: WindowsApplication.EnableFirstTouchEvent
cvars.description: Enable FirstTouch Event which prevents small pop on some touch input devices

cvars.name: WindowsCursor.UseInvisibleCursorForNoneCursorType
cvars.description: If enabled, sets the platform HCursor to a transparent cursor instead of null when the mouse cursor type to None.

cvars.name: WindowsPlatformCrashContext.ForceCrashReportDialogOff
cvars.description: If true, force the crash report dialog to not be displayed in the event of a crash.

cvars.name: WindowTitleBar.ForceWindowButtonsHidden
cvars.description: If true, force the window title bar buttons to be hidden.

cvars.name: wp.Editor.DisableLevelInstanceEditorPartialLoading
cvars.description: Allow disabling partial loading of level instances in the editor.

cvars.name: wp.Editor.EnableSpatialHashValidation
cvars.description: Whether to enable World Partition editor spatial hash validation

cvars.name: wp.Editor.HLOD.AllowShowingHLODsInEditor
cvars.description: Allow showing World Partition HLODs in the editor.

cvars.name: wp.Editor.LoadingRangeBugItGo
cvars.description: Loading range for BugItGo command.

cvars.name: wp.Editor.WorldExtentToEnableStreaming
cvars.description: World extend to justify enabling streaming.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.BlockOnSlowStreaming
cvars.description: Set if streaming needs to block when to slow to catchup.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.BlockOnSlowStreamingRatio
cvars.description: Ratio of DistanceToCell / LoadingRange to use to determine if World Partition streaming needs to block

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.BlockOnSlowStreamingWarningFactor
cvars.description: Factor of wp.Runtime.BlockOnSlowStreamingRatio we want to start notifying the user

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.DebugDedicatedServerStreaming
cvars.description: Turn on/off to debug of server streaming.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.DrawContentBundles
cvars.description: Enable to draw debug display of content bundle.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.EnableServerStreaming
cvars.description: Set to 1 to enable server streaming, set to 2 to only enable it in PIE. Changing the value while the game is running won't be considered.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.EnableServerStreamingOut
cvars.description: Turn on/off to allow or not the server to stream out levels (only relevant when server streaming is enabled) Changing the value while the game is running won't be considered.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.EnableSimulationStreamingSource
cvars.description: Set to 0 to if you want to disable the simulation/ejected camera streaming source.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.FilterRuntimeSpatialHashGridLevel
cvars.description: Used to choose filter a single world partition runtime hash grid level.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.ForceRuntimeSpatialHashZCulling
cvars.description: Used to force the behavior of the runtime hash cells Z culling. Set to 0 to force off, to 1 to force on and any other value to respect the runtime hash setting.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HashSet.ShowDebugDisplayLevel
cvars.description: Used to choose which level to display when showing runtime partitions.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HashSet.ShowDebugDisplayLevelCount
cvars.description: Used to choose how many levels to display when showing runtime partitions.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HashSet.ShowDebugDisplayMode
cvars.description: Used to choose what mode to display when showing runtime partitions (0=Level Streaming State, 1=Data Layers, 2=Content Bundles).

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.ForceDisableShadows
cvars.description: Force disable CastShadow flag on World Partition HLOD actors

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupDebugDraw
cvars.description: Draw debug display for the warmup requests

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupEnabled
cvars.description: Enable HLOD assets warmup. Will delay unloading of cells & transition to HLODs for wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupNumFrames frames.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupNanite
cvars.description: Enable Nanite warmup for HLOD assets. Requires wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupEnabled to be 1.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupNumFrames
cvars.description: Delay unloading of a cell for this amount of frames to ensure HLOD assets are ready to be shown at the proper resolution. Set to 0 to force disable warmup.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupVT
cvars.description: Enable virtual texture warmup for HLOD assets. Requires wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupEnabled to be 1.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupVTScaleFactor
cvars.description: Scale the VT size we ask to prefetch by this factor.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.HLOD.WarmupVTSizeClamp
cvars.description: Clamp VT warmup requests for safety.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.LevelStreamingContinuouslyIncrementalGCWhileLevelsPendingPurgeForWP
cvars.description: Force a GC update when there's more than the number of specified pending purge levels.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.MaxLoadingStreamingCells
cvars.description: Used to limit the number of concurrent loading world partition streaming cells.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.PlayerController.ForceUsingCameraAsStreamingSource
cvars.description: Whether to force the use of the camera as the streaming source for World Partition. By default the player pawn is used. 0: Use pawn as streaming source, 1: Use camera as streaming source

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RecordReplayStreamingSources
cvars.description: Set to 1 to record streaming sources when recording replay.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RuntimeSpatialHashCellToSourceAngleContributionToCellImportance
cvars.description: Value between 0 and 1 that modulates the contribution of the angle between streaming source-to-cell vector and source-forward vector to the cell importance. The closest to 0, the less the angle will contribute to the cell importance.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RuntimeSpatialHashPlacePartitionActorsUsingLocation
cvars.description: Set RuntimeSpatialHashPlacePartitionActorsUsingLocation to true to place partitioned actors into their corresponding cell using their location instead of their bounding box.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RuntimeSpatialHashPlaceSmallActorsUsingLocation
cvars.description: Set RuntimeSpatialHashPlaceSmallActorsUsingLocation to true to place actors smaller than a cell size into their corresponding cell using their location instead of their bounding box.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RuntimeSpatialHashSnapNonAlignedGridLevelsToLowerLevels
cvars.description: Set RuntimeSpatialHashSnapNonAlignedGridLevelsToLowerLevels to false to avoid snapping higher levels cells to child cells. Only used when GRuntimeSpatialHashUseAlignedGridLevels is false.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RuntimeSpatialHashSortUsingCellExtent
cvars.description: Set to 1 to use cell extent instead of cell grid level when sorting cells by importance.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RuntimeSpatialHashSortUsingCellPriority
cvars.description: Set to 1 to use cell priority before distance to/angle from source as part of the sorting criterias when sorting cells by importance.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.RuntimeSpatialHashUseAlignedGridLevels
cvars.description: Set RuntimeSpatialHashUseAlignedGridLevels to false to help break the pattern caused by world partition promotion of actors to upper grid levels that are always aligned on child levels.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.ServerDisallowStreamingOutDataLayers
cvars.description: Comma separated list of data layer names that aren't allowed to be unloaded or deactivated on the server

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.ServerStreamingSourceMinimumExtraAngle
cvars.description: Minimum value added to the angle of the streaming source shape sector used by the server (in degree).

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.ServerStreamingSourceMinimumExtraRadius
cvars.description: Minimum value added to the radius of the streaming sources used by the server (in Unreal unit).

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.ShowRuntimeSpatialHashCellStreamingPriority
cvars.description: Enable to show a heatmap of the runtime spatial hash grid cells based on their priority (0=disabled, 1=heatmap, 2=grayscale.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.ShowRuntimeSpatialHashGridLevel
cvars.description: Used to choose which grid level to display when showing world partition runtime hash.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.ShowRuntimeSpatialHashGridLevelCount
cvars.description: Used to choose how many grid levels to display when showing world partition runtime hash.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UpdateStreaming.EnableOptimization
cvars.description: Set to 1 to enable an optimization that skips world partition streaming update if nothing relevant changed since last update.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UpdateStreaming.ForceUpdateFrameCount
cvars.description: Frequency (in frames) at which world partition streaming update will be executed regardless if no changes are detected.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UpdateStreaming.LocationQuantization
cvars.description: Distance (in Unreal units) used to quantize the streaming sources location to determine if a world partition streaming update is necessary.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UpdateStreaming.RotationQuantization
cvars.description: Angle (in degrees) used to quantize the streaming sources rotation to determine if a world partition streaming update is necessary.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UpdateStreamingSources
cvars.description: Set to 0 to stop updating (freeze) world partition streaming sources.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UpdateStreamingStateTimeLimit
cvars.description: Maximum amount of time to spend doing World Partition UpdateStreamingState (ms per frame).

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UseMakingInvisibleTransactionRequests
cvars.description: Whether the client should wait for the server to acknowledge visibility update before making partitioned world streaming levels invisible. Changing the value while the game is running won't be considered.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UseMakingVisibleTransactionRequests
cvars.description: Whether the client should wait for the server to acknowledge visibility update before making partitioned world streaming levels visible. Changing the value while the game is running won't be considered.

cvars.name: wp.Runtime.UseReplayStreamingSources
cvars.description: Set to 1 to use the recorded streaming sources when playing replay.

cvars.name: XInput.ForceControllerStateUpdate
cvars.description: Force XInput refresh of controller state on each frame. 0: Not Enabled, 1: Enabled

cvars.name: xr.VRS.DynamicFoveation
cvars.description: Whether foveation level should adjust based on GPU utilization 0: Disabled (default); 1: Enabled

cvars.name: xr.VRS.FoveationLevel
cvars.description: Level of foveated VRS to apply (when Variable Rate Shading is available) 0: Disabled (default); 1: Low; 2: Medium; 3: High; 4: High Top;

cvars.name: xr.VRS.FoveationPreview
cvars.description: Include foveated VRS in the VRS debug overlay. 0: Disabled; 1: Enabled (default)

cvars.name: xr.VRS.GazeTrackedFoveation
cvars.description: Enable gaze-tracking for foveated VRS 0: Disabled (default); 1: Enabled
